FrankenScipy cross-check -> bedrock-conformance bead as planned.

title: fs-ivl core: outward-rounded intervals (next_up/next_down), affine arithmetic, dd/qd ladder
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` core: interval + affine arithmetic and the double-double/quad-double escalation ladder (plan §6.4). This crate is WHAT THE WORD "CERTIFIED" MEANS everywhere else in the system.

## Scope
- Interval arithmetic with RIGOROUS outward rounding via next_up/next_down directed nudging — no global rounding-mode fiddling (Rust-safe, thread-safe, works under any SIMD mixing). All arithmetic ops + elementary functions using fs-math's DECLARED ENCLOSURES (the dependency that makes elementary-function intervals rigorous rather than folkloric).
- Affine arithmetic: noise-symbol form for correlation-aware tightness (kills the dependency problem interval arithmetic suffers on x−x patterns; essential for deep F-rep DAG evaluation).
- Double-double/quad-double types (built on fs-la-mixed-precision's EFT primitives — one implementation) serving as the high-precision oracle for certify-certifiers and the escalation ladder's top rungs.
- Directed-rounding vector ops via fs-simd where lane semantics allow, scalar fallback where they don't — correctness NEVER traded for lanes in certified paths.
- Containment discipline: every operation's postcondition is enclosure (result interval ⊇ true value set); this is the G0 law tested under random expression rewrites.

## Acceptance criteria
Containment property suite green across arithmetic + elementary functions (billions of randomized cases nightly vs dd/qd oracle); affine arithmetic demonstrably tighter than plain intervals on correlated expressions (measured tightness ratios ledgered); no rounding-mode global state anywhere (grep-lintable).

## Testing
G0: containment under rewrites (x·(y+z) vs x·y+x·z must both contain truth); known hard cases (Rump's polynomial); cross-ISA identical interval endpoints in strict mode (G5). Logs: tightness statistics, violation counts (must be zero), oracle-disagreement forensics.
close_reason: fs-ivl core landed + fs-math extended. fs-math (additive, L0): eft module (two_sum/quick_two_sum/two_prod-via-FMA, exactness properties bitwise-tested 300k cases) + dd module (double-double via std operator traits, documented bounds add/mul <= 2^-104 and div/sqrt <= 2^-103, normalization invariant property-tested 200k, known-value + field-property batteries) - relocated from 6ys.8 scope so fs-la refinement and fs-ivl share ONE implementation; det golden hash untouched. fs-ivl (L1): Interval with outward rounding via next_up/next_down nudging (1 ulp basic ops, declared-budget nudges for elementary fns), WHOLE-line semantics for zero-divisors and 0*inf, conservative critical-point-counting sin/cos vs interval-pi (extrema capture tested), Affine/AffineCtx noise-symbol forms (x-x collapses, tightness ratio > 1e10 vs plain IA, first-order identity-zero stays O(radius^2) independent of center scale). CONTAINMENT LAW test battery: 100k dd-oracle arithmetic checks, 20k rewrite pairs, 50k intervals x 6 elementary fns vs libm point oracle, 20k checks over 4000 random DAGs - 0 violations; Rump's polynomial contains exact -54767/66192 with honestly-wide enclosure. Cross-ISA golden hash 0x3712a4c12d5e5864 IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/ivl_verify.eVYb3f/run.log, all suites green both ISAs). clippy -D warnings clean (Dd/Interval/Affine use std operator traits), fmt applied, xtask check-all 16 crates 0 violations. CONTRACT.md complete for fs-ivl + fs-math contract extended with eft/dd sections. Recorded deferrals (in CONTRACT no-claim + this bead's comments): quad-double, Taylor models, exact predicates, affine elementary fns (Chebyshev linearization), SIMD lanes, billions-scale nightly battery (CI bead huq.4 scope). Dep on 6ys.8 was removed pre-work (EFT relocation, see comments on both beads).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent, post fs-fft + fs-sparse). Dependency on 6ys.8 removed - EFT/dd relocated to fs-math (L0), see comment there. v1 design: (1) Interval{lo,hi} with outward rounding via fs-math nudge_out/next_up/next_down (already documented in fs-math CONTRACT as fs-ivl's directed-rounding primitive) - basic ops nudge 1 ulp (correctly-rounded ops are within 1/2 ulp so 1 step is RIGOROUS), elementary functions nudge by fs-math's DECLARED budgets (exp/expm1/ln/sin/cos 3, tanh 5, sqrt 0). sin/cos handle non-monotonicity by counting critical points against a conservative interval enclosure of pi; width >= 2pi gives [-1,1]. Division by a zero-containing interval returns the whole line [-inf,+inf] (documented: 'no useful enclosure' is an answer, not a panic). (2) Affine{center, terms, err} noise-symbol form: add/sub symbol-wise exact with rounding absorbed into err; mul standard AA with rad*rad nonlinear term; deterministic symbol ids via AffineCtx. Tightness-ratio tests vs plain intervals on x-x and correlated chains, ratios logged. (3) Dd double-double in fs-math on the EFT primitives; Qd DEFERRED with context (dd is the v1 oracle; Rump's polynomial is tested as containment of the EXACT rational -54767/66192, which needs no qd - the lesson is the interval's honest wide enclosure vs naive double's confident 1e21 lie). (4) G0 containment suite: random expression trees, interval eval contains dd-oracle point evals at interior samples; rewrite pairs x*(y+z) vs x*y+x*z both contain truth. (5) Cross-ISA golden hash over battery endpoints, trj-verified. SIMD: scalar everywhere v1 (the bead's own correctness-over-lanes principle).

title: fs-ivl Taylor models: rigorous remainder enclosures, interval Newton/Krawczyk, Lipschitz extraction
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` Taylor models + certified root isolation (plan §6.4): multivariate polynomial + interval remainder enclosures, interval Newton, and the Krawczyk operator.

## Scope
- Taylor models: multivariate polynomial part (fixed max degree, sparse monomial storage) + rigorous interval remainder; arithmetic (add/mul/compose), integration/differentiation, range bounding (better-than-interval enclosures for smooth functions over wide domains — the tool that makes NURBS→SDF certification and Lipschitz-bound extraction feasible).
- Interval Newton and Krawczyk operators for certified root isolation: existence + uniqueness verification in boxes, bisection-based search with certified exclusion; multivariate variants for intersection problems (curve-curve, ray-patch polishing in fs-render-charts and convert-nurbs-sdf).
- Lipschitz/derivative bound extraction: certified global/local Lipschitz constants from Taylor-model derivative enclosures — feeds certified sphere tracing (§10.2) and F-rep evaluator bounds (§7.2).
- Escalation integration: when a Taylor model's remainder is too wide for the caller's budget, escalate degree or subdivide, with cost accounting (P4 budgets apply to certification too).

## Acceptance criteria
Enclosure property (TM range ⊇ true range) verified vs oracle sampling + dd/qd; root isolation: all roots found with certified uniqueness on polynomial batteries (Wilkinson-class adversaries included); Krawczyk existence proofs validated on known fixture systems; Lipschitz bounds conservative-but-useful (tightness ratio tracked).

## Testing
G0 containment laws for TM arithmetic/composition; comparative tightness TM vs interval vs affine (documented crossover guidance); certified-root determinism. Logs: remainder widths, subdivision trees, isolation certificates with box coordinates.
close_reason: fs-ivl Taylor models + certified roots landed (new modules taylor.rs/newton.rs; existing modules and their golden hashes untouched): TaylorModel1 univariate (f64 poly in (x-c) + rigorous Interval remainder, coefficient rounding absorbed via the affine-module store-midpoint/absorb-width pattern, mul with truncated-term + cross-remainder bounds, exp/sin compositions w/ Lagrange remainders + declared-budget slack), interval Newton driver w/ certification semantics (Certified ONLY on strict-interior contraction; empty intersection = certificate of ABSENCE; deterministic sorted+merged presentation), Krawczyk operator, lipschitz_bound (outward-rounded derivative-enclosure magnitude - the fs-geom ChartSample primitive, cross-lane note in the claim comment). Battery 7/7 BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/tm_verify.FnnCv7/run.log): functional containment (41 point checks), remainder superlinear convergence >20x per halving at order 5 PLUS the decisive dependency-fixture comparison (x - x^2: TM excess 1e6+ below IA excess) - with the instructive negative recorded in CONTRACT: monotone single-occurrence expressions give IA near-zero excess (measured 2e-15), so the first comparison fixture was WRONG in a way worth documenting; sqrt2 + sin roots Certified with tight boxes, x^2 double root NEVER falsely certified (the honesty case), Krawczyk agrees+contracts, Lipschitz tight on sin/cubic, golden hash 0xa16d9759a35295d8 IDENTICAL cross-ISA. clippy -D warnings, fmt; the 1 workspace xtask violation is the other agent's in-flight scaffold (not fs-ivl). Recorded deferrals: multivariate TMs, TM division, verified ODE integration, higher-dim Newton/Krawczyk (needs interval linear algebra).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: CLAIMED (numerics-spine agent; post 6ys.15). v1 design, new modules in fs-ivl (taylor.rs + newton.rs; existing interval/affine/predicates modules and their golden hashes UNTOUCHED): (1) TaylorModel1 (univariate, fixed order n default 5): stored f64 polynomial coefficients in (x-c) + Interval remainder, INVARIANT f(x) in P(x-c)+remainder for all x in domain (the containment law extended to functional enclosures). Rounding rigor via the affine-module pattern: every coefficient op computed with Interval arithmetic, midpoint stored, width absorbed into remainder (outward-rounded). Ops: add/sub/scale, mul (truncated product; dropped高-order terms bounded by interval eval over the centered domain and folded into remainder), compose exp/sin with LAGRANGE remainders (sin: |R_n| <= |h|^(n+1)/(n+1)! since all derivs <= 1; exp: e^(sup) * |h|^(n+1)/(n+1)! with sup from interval eval - both outward-rounded). eval_interval(subbox) for tight enclosures over subdomains. KEY TEST (the point of TMs): enclosure width on a degree-8 Horner polynomial over subdivided boxes shrinks like O(w^(n+1)) vs plain interval arithmetic's O(w) - measured convergence-order assertion. (2) INTERVAL NEWTON (1D): N(X) = m - f(m)/F'(X) intersect X with certification semantics: N(X) strictly interior to X => EXISTS UNIQUE root (returned as Certified), else Possible/Empty; recursive bisection driver returning certified root boxes. Honesty case tested: x^2 (double root) must come back Possible, never falsely Certified; sin roots at k*pi and x^2-2 at +-sqrt2 Certified. (3) KRAWCZYK operator (1D): K = m - y f(m) + (1 - y F'(X))(X - m), y = 1/f'(m); same certification; agreement-with-Newton test. (4) LIPSCHITZ EXTRACTION: lipschitz_bound(fp_enclosure, domain) = outward-rounded mag of the derivative enclosure - THE primitive fs-geom's ChartSample certified-Lipschitz contract consumes (cross-lane note left for the geometry agent). Tests: containment at sampled points, convergence order, Newton/Krawczyk batteries incl the double-root honesty case, Lipschitz on sin/poly with known sup|f'|, golden hash + trj. DEFERRED (recorded): multivariate TMs, TM-based ODE verified integration, higher-order interval Newton, fs-geom integration itself (their bead).

title: fs-ivl exact predicates: Shewchuk adaptive orient2d/3d, incircle/insphere + symbolic perturbation
description:
## What
`fs-ivl` exact geometric predicates (plan §6.4): Shewchuk-style adaptive-precision floating-point expansions providing EXACT orient2d/orient3d/incircle/insphere — the difference between a mesher that works and a mesher that works until Tuesday.

## Scope
- Floating-point expansion arithmetic (grow-expansion, expansion-sum, scale-expansion — exact ops on sums of non-overlapping floats) as safe Rust with the EFT primitives shared from the mixed-precision/ivl family.
- Adaptive-precision predicate ladder: fast filtered path (interval/static filter — computed error bound; if sign is certain, done) escalating through progressively exact stages only when needed; the standard Shewchuk staging, in-house per P1.
- Predicates: orient2d, orient3d, incircle, insphere; plus the derived ones the mesh pipeline needs (segment/triangle orientation tests, symbolic perturbation hooks for degeneracy — SoS-style consistent tie-breaking so Delaunay is deterministic even on cospherical inputs, P2).
- Consumers: fs-mesh-delaunay (BRIO incremental Delaunay), fs-rep-mesh repair (winding numbers use robust orientation), medial axis (filtered Voronoi), self-intersection certificates.

## Acceptance criteria
Exactness verified against arbitrary-precision oracle (dd/qd + exact rational fallback in tests) on adversarial near-degenerate batteries (points on a common sphere perturbed by 1 ulp, collinear grids, Kettner-class breakdown cases that famously crash naive meshes); filter efficiency measured (% resolved at fast stage) — performance without correctness compromise.

## Testing
G0: sign agreement with oracle across generated degeneracy families; symbolic-perturbation consistency (same input ordering → same tie resolution, both ISAs); throughput benchmarks (filter hit rates). Logs: per-stage resolution counts, escalation histograms, worst-case inputs archived as fixtures.
close_reason: Exact geometric predicates landed in fs-ivl (expansion + predicates modules). EXPANSION ARITHMETIC: faithful Shewchuk ports on the shared fs-math EFTs — fast_expansion_sum_zeroelim (branchless magnitude merge), scale_expansion_zeroelim (FMA two_prod, no Dekker splitting), expansion_product (divide-and-conquer distillation), exact diffs, sign-from-largest-component; exactness proven by RESIDUAL LAWS (result minus inputs distills to the empty expansion — no tolerance, no oracle ceiling) plus a catastrophic-cancellation battery. PREDICATES: orient2d (full faithful A/B/C/D adaptive ladder with Shewchuk's proven arrangement-specific error-bound constants, epsilon = 2^-53), orient3d / incircle / insphere (stage-A static filter -> exact difference-expansion determinants; exact by translation invariance), all with *_with_stage telemetry. ACCEPTANCE PROVEN: i128 integer-lattice oracle agreement over thousands of seeded rounds in 2D and 3D with degeneracy hits asserted (points on common circles/spheres, collinear grids -> exact Zero); dyadic-scaled and 1-ulp perturbation batteries with analytic ground truth; Kettner-class half-ulp grid at (0.5,0.5) where the naive float determinant demonstrably misjudges while the exact ladder matches exact-integer reformulation truth and stays antisymmetric; filter efficiency measured and logged (>99% of general-position inputs resolve at stage A — performance without correctness compromise). SoS: orient2d_sos implements the Edelsbrunner-Mucke ladder (derived term-by-term in comments: D; x3-x2; y2-y3; x1-x3; +1) — total, antisymmetric, deterministic, proven on coincident/collinear worst cases; orient3d_sos = documented deterministic projection cascade. Documented no-claims in CONTRACT.md: Shewchuk's standard overflow/underflow domain caveat; incircle/insphere SoS not provided (hooks + orientation SoS only; full 3D E-M ladder deferred until fs-mesh Delaunay needs it); exact tail deliberately unoptimized Vec (cold path). 23 lib + 4 conformance + 2 legacy tests green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; xtask check-all 19 crates 0 violations. Note: dsr not present on this machine — verification via local cargo lanes per above.

title: fs-cheb: chebfun-style function objects, spectral collocation eigenproblems (Orr-Sommerfeld-ready)
description:
## What
`fs-cheb`: compute with FUNCTIONS as values — the chebfun-inspired layer (plan §6.5 [F]): 1D/2D/3D functions as adaptively truncated Chebyshev/Fourier expansions with near-machine-precision automatic degree selection.

## Scope
- 1D core: adaptive sampling → DCT (via fs-fft) → coefficient truncation at machine-precision plateau; algebra (+,×,∘), calculus (differentiate, integrate, cumsum), rootfinding (colleague-matrix eigenvalues via fs-la-eigen), extrema, norms.
- 2D/3D: low-rank representations (Chebyshev–Chebyshev outer products, adaptive cross approximation) for smooth multivariate functions.
- Periodic (Fourier) variant for closed curves/angular profiles.
- Spectral collocation eigenproblem machinery: differentiation matrices with boundary-condition row replacement, generalized eigenproblems — THE ORR–SOMMERFELD PATH: the vessel flagship's laminarity objective (§15.3) solves Orr–Sommerfeld eigenproblems along lip streamlines with this exact machinery; API must make "modal growth rates σ₁…σ₈ along a path" a first-class query.
- Named consumers: airfoil sections and spline profiles, BC specification via fs-scenario ("the inlet velocity profile is THIS FUNCTION, not a lookup table"), 1D stability eigenproblems, quadrature of smooth integrands.
- Qty integration: dimensioned function objects (a profile of velocity vs arc-length carries units end to end).

## Acceptance criteria
Machine-precision recovery of analytic fixtures (exp, Runge, oscillatory) with expected degree growth; rootfinding finds all roots on batteries with certified none-missed cross-checks (fs-ivl Newton where certification requested); Orr–Sommerfeld: reproduce published critical Reynolds number for plane Poiseuille (Re_c ≈ 5772.22, eigenvalue to documented digits) — the acceptance test for the whole spectral stack.

## Testing
G0: algebra/calculus identities (d/dx ∫ = id to precision), truncation-plateau detection robustness (noise floors); FrankenScipy special-function cross-checks. Logs: degrees chosen, plateau diagnostics, eigenvalue convergence tables vs collocation order.
close_reason: fs-cheb v1 landed (NEW crate, L1, deps fs-fft/fs-la/fs-math): Cheb1 adaptive Chebyshev function objects on first-kind points - the values<->coefficients map is exactly fs-fft's DCT-II/III pair (the reason those DCTs were built), plateau truncation at 2.2e-15 rel deliberately above the DCT noise floor (bring-up measurement: floor-chasing inflated sin(20x) from ~degree 45 to 1090 - constant + rationale documented in-code), Clenshaw eval, differentiate (coefficient recurrence + domain chain rule), definite integral (even-coefficient formula), add/mul, subdivision+bisection+Newton roots. Spectral collocation: Chebyshev-Lobatto diff_matrix (Trefethen construction, negative-sum trick stated BITWISE-testably: diagonal exactly negates the off-diagonal sum in construction order - tested), dirichlet_laplace_eigs demo (shift-inverted iteration with shifts from a symmetric FD surrogate via landed jacobi_eigh - deterministic, non-circular, validates the collocation eigen path to 1e-6 vs analytic (k pi/2)^2). title: fs-ad forward mode: SIMD-packed Dual<N>, second-order duals, generic-scalar operator contract
description:
## What
`fs-ad` forward mode: `Dual<const N: usize>` with SIMD-lane packing (plan §6.6 regime 1) — for few-parameter sensitivities and for STRESS-TESTING HAND/GENERATED ADJOINTS (its most load-bearing job: the gradient-verification gate compares adjoints against forward-mode duals).

## Scope
- `Dual<N>`: value + N-wide derivative vector, const-generic so lanes pack into fs-simd registers; full arithmetic + elementary functions (via fs-math with derivative enclosures where certified paths need them); Qty-compatible (dimensioned duals — dJ/dθ carries units of J/θ).
- Second-order forward (Dual-over-Dual or dedicated Taylor-2) for Hessian-vector sanity checks against second-order adjoints (gradient-stack's TR-Newton needs verified Hv).
- Batch seeding utilities: seed matrices for Jacobian columns, sparsity-aware seeding (graph coloring hooks via FrankenNetworkx for compressed Jacobians).
- Integration contracts: every FLUX operator generic over scalar type S: Real so duals flow through primal code UNCHANGED (the design that makes "stress-test the adjoint" a one-liner); fs-opdsl emits dual-compatible code by construction.
- NOT here: reverse mode (FrankenTorch tape bridge + adjoint-by-construction live in fs-ad-adjoint-infra).

## Acceptance criteria
Derivative correctness vs symbolic fixtures + complex-step-quality FD on randomized compositions; genericity proven by running the PV-skeleton solver end-to-end on Dual<4> (compile + correct sensitivities); SIMD packing shows expected throughput scaling (N=4/8 near-linear on both ISAs).

## Testing
G0: chain/product/quotient rule laws under random composition; elementary-function derivative tables; NaN/edge propagation (derivative of |x| at 0 → documented convention). Logs: per-function derivative ULP stats, packing throughput tables.
close_reason: Implemented and verified on both ISAs. Dual<T: Real, const N> generic over its scalar — NESTED duals give exact higher-order derivatives from ONE implementation (second_directional: vT-H-v via Dual<Dual<f64,1>,1>, validated vs analytic f'' of sin(x^2)); Real trait = the generic-scalar contract, with f64's impl routing elementary fns through fs-math STRICT det (genericity preserves cross-ISA determinism BY DESIGN); helpers gradient/jvp/second_directional. KEY INVARIANT PROVEN: primal-bitwise fidelity — dual evaluation is bit-identical to the scalar path on 2000 random 3-deep composites (this test CAUGHT a real bug: mul_add desugaring to mul+add broke fused-primal fidelity — fixed with fused value channel + pairwise-fused derivative; without it every gradient check against fused kernels would drift). Also: 500-point grad-vs-central-FD battery (rel<5e-9), GENERIC Newton cbrt differentiated through convergence (d c^(1/3)/dc to 1e-10 — the differentiate-through-solution genericity demo), packed-vs-single-lane bitwise equivalence, documented kink/singularity conventions (abs'(0)=0 subgradient; sqrt'(0)=+inf honest), primal-only comparison semantics. Verified: 8 tests green aarch64 + trj x86 EXIT:0; clippy clean; CONTRACT.md complete. HONEST SUBSTITUTIONS (recorded in contract + claim comment): PV-skeleton-on-Dual acceptance -> generic-solver demo (vskeleton Real-refactor recorded for opdsl era); Qty<S:Real> generalization recorded follow-up; sparsity seeding + explicit SIMD lanes consumer-driven.

Ops + det-backed exp/ln/sin/cos/tanh/sqrt/powi/abs/mul_add; seeding helpers (gradient/jvp/hessian-vector); FD-vs-dual verification battery on random compositions; genericity demo: generic-over-Real CG/Newton mini-solvers differentiated through convergence. title: fs-ad adjoint infra: IFT differentiate-through-solution, revolve checkpointing w/ ledger spill, FrankenTorch bridge, CI gradient gate
description:
## What
`fs-ad` adjoint infrastructure (plan §6.6 regimes 2–3): the FrankenTorch tape bridge and the ADJOINT-BY-CONSTRUCTION machinery — differentiate through THE SOLUTION via the implicit function theorem, never through solver iterations.

## Scope
- IFT contract: for F(u,θ)=0 solved to tolerance, dJ/dθ = −λᵀ∂F/∂θ where λ solves the TRANSPOSED system ∂F/∂uᵀλ = ∂J/∂uᵀ — using the SAME preconditioner infrastructure as the primal (transpose-apply supplied by operators; solver-stack provides transposed solves). Differentiating through Krylov iterations is both wasteful and WRONG (iteration count isn't differentiable); this contract forbids it structurally.
- Tolerance/error interaction: adjoint accuracy budgeted explicitly (inexact-adjoint error terms feed the Error Ledger — gradient error is part of the evidence, not a surprise).
- Time-dependent adjoints: binomial (revolve-style) checkpointing schedules — optimal recompute-vs-store tradeoffs — INTEGRATED WITH THE LEDGER so checkpoints spill to storage gracefully (long-horizon LES/time-history adjoints don't OOM; they degrade to storage per the governor's policy).
- FrankenTorch tape bridge: reverse-mode for learned components (surrogates, neural charts); custom differentiable ops contributed upstream per §12 contract — "adjoint linear solve" node, "IFT node", "conformal calibration layer" — so solver adjoints compose with NN autograd in one graph.
- Verification harness (THE MERGE GATE, §8.7): every registered adjoint checked against Dual<N> forward mode and complex-step-quality FD on randomized problems in CI; a solver without a passing gradient check CANNOT MERGE.

## Acceptance criteria
IFT gradients match forward-mode to tolerance on PV-skeleton + fixture PDEs; revolve schedules verified optimal (checkpoint-count tables vs theory) and Ledger-spill exercised; tape bridge round-trips a surrogate training step with our custom nodes; gate wired into CI (seeded-broken adjoint demonstrably blocks).

## Testing
G0 adjoint-consistency laws (⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ for every linearized operator); checkpoint-schedule property tests (recomputation counts); end-to-end gradient checks with tolerance model documented. Logs: gradient-check tables (adjoint vs dual vs FD, per-direction), checkpoint spill events, transposed-solve residuals.
close_reason: fs-ad adjoint infra v1 landed (fs-ad gains fs-la dep, L1->L1 legal): (1) IFT module - ift_gradient for F(u,p)=0 with dense column-wise dual Jacobians, adjoint solve via fs_la Lu::solve_transpose, IftReport carrying primal residual (the honesty number: gradient formula exact only at F=0) + adjoint residual, singular dF/du -> typed FactorError. Tested vs full-pipeline re-solve central FD (1e-7) AND vs differentiate-through-converged-Newton (1e-10 agreement - the plan's regime-1-vs-regime-2 cross-check). (2) REVOLVE module - binary-treeverse checkpointed reverse sweeps; RevolveStats instruments both resource bounds (peak snapshots <= ceil(log2 L)+1, fwd re-evals <= L*log2 L, both ASSERTED in tests); HEADLINE RESULT: checkpointed adjoint BITWISE EQUAL to full-storage adjoint on a 100-step nonlinear chain (deterministic recomputation reproduces identical states - checkpointing is a memory policy invisible to the numbers); also verified vs forward duals on both dtheta and dx0 channels; insufficient budget refused with structured panic; degenerate 0/1-step chains covered. (3) GRADCHECK module - the CI gradient-gate primitive (plan 8.7), dual vs central-FD scale-aware, JSON-line report; proven to catch the value()/from_f64 derivative-killing bug class (O(1) error detected on seeded specimen). 18/18 tests green on BOTH ISAs (trj /data/tmp/adj_verify.Wls7vD/run.log), clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 19 crates 0 violations, CONTRACT.md sections in force. FrankenTorch bridge + ledger spill + binomial revolve + matrix-free IFT split to frankensim-o3ui with full design context. NOTE for huq.4 owner (CI): fs_ad::gradcheck is ready to wire into merge gates.

title: Bedrock conformance suite: per-crate contracts, FrankenScipy oracle battery, cross-crate integration, G5 sweep
description:
## What
The BEDROCK conformance + G0 suite: the layer-wide executable contract proving L1's crates against each other, against FrankenScipy, and against the Gauntlet's law framework — with the detailed logging that makes failures self-diagnosing.

## Scope
- Per-crate conformance suites registered in the contract harness (contract-conformance-infra): fs-la (GEMM/batched/factorizations/eigen/randomized/mixed), fs-sparse (formats/SpMV/preconditioners), fs-fft, fs-ivl (core/Taylor/predicates), fs-cheb, fs-ad (forward/adjoint-infra), fs-rand (philox/qmc), fs-math.
- FrankenScipy oracle battery (§12 contract): special functions, reference optimizers' test problems, sparse baselines, quadrature/ODE references — MATCH in Gauntlet, beat on roofline; disagreements auto-filed as bug reports with full reproducers (for whichever side is wrong).
- Cross-crate integration cases: FFT-based DCT feeding fs-cheb; EFT primitives shared by mixed-precision and fs-ivl agree bitwise; Dual<N> flowing through fs-la kernels; interval evaluation of fs-math functions.
- Determinism sweep: the whole layer under G5 (hash equality across thread counts, both ISAs).
- Logging standard enforced: every conformance case emits structured JSON (case id, inputs digest, tolerances, computed-vs-reference, margins, timing) — the layer's failures must be diagnosable FROM LOGS ALONE.

## Acceptance criteria
All suites green on both ISAs in CI; oracle battery green; seeded violations per crate (one each) demonstrably caught with actionable logs; suite runtime budgeted (PR-gate subset vs nightly-full split documented).

## Testing
This IS the testing bead — its own meta-tests verify harness discovery, JSON schema validity of logs, and reproducer replay from logged seeds.

title: fs-rand extensions: gamma/beta/Dirichlet/alias/vMF distributions, ziggurat perf lane, SIMD bulk, nightly battery
description:
## What
fs-rand extended distributions + performance lanes: the remainder of the
fs-rand-philox bead's scope, split so DETERMINISM-contract and downstream
statistical consumers are not serialized behind distributions nobody needs
yet. Same doctrine: strict-mode determinism first, replayable consumption
contracts, budget-gated statistical tests.

## Scope
- Distributions (consumer-driven): gamma (Marsaglia-Tsang w/ deterministic
  rejection contract), beta, Dirichlet, categorical/alias method (Vose,
  deterministic table construction), von Mises-Fisher (rendering/GA
  direction sampling), truncated normal/exponential variants.
- Ziggurat normal as the PERF path (table generation deterministic,
  documented layer boundaries; Box-Muller stays the strict default until
  ziggurat passes the same cross-ISA determinism gates).
- SIMD bulk generation: 4x/8x counter lanes via fs-simd ops for fill-heavy
  consumers (LBM initialization, MC batches); tier-equivalence tests vs the
  scalar stream (bitwise).
- Full statistical battery for nightly CI: PractRand-class or documented
  dev-only oracle (isolated per the dependency policy), plus the
  inter-stream correlation matrix sweep at scale.

## Acceptance criteria
Each distribution: moment/KS gates + deterministic-consumption replay test +
cross-ISA spot verification; ziggurat: bitwise-equal across ISAs or
explicitly gated out of strict mode; SIMD lanes: bitwise-equal to scalar
stream; nightly battery wired into CI with failure -> issue discipline.

## Testing
Same harness patterns as fs-rand core (KAT-style fixtures where published
values exist, seeded moment gates, shuffle-invariance extended to new
consumers). Logs: JSONL verdicts per case.

title: fs-rand QMC: Sobol Joe-Kuo + Owen scrambling, rank-1 lattice CBC rules
description:
## What
`fs-rand` QMC: quasi-random sequences (plan §6.7) — Sobol' with Joe–Kuo direction numbers + Owen scrambling, and rank-1 lattice rules via component-by-component construction.

## Scope
- Sobol' sequences: Joe–Kuo direction numbers (up to high dimension, table embedded), Gray-code incremental generation, skip-ahead; OWEN SCRAMBLING (nested uniform scrambling driven by Philox keys — randomized QMC with provable variance benefits and deterministic replay via seed).
- Rank-1 lattice rules: CBC construction for prescribed dimensions/weights, embedded/extensible variants, periodization maps for non-periodic integrands.
- Digital-net utilities: t,m,s-quality diagnostics, projections quality checks (2D projection scatter tests that catch bad direction numbers).
- Consumers documented in API: UQ workloads (§8.8) gain 1–2 orders of magnitude over MC at typical dimensionalities; low-discrepancy points also drive Bayesian-optimization multistart (§9.4) and light-transport sampling (§10.1).

## Acceptance criteria
Known-answer tests vs reference Sobol' implementations on standard test integrands (Genz families) demonstrating expected convergence-rate improvement vs MC (slope check, ledgered); Owen scrambling: unbiasedness property tests + variance-reduction demonstration; lattice CBC reproduces published worst-case errors on fixture cases.

## Testing
Equidistribution property tests (star-discrepancy proxies on projections); scrambling determinism (same seed → same points, both ISAs); dimension-stress tests (d up to several thousand for KL-expansion use). Logs: convergence-slope tables MC vs QMC vs RQMC per integrand.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with identical cross-ISA results. Sobol base-2: embedded Joe-Kuo D6 head (dims 1..=10; table preconditions ASSERTED at load — m_k odd, m_k < 2^k — so corruption fails loudly), Gray-code sequential + RANDOM ACCESS point(n); the table's validity is proven MECHANICALLY by exact (0,m,1)-stratification tests (every dim, m=1..8: exactly one point per dyadic bin) + 2D elementary-interval battery (every a+b=m split, exactly one point per box). TRUE Owen nested-uniform scrambling via the Philox lazy random tree (flip bit per binary-tree node keyed by (seed,dim,level,prefix) — zero storage, seed-replayable, RANDOM-ACCESS): tested net-preserving, bitwise-replayable, seed-sensitive. Rank-1 lattices: CBC construction in the gamma=1 Korobov space (B2 kernel) with korobov_error_sq diagnostic — decay 4.46e-4@n=257 -> 5.26e-5@n=1031 (near n^-2 rate), beats naive vectors; baker periodization. THE PAYOFF GATE: scrambled-Sobol RMSE 3.17e-6 vs plain-MC 1.53e-3 at n=4096/dim=5 on Genz product-peak — ~480x, far past the plan's 1-2-orders claim, and the SAME DIGITS on trj x86 (EXIT:0; QMC stack bit-reproduces cross-ISA). 17 tests, clippy clean. NO-CLAIMS: dims>10 (Joe-Kuo full-table import recorded), Owen perf path (hash-based approximation recorded; correct lazy-tree first), higher-order digital nets.

Deferred: 21201-dim table import, higher-order nets, Owen perf path.

title: fs-la GEMM: BLIS-style autotuned microkernel + packing, f64/f32/mixed, >=75% peak target
description:
## What
`fs-la` GEMM: BLIS-style dense matrix multiply — the compute-bound flagship kernel (plan §6.1, targets §14.1).

## Scope
- Architecture: arch-specific register microkernel (shapes chosen by autotuner, typically 8–14 rows × 2–4 vector columns) wrapped in autotuned KC/MC/NC cache blocking, with packing into fs-alloc arena buffers. Microkernels are fs-simd unsafe capsules with scalar twins.
- Precisions: f64, f32, and f32-storage/f64-accumulate mixed mode (the storage-vs-accuracy compromise used throughout).
- Parallelization: tile-level via fs-exec (loop partitioning respecting CCD L3 islands; packing buffers CCD-local); deterministic mode uses fixed loop orders + deterministic reduction for beta-accumulation edge cases.
- Autotuner integration: microkernel shape + blocking sweep registered as a calibration kernel; TilePlans read tuned params.
- Roofline targets (stated to be FAILABLE, re-measured per machine): ≥75% of measured f64 peak on both ISA families (M-class NEON ~0.5 TF order; TR ~6–7 TF all-core). SME2 upside handled by the separate exploratory bead.
- Honest positioning: generic huge-GEMM is NOT FrankenSim's hot loop — batched small dense is (see fs-la-batched) — but GEMM anchors packing/blocking infrastructure, POD/surrogate work, and credibility (risk §17: "pure-Rust kernels miss BLAS-class performance" → mitigated by microkernel discipline + autotuner + CI).

## Acceptance criteria
Correctness vs naive reference to tight ULP bounds across shape sweep (including degenerate: k=0, m=1, tall-skinny, wide); roofline: ≥75% measured peak at large square sizes on both ISAs, ledgered; deterministic-mode bit-stability across thread counts (G5).

## Testing
G0 property tests (associativity-with-tolerance, transpose identities, submatrix consistency); FrankenScipy cross-check battery; perf regression lane (±tolerance band → CI failure). Logs: shape sweeps with GFLOP/s, attainment %, chosen microkernel/blocking parameters.
close_reason: fs-la GEMM v1 landed: BLIS-style blocked+packed f64 GEMM (NC->KC->MC, MR8xNR4 register microkernel, safe Rust, fused mul_add), f32 and mixed f32-storage/f64-accumulate paths, BLAS beta semantics. Tests 6/6 on BOTH ISAs: bitwise same-order-oracle equality across 9-shape sweep (packing proven arithmetic-neutral), degenerate/edge semantics (k=0, beta=0 NaN overwrite, alpha=0, empty dims), transpose identity 1e-13, submatrix consistency bitwise (m/n tiling bit-neutral - recorded for the parallel lane), mixed == widened-f64 bitwise, f32 within 1e-4, golden hash 0x1d7aa3c6b6317ef0 IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/la_verify.uLxsX7/run.log). clippy -D warnings clean, fmt, xtask check-all 17 crates 0 violations. CONTRACT.md updated to PARTIAL with GEMM sections in force (KC-is-bit-contract documented). SIMD capsules + autotune + CCD-parallel + 75% roofline gate split to frankensim-xdgf (gated on autotuner wf9.12) with all determinism facts recorded so nothing is rediscovered.

title: fs-la batched small dense: SIMD-across-batch 6x6-48x48 kernels (FEM hot loop), >=60% peak
description:
## What
`fs-la` batched small dense: the ACTUAL hot loop of FEM-adjacent work (plan §6.1) — element matrices are 6×6…48×48, and they dominate assembly-adjacent compute.

## Scope
- Layout doctrine: interleave ACROSS the batch (SoA-of-matrices via #[derive(Soa)] conventions) so SIMD lanes run across elements, not within one tiny matrix — the design that yields routinely 5–15× over loop-over-LAPACK patterns.
- Operations: batched GEMM (small fixed shapes, monomorphized per size class), batched Cholesky/LU with pivoting strategies appropriate at small scale, batched triangular solves, batched eigen-3×3/6×6 closed-form/Jacobi paths (stress principal directions, metric tensors), batched determinant/inverse for 2×2/3×3/4×4 (Jacobians).
- Size-class dispatch: const-generic kernels for the common family (4,6,8,12,16,24,32,48) + a general fallback; autotuner picks interleave width per machine.
- Cancellation: batches chunked to tile quanta with Cx poll points (bounded latency even inside assembly storms).
- Consumers: FEEC assembly/apply (fs-feec-highorder sum-factorization tails), fiber-beam section state updates (seismic flagship's hot loop per §15.2), IPC contact Hessians, LBM moment transforms where shaped as small dense.

## Acceptance criteria
≥60% of peak FLOPs on both ISAs for the core size classes (roofline table §14.1), ledgered; exact agreement (ULP-bounded) with scalar per-matrix reference across the full size×precision matrix; deterministic across thread counts.

## Testing
G0 properties per op (LL^T reconstruction, PA=LU residuals, inverse·A=I to tolerance) across random well/ill-conditioned batches; special-case batteries (singular members flagged with structured per-element error, batch continues); perf lane vs targets. Logs: per-size-class GFLOP/s tables, interleave choices, condition-number histograms in failures.

title: fs-la factorizations: blocked Cholesky/LU/QR, deterministic TSQR, one-sided Jacobi SVD
description:
## What
`fs-la` factorizations: blocked dense Cholesky/LU/QR, TSQR, and one-sided Jacobi SVD (plan §6.1).

## Scope
- Blocked right-looking Cholesky and partial-pivot LU with arena packing, tile-parallel panel/update via fs-exec; blocked Householder QR with compact-WY representation.
- TSQR (tree QR) for tall-skinny blocks — THE tool for Krylov basis orthogonalization and POD snapshot compression; tree shape FIXED by tile index for determinism (the reduction-tree doctrine applied to orthogonalization).
- One-sided Jacobi SVD for accuracy-critical small/medium problems (superior relative accuracy for small singular values — matters for rank decisions in certificates); blocked driver for medium sizes.
- Condition estimation (1-norm estimators), iterative refinement hooks (consumed by fs-la-mixed-precision), and structured error reporting: singularity/indefiniteness surfaces as typed diagnostics with the offending pivot/block, never a panic.
- Deterministic pivoting: tie-breaking by lowest index under equal magnitude (P2).

## Acceptance criteria
Residual bounds met across random + adversarial (Kahan, Hilbert-class) matrices: ‖A−LLᵀ‖/‖A‖, ‖PA−LU‖, ‖A−QR‖, SVD orthogonality + reconstruction to size-appropriate tolerances; TSQR: Q orthogonality to machine precision independent of tree shape × thread count (bitwise stable in deterministic mode); FrankenScipy cross-checks green.

## Testing
G0 properties (factor-reconstruct round-trips, permutation validity, orthogonality invariants); shape/precision sweep incl. degenerate (0-dim, 1-column); determinism battery. Logs: residual tables, pivot-growth statistics, tree shapes.

title: fs-la eigensolvers: LOBPCG + Lanczos (selective reorth), matrix-free, resumable, adjoint hooks
description:
## What
`fs-la` eigensolvers: LOBPCG and Lanczos with selective reorthogonalization — chosen precisely because they are MATRIX-FREE-NATIVE (plan §6.1): modal analysis, buckling, and stability eigenproblems all reduce to operator applications (P6).

## Scope
- LOBPCG: blocked, preconditioned (accepts any fs-sparse/solver-stack preconditioner as an operator), with basis-degeneracy detection + recovery (soft-locking, orthogonalization via TSQR), constraint deflation (known nullspaces — rigid-body modes in free-free elasticity), and generalized problems (K,M pencils; M-inner products).
- Lanczos with selective reorthogonalization: for extremal + interior (shift-invert via solver-stack solves) spectra; Ritz convergence monitoring with residual-based stopping tied to caller's budget (P4).
- Both: resumable SolverState (pause/checkpoint/fork per fs-exec-speculation), cancellation-clean, deterministic mode (fixed orthogonalization order), adjoint hooks (eigenvalue derivatives via Ritz vectors — needed for eigenfrequency objectives in topo-simp).
- Consumers wired by design: buckling (fs-solid-stability geometric-stiffness pencils), manifold harmonics (Laplace–Beltrami basis), KL expansions (fs-uq), Orr–Sommerfeld validation cross-checks (fs-cheb).

## Acceptance criteria
Standard batteries (diagonal, Laplacian stencils, generalized pencils with known spectra) converge to requested tolerances; selective reorthogonalization keeps loss-of-orthogonality bounded (monitored, asserted); matrix-free contract honored (operator-apply count is THE cost metric, logged); FrankenScipy cross-check on shared problems.

## Testing
G0: eigenpair residuals ‖Av−λMv‖, orthogonality, monotonic Ritz convergence; degenerate/clustered-spectrum stress tests; resumability equality test (pause/resume = uninterrupted). Logs: convergence histories, reorthogonalization events, apply counts.

Every EigenPair carries TRUE recomputed operator residual. Battery 6/6 BOTH ISAs: analytic 1D Laplacian spectrum (both ends, full Krylov - both ends of that spectrum are clustered, documented), analytic 2D 5-point Laplacian via matrix-free stencil closure incl degenerate eigenvalue pair (NO fs-sparse coupling - formats compose at call sites per design), dense-Jacobi agreement on random SPD (Lanczos 1e-9, LOBPCG 1e-6 on a clustered small end - honest tolerance documented), orthogonality/reconstruction, resumability bitwise. HEADLINE INCIDENT (evidence the discipline works): first REAL cross-ISA golden-hash divergence of the project - start vectors used platform libm sin, x86 hashed 0x5519... vs ARM 0x325b...; caught by trj verification, fixed with fs_math::det::sin, hash re-recorded 0x87da0cb32344b097 and now IDENTICAL cross-ISA (trj /data/tmp/eigen2_verify.ItYpTq/run.log). Audit then found latent std-powf in the mixed-battery fixture (matched by luck) - hardened to strict exp/ln, justified bump to 0x8e092d4aff1b5028, verified cross-ISA. CONTRACT now states the rule: NO platform libm in any path feeding solver state. clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 19 crates 0 violations. Recorded follow-ups: selective reorth omega recurrence, LOBPCG deflation/soft-locking + real preconditioners (solver-stack bead), eigenvector adjoints (frankensim-o3ui family), shift-invert Lanczos for interior eigenvalues.

title: fs-la randomized NLA: rangefinder/RSVD/Nystrom, sketch-precondition LS, Hutchinson/Hutch++ [F]
description:
## What
`fs-la` randomized NLA (plan §6.1 [F]): randomized range finders, randomized SVD/Nyström, sketch-and-precondition least squares, and Hutchinson/Hutch++ stochastic trace estimation.

## Scope
- Randomized range finder with adaptive rank determination (posterior error estimates, block power iterations for spectral-decay-poor cases); randomized SVD on top; Nyström for PSD kernels (GP approximations in bayesopt).
- Sketch-and-precondition least squares: sketch (subsampled randomized trig transform via fs-fft or sparse sign sketches), QR the sketch, precondition LSQR-style iterations — the modern fast path for tall dense LS.
- Hutchinson + Hutch++ trace estimators: log-det and tr(f(A)) machinery; the named consumer is SENSITIVITY AGGREGATION IN TOPOLOGY OPTIMIZATION and log-det terms in GP likelihoods. Variance-reduced (Hutch++) split with deterministic Philox probe streams — stochastic estimates are still replayable (P2) and e-process-monitorable (stopping when confidence sequence is tight enough, wired later via fs-eproc).
- All randomness through fs-rand keyed streams; all results carry probe counts + variance estimates as evidence metadata (Evidence<T> integration point).

## Acceptance criteria
Rank-r approximation errors within theoretical bounds (oversampling p → expected ‖A−QQᵀA‖ bounds) on synthetic spectra (fast/slow decay, gaps); Hutch++ variance beats Hutchinson at matched probe counts (demonstrated, ledgered); sketch-LS matches direct QR solutions to tolerance with the expected speed crossover documented.

## Testing
G0 property: containment of error estimates (estimated ≥ actual with declared probability, empirically validated); determinism across thread counts; FrankenScipy/dense-SVD cross-checks. Logs: spectra, probe counts, error-vs-bound tables.

title: fs-la mixed precision: f32-factor/f64-refine policy engine, EFT/double-double ladder
description:
## What
`fs-la` mixed precision with iterative refinement AS A DEFAULT POLICY (plan §6.1): factor/precondition in f32, refine to f64, escalate to double-double only where the Error Ledger demands it.

## Scope
- Policy engine: given a target accuracy (from the caller's budget, P4) and a condition estimate, choose the precision ladder (f32 factor + f64 refine | f64 direct | f64 factor + dd refine); record the decision + achieved residual as evidence.
- Iterative refinement drivers for LU/Cholesky/QR-based solves with convergence monitoring (residual reduction per step; stall detection → escalate or report structured failure with diagnosis: "condition ~1e12 exceeds f32-factor regime").
- Error-free transformations (TwoSum/TwoProd/Dekker splitting) as the in-house building blocks; double-double arithmetic type (add/mul/div/sqrt with documented relative-error bounds) — shared with fs-ivl's high-precision oracle needs (single implementation, two consumers).
- Integration: solver-stack inherits the policy for Krylov inner/outer precision splits; fs-la-batched gets f32-storage/f64-accumulate variants; Error Ledger (fs-plan-models) receives per-solve precision provenance.

## Why
The bandwidth-starved reference machine (TR: ~3.4 GB/s/core) makes f32 storage a near-2× bandwidth lever; refinement buys back f64 accuracy for well-enough-conditioned problems — but ONLY as a policy with evidence, never a silent downgrade.

## Acceptance criteria
Refinement recovers f64-accurate solutions (componentwise residual targets) on condition-controlled batteries up to the documented threshold; escalation triggers exactly where theory predicts (threshold sweep test); dd arithmetic passes known-answer + property tests (error bounds).

## Testing
G0: EFT exactness properties (TwoSum invariants are EXACT — bitwise testable); refinement convergence-rate checks; policy-decision determinism. Logs: per-solve ladders chosen, residual trajectories, escalation events with reasons.
close_reason: fs-la mixed precision landed: solve_adaptive precision ladder (f32-factor/f64-refine -> f64-direct -> f64-factor/dd-refine) with refinement AS POLICY. ResidualTarget{backward, forward:Option} - the forward mode is the load-bearing design point discovered during testing: stable f64 solves are already backward-accurate to ~eps (measured 4.7e-17 direct), so ONLY a forward-error target below kappa*eps meaningfully engages the dd rung; API and CONTRACT document this explicitly. RefineReport = full evidence object (ladder, steps, achieved, escalated, Hager condition estimate, Demmel-style correction-ratio forward estimate, residual trajectory); run-dry reported honestly (converged=false + best-achieved), f32 singularity auto-escalates (tested with f32-collapsing matrix), stall rule deterministic. dd residuals via fs_math::dd (the relocated single implementation - the relocation paid off exactly as planned). Battery 7/7 BOTH ISAs: condition-controlled matrices (A=Q1 diag Q2^T via landed QR), f32 rung converges kappa<=1e4 with contracting trajectories, policy vetoes f32 at kappa=1e10 with cond estimate >1e8, dd rung beats direct forward error >=100x at kappa=1e10 against a past-convergence reference (test ground truth = exact solution of the STORED problem - fl(b) rounding is unrecoverable and documented as a no-claim), impossible-target honesty, bitwise decision determinism, golden hash 0xfeaa02d5a8b35aa9 IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/mixed_verify.i2kkbq/run.log). clippy -D warnings, fmt, xtask 19 crates 0 violations. CONTRACT.md mixed-precision sections in force (invariants 7-8). Recorded follow-ups: componentwise backward targets, Krylov inner/outer splits (solver-stack bead consumes this), fs-la-batched f32-storage variants, ledger event emission (fs-obs wiring).

title: fs-sparse: CSR/BSR/SELL-C-sigma, deterministic tiled assembly, SpMV/SpMM >=85% STREAM
description:
## What
`fs-sparse` core: sparse formats and SpMV/SpMM tuned for BOTH ISAs (plan §6.2).

## Scope
- Formats: CSR (generality), BSR (block CSR matched to FEM block structure — vector-valued unknowns make dense micro-blocks, and BSR turns them into batched-small-dense-adjacent work), and SELL-C-σ (sliced ELL with row sorting) for SIMD-regular SpMV on both NEON and AVX-512.
- Assembly path: COO → parallel sort → dedupe/accumulate → target format, ALL TILED with deterministic accumulation order (assembly is a reduction; P2 applies — same mesh, same matrix, bitwise, regardless of thread count).
- SpMV/SpMM: format-specialized kernels (fs-tilelang where the shape fits), CCD-sharded row ranges with first-touch, software prefetch with autotuned distance; SpMM (sparse × dense block) for block-Krylov/POD workloads.
- Roofline honesty: SpMV is bandwidth-bound; target ≥85% of measured STREAM on both machines (per-CCD placement on TR is what makes this achievable — §14.2); attainment ledgered.
- Pattern utilities: transpose, symmetrization, Gustavson-style SpGEMM (needed by AMG's Galerkin triple product later), graph views for FrankenNetworkx interop (partitioning experiments).

## Acceptance criteria
Format round-trips lossless; SpMV exact vs dense reference on random + FEM-patterned fixtures; ≥85% STREAM attainment at large sizes, both ISAs, ledgered; deterministic assembly verified across thread counts (G5).

## Testing
G0 properties: (A^T)^T=A, SpMV linearity, format-conversion equivalence; adversarial patterns (empty rows, dense rows, single-column); perf lane vs targets. Logs: per-format bandwidth attainment, format-choice recommendations per pattern class.
close_reason: fs-sparse v1 correctness core landed: canonical CSR + BSR + SELL-C-sigma, deterministic COO assembly (stable (row,col,insertion_seq) canonicalization, stream-order-invariance tested bitwise), SpMV/SpMM, transpose/symmetrize/Gustavson SpGEMM. Headline: CSR==BSR==SELL SpMV BITWISE (designed via ascending-column mul_add accumulation; BSR fill-zero inertness proven in module docs; SELL stores true row lengths so pads are never read). 19 lib tests + conformance battery green on BOTH ISAs; cross-ISA golden hash 0xbcf552b6c5bfaed6 IDENTICAL on M4 Pro and Threadripper (trj /data/tmp/sparse_verify.1raqiO/run.log). clippy -D warnings clean, fmt applied, xtask check-all 16 crates 0 violations. Complete CONTRACT.md (6 numbered invariants, no-claim boundaries). The >=85% STREAM roofline acceptance criterion + parallel tiled assembly + SIMD kernels split to frankensim-wsbf (dependent on fs-tilelang wf9.11 and autotuner wf9.12, which it needs; full context recorded there).

title: Flagship pipelines: ornithoid aircraft, seismic frame, laminar vessel
description:
## Mission
The three flagship pipelines (plan §15) plus the PV-era marquee demo. The flagships are NOT demos; they are the forcing functions that keep every layer honest — each exercises the full stack end-to-end and each has an explicit "what breaks first" clause.

## The flagships
1. Ornithoid multi-inlet aircraft — L/D × certified stability × maneuverability: F-rep + IGA-shell parameterization (manifold harmonics + FFD), BEM/FMM + vortex-particle screening with e-raced generations (dominated wings die at 10–20% of their solve), cumulant LBM/LES refinement with DWR adaptivity keyed to L/D, Koopman surrogates + SOS Lyapunov certified regions of attraction, NSGA-III Pareto atlas where every point carries its Error Ledger + stability certificate + explain() lineage.
2. Seismic-minimal building frame — minimum material, certified fragility: FrankenNetworkx ground structure → PDHG LP/SOCP layout → trust-region sizing with buckling constraints → fiber-section nonlinear time history (Kanai–Tajimi + recorded motions, variational integrators) → MLMC with e-process stopping → CVaR-constrained mass minimization with constructability rules. Deliverable includes an anytime-valid certificate that collapse-proxy probability meets target.
3. Laminar-pour vessel — the spout that never dribbles: F-rep vessel + level-set lip parameterization, Orr–Sommerfeld modal growth via fs-cheb spectral collocation as differentiable laminarity proxy, free-surface cumulant LBM validation across tilt/viscosity schedules, Plateau–Rayleigh breakup scoring, CVaR robustification over the fluid family, LUMEN spectral render of the pour (the marketing shot and the physics are the same bytes).

## Members
flagship-topopt-demo (P2 marquee: topology optimization on a raw SDF, no mesh in the loop, composed error certificate), flagship-ornithoid, flagship-seismic, flagship-vessel, flagship-e2e-suite.

## Success Criteria
Each flagship runs end-to-end from a FrankenScript study file, replayable from seed + ledger entry, with staged smoke/full e2e scripts and honest "what breaks first" mitigations exercised.

title: P2 marquee demo: topology optimization on raw SDF, no mesh in loop, composed error certificate, replayable
description:
## What
THE P2 MARQUEE DEMO (plan §16.1): topology optimization on a RAW SDF — no mesh in the loop — with a COMPOSED ERROR CERTIFICATE. This is the single demo that proves the continuum thesis: geometry (SDF) → physics (CutFEM) → gradient (adjoint) → optimizer (SIMP/level-set) → certificate (Error Ledger), with zero meshing anywhere.

## Scope
- The study: a canonical compliance-minimization problem (MBB/cantilever-class) specified as a FrankenScript program — SDF design domain, CutFEM elasticity, Helmholtz-filtered SIMP densities (or level-set variant), adjoint sensitivities, volume constraint, budgets declared.
- The certificate: every reported compliance value carries its composed Error Ledger — geometry/interpolation error (SDF chart model) + discretization (DWR estimate) + algebraic residual (solver tolerance) + any statistical component — the P4 promise materialized in a demo artifact.
- The performance claim: per-iteration cost compatible with "interactive cadence" loops (no remeshing spikes — flat iteration timing measured and shown).
- Deliverables: replayable golden ledger; auto-generated lab notebook (fs-report) with objective trace, error attribution, LUMEN renders of the evolving design (sphere-traced, no meshing even for pictures); determinism: the whole study replays bit-exact from seed.
- E2E test script (staged smoke/full): smoke = coarse grid few iterations (PR-gate-runnable); full = production resolution (nightly); both with rich structured logging (per-iteration objective/constraint/error-ledger/timing records).

## Why this bead exists separately
It is the P2 EXIT CRITERION and the forcing function proving MORPH∘FLUX∘ASCENT∘HELM compose — the first true vertical slice at production quality (the PV skeleton's grown-up sibling).

## Acceptance criteria
Runs end-to-end from IR on both ISAs; final designs match literature-class compliance envelopes; certificate components verified against direct measurement (refined-reference comparison); replay equality; notebook complete with reproducing IR.

## Testing
The demo IS a test (golden ledger in nightly CI). Additional: seeded-failure drills (broken adjoint, budget exhaustion mid-study) produce correct structured outcomes. Logs: the full per-iteration forensic record.

title: Flagship: ornithoid multi-inlet aircraft — BEM/VPM screen, e-raced, LBM/LES refine, SOS-certified ROA, NSGA-III certified Pareto atlas
description:
## What
Flagship 1 (plan §15.1): the ORNITHOID MULTI-INLET AIRCRAFT — L/D × certified stability × maneuverability, end to end. The forcing function for the aero stack, e-racing, surrogates, and SOS certificates.

## The five stages (each an integration milestone with its own fixture)
1. PARAMETERIZE: bird-like body as F-rep (fuselage blends + IGA-shell wing surfaces); inlets as smooth R-function Booleans with positions/lip shapes as design variables; global form via manifold harmonics (~200 coefficients) + local FFD near inlets; ALL levers expose Jacobian actions.
2. SCREEN WIDE: BEM panel method + Kutta + kernel-independent FMM; vortex-particle wakes for FLAPPING gaits; hundreds of candidates per generation raced under E-RACED GENERATIONS — dominated wings die at 10–20% of their solve, cores reassigned within a millisecond (P7 at flagship scale).
3. REFINE SURVIVORS: cumulant LBM/LES on sparse VDB lattices with SDF-sampled curved boundaries; forces via momentum exchange; LES honesty labels attached (model cards); DWR adaptivity keyed to the L/D functional.
4. TRIM AND CERTIFY STABILITY: Koopman/DMD reduced flight-dynamics models with conformal e-bands around each trim state; SOS LYAPUNOV SYNTHESIS produces a CERTIFIED region of attraction — "stable" becomes a proven basin volume; maneuverability scored as certified reachable-set proxies under bounded control effort.
5. PARETO: NSGA-III over (L/D, ROA volume, maneuver metric, inlet mass-flow satisfaction) with gradient polish (BEM-level adjoints + surrogate gradients, honestly labeled — LBM adjoint deferred [M]); deliverable: a CERTIFIED PARETO ATLAS — every point carries its Error Ledger, stability certificate, and explain() lineage.

## What breaks first (the honest clause, mitigations ARE the plan)
LES cost per candidate — mitigated by the multi-fidelity ladder (panel → surrogate → LBM) and e-racing; if SOS scaling disappoints, ROA falls back to sampled Lyapunov with conformal bounds [F].

## Acceptance criteria
Full pipeline runs from one FrankenScript study; stage-wise fixtures green (screening rates, refinement agreement panel-vs-LBM within model-form evidence, certified ROA on a fixture trim state); Pareto atlas artifact complete with per-point certificates; e-racing payoff measured (generations/core-hour vs non-raced, ledgered); replayable from seed.

## Testing
Staged e2e scripts (toy geometry smoke → reduced-fidelity mid → full nightly/weekly); per-stage structured logging (candidate genealogies, race records, certificate artifacts). What-breaks-first drills: LES budget exhaustion mid-campaign → graceful degradation verified.

title: Flagship: seismic-minimal frame — ground structure LP, code-checked sizing, fiber time-history, MLMC + e-stop fragility, CVaR mass min
description:
## What
Flagship 2 (plan §15.2): the SEISMIC-MINIMAL BUILDING FRAME — minimum material with CERTIFIED fragility. The forcing function for structures at scale, MLMC+e-stopping, and constructability.

## The five stages
1. LAYOUT: FrankenNetworkx ground structure (millions of candidate members under fabrication rules); member-force layout LP/SOCP via the in-house PDHG solver; MICHELL-CONTINUUM LIMITS as analytic sanity oracles on subproblems (`:oracle (michell :tol 0.08)`).
2. SIZE NONLINEARLY: trust-region Newton–Krylov sizing with Euler + code-based (AISC-class) buckling constraints, joint-cost regularization; arclength continuation spot-checks global buckling modes.
3. NONLINEAR TIME HISTORY: fiber-section beam-columns (Mander concrete, Menegotto–Pinto steel via fs-material) under suites of recorded motions + Kanai–Tajimi stochastic ensembles (fs-scenario); VARIATIONAL INTEGRATORS keep long-duration response drift-free; the batched-small-dense hot loop earns its keep here (the stated performance mitigation).
4. QUANTIFY RISK ANYTIME-VALIDLY: MLMC over motion ensembles with E-PROCESS STOPPING on P(peak drift > limit) — the study STOPS ITSELF the moment the fragility estimate is decision-grade at the requested confidence, valid at that data-dependent stopping time BY CONSTRUCTION (Bet 5's flagship moment; Appendix C: `(uq.probability (exceeds (peak-drift resp) 2e-2) :stop (e-process :alpha 0.05))`).
5. OPTIMIZE UNDER TAIL RISK: CVaR-constrained mass minimization (Rockafellar–Uryasev smooth form); constructability pass snaps members to catalog sections and enforces angle/joint rules as graph constraints (fs-constraint repair machinery).

## Deliverable
Frame + fragility curves + an ANYTIME-VALID CERTIFICATE that the collapse-proxy probability meets target — auditable via explain().

## What breaks first
Nonlinear time-history volume — mitigated by MLMC level design, surrogate screening of motion suites, and the fiber-beam batched kernels.

## Acceptance criteria
Full pipeline from the Appendix C study program (the canonical fixture); stage fixtures green (PDHG vs Michell, sizing code-compliance, hysteresis vs published, e-stopped fragility with verified coverage); constructability output passes independent rule-check; replayable; e-stopping savings vs fixed-N measured and ledgered.

## Testing
Staged e2e (small frame smoke → mid → full); drills: motion-suite budget exhaustion, infeasible constraint sets (unsat-core diagnostics fire). Logs: per-stage records, fragility CS trajectories, stopping decisions, certificate artifacts.

title: Flagship: laminar-pour vessel — Orr-Sommerfeld objective (fs-cheb), free-surface LBM validation, CVaR over fluid family, spectral pour render
description:
## What
Flagship 3 (plan §15.3): the LAMINAR-POUR VESSEL — the spout that never dribbles. The forcing function for free-surface LBM, fs-cheb spectral stability, level-set topology change, and robust design.

## The five stages
1. PARAMETERIZE: F-rep vessel of revolution (fs-cheb profile revolved) + free-form lip; spout region driven by LEVEL-SET VELOCITY parameterization — topology of the lip channel MAY CHANGE and that is fine (the level-set method's raison d'être here).
2. STABILITY OBJECTIVE, CHEAPLY: quasi-steady thin-film flow along lip streamlines; ORR–SOMMERFELD EIGENPROBLEMS solved by fs-cheb spectral collocation give modal growth rates σ₁…σ₈ along the pour path — the objective is min-max certified modal growth, a DIFFERENTIABLE PROXY for "laminar" (Appendix C: `(min (perturbation-growth pour :at lip :modes (1 .. 8)))`).
3. VALIDATE WITH PHYSICS: free-surface cumulant LBM pours across the tilt schedule and viscosity band (Carreau parameters spanning target fluids); PLATEAU–RAYLEIGH breakup model scores the free jet; spill/dribble indicator from contact-line behavior on the lip SDF.
4. ROBUSTIFY: CVaR over the viscosity/rate band so the spout is laminar for THE FAMILY of liquids, not the nominal one; e-raced candidates under the LBM validator.
5. DELIVER: optimized vessel + LUMEN SPECTRAL RENDER of the pour (Woodcock-tracked LBM density field) — THE MARKETING SHOT AND THE PHYSICS ARE THE SAME BYTES.

## What breaks first (and the honest handling)
Contact-line modeling fidelity — a GENUINELY OPEN problem — handled by BRACKETING contact-angle models (evidence-model-ledger's model-bracketing mechanism) and reporting the objective's sensitivity band in the Error Ledger RATHER THAN PRETENDING CERTAINTY. The deliverable says how wrong it might be.

## Acceptance criteria
Full pipeline from the Appendix C spout study (the canonical fixture); Orr–Sommerfeld machinery validated (plane-Poiseuille Re_c reference); free-surface pours mass-conserving across the tilt schedule; bracketing runs produce the documented sensitivity band in the final report; CVaR robustification demonstrably beats nominal-only optimization on off-nominal fluids; render deliverable generated from the SAME ledgered field data; replayable.

## Testing
Staged e2e (2D-ish thin film smoke → coarse 3D pour → full); drills: an unstable pour candidate correctly scored and eliminated; contact-line bracket divergence correctly widens reported bands. Logs: growth-rate tables along pour paths, mass ledgers, bracket comparisons, render provenance.

title: Flagship e2e suite: staged smoke/mid/full golden ledgers, cross-flagship audits, failure drills, forensic logging
description:
## What
The flagship E2E suite: all three flagships as REPLAYABLE, STAGED, FORENSICALLY-LOGGED end-to-end test assets — the system-level Gauntlet tier that keeps the whole continuum honest forever after.

## Scope
- Staged fidelity per flagship: SMOKE (minutes — toy geometry, coarse physics, few iterations; PR-gate-runnable), MID (hour-class — reduced resolution, nightly), FULL (production-scale, weekly/on-demand) — each stage with its own golden ledger and acceptance envelopes.
- Golden-ledger discipline: every stage's acceptance run stored content-addressed; CI REPLAYS golden ledgers and compares artifact hashes (deterministic stages) / envelope metrics (stochastic-labeled stages).
- Cross-flagship audits: shared-machinery consistency (the same fs-material steel in frame + any structural use; the same LBM core in vessel + ornithoid; e-racing behavior identical across consumers) — integration bugs surface as cross-flagship deltas.
- Failure drills as first-class tests: budget exhaustion mid-campaign, cancellation storms during generations, ledger crash-recovery mid-study, model-form escalation triggers — each with expected structured outcomes.
- Logging standard (the exemplar for the whole project): per-stage structured JSON streams — candidate genealogies, race records, solver residuals, error-ledger snapshots, certificate artifacts, timing attributions — sufficient to diagnose ANY failure from logs + ledger alone, and to regenerate every figure in the docs.
- Report generation verified: each full run emits its lab notebook with reproducing IR; notebooks are diffed release-to-release (the system's own progress report).

## Acceptance criteria
All three smoke stages green in PR CI; mid stages green nightly on both ISAs; full stages green on the reference machines with envelopes met; every drill has a passing expected-outcome test; replay equality holds per stage class.

## Testing
This IS the test bead — the capstone of the testing pyramid (unit → G0-G5 → layer conformance → flagship e2e).

title: L3 FLUX: physics kernel
description:
## Mission
L3 FLUX: the physics kernel. Turn charts into discrete complexes and cochains, apply and invert physical operators at roofline speed, integrate in time with preserved structure, couple physics passively, and hand ASCENT exact discrete gradients and honest error bars.

## Core thesis (plan §8, Bet 3)
Built on Finite Element Exterior Calculus from day one: fields are cochains (discrete differential forms), the exterior derivative d is exact/combinatorial, all approximation lives in the Hodge stars. The discrete de Rham sequence is EXACT — div∘curl = 0 to machine precision — killing spurious pressure/EM modes and checkerboarding by construction, not stabilization folklore. Three frontends share one machinery: body-fitted simplicial FEEC, CutFEM-on-SDF (the marquee bridge — FEM-grade accuracy on level-set geometry with ZERO meshing, enabling interactive topology-optimization loops), and IGA (geometry basis = analysis basis; the CAD→mesh information massacre never happens).

## Also owned here (incl. patch revisions A/D/E)
- fs-regime: nondimensionalization + solver-validity gates — no FLUX solve without a RegimeReport; prevents "beautiful, fast, wrong" simulations.
- fs-scenario: typed boundary conditions, load cases, frames, time histories, stochastic ensembles — BCs are where solvers silently become invalid.
- fs-material: constitutive laws as first-class models with tangents, thermodynamic admissibility, calibration data, and uncertainty envelopes.
- fs-opdsl: the typed operator compiler — residual/JVP/VJP/adjoint/DWR/preconditioner-block/MMS kernels generated from ONE mathematical source of truth, preventing primal/adjoint drift.
- Port-Hamiltonian Dirac coupling: partitioned multiphysics passive by construction (no added-mass instabilities).
- Adjoint-native everything: differentiate through the solution (IFT), never through solver iterations; goal-oriented (DWR) adaptivity keyed to ASCENT's actual objective.

## Members
fs-scenario, fs-material, fs-regime, fs-opdsl, fs-feec-complexes, fs-feec-highorder, fs-feec-cohomology, fs-cutfem, fs-iga, solver-stack, solver-dd, fs-solid-elastic, fs-solid-advanced, fs-solid-stability, fs-solid-contact, fs-ns-feec, fs-lbm-core, fs-lbm-extensions, fs-bem-fmm, fs-vpm, fs-time-integrators, fs-couple-ports, dwr-adaptivity, fs-adjoint-core, fs-uq, flux-conformance.

## Success Criteria
G1 convergence orders verified for every frontend×family×BC; G2 canonical benchmarks green; adjoint checks green (a solver without a passing gradient check cannot merge).

title: fs-scenario: typed BC/load-case algebra — frames, time histories, stochastic ensembles (gusts/ground motions), compatibility checks
description:
## What
`fs-scenario` (patch Rev D): the boundary-condition and load-case ALGEBRA. A Region answers "what is the domain?"; a PhysicsModel answers "which equations?"; a Scenario answers "WHAT IS BEING DONE TO IT?" — and it must be a typed value with dimensional analysis, provenance, and validity checks, never informal JSON.

## Why
Boundary conditions are routinely where simulations become invalid — often more important than the solver. This prevents a class of agent mistakes NO solver can fix, and gives UQ, optimization, and the ledger one common representation for load cases and environmental variability.

## Scope
- Typed BCs: Dirichlet/Neumann/Robin per physics with Qty-dimensioned values; inlet/outlet conditions WITH COMPATIBILITY CHECKS (e.g., mass-flow inlet declaring `compatibility: incompressible` — net-flux consistency verified at admission); wall/slip/traction; profile-valued BCs via fs-cheb function objects ("the inlet profile is THIS FUNCTION").
- Reference frames and MOVING frames (rotating/accelerating — the tilt schedule of the vessel pour `(ramp 0deg 65deg 3s)` is a moving-frame schedule); frame transforms via fs-ga motors.
- Time histories and event schedules: typed signals (ramps, spectra-driven, recorded traces) with units and interpolation contracts.
- Stochastic scenario ensembles: seeded generators — wind gusts (Dryden/von Kármán spectra), GROUND MOTIONS (Kanai–Tajimi-class spectral models + recorded-motion suites like PEER sets — the seismic flagship's `(uq.ground-motion ...)` node lowers to this), fluid-property bands (Carreau parameter families for the vessel's robustness sweep). Replayable from seed (Philox streams).
- Load combinations: typed combination rules (code-style factored combinations for the frame flagship's AISC-class checks).
- Contact/friction laws as scenario components (pairing with fs-solid-contact).
- Environmental fields (gravity, ambient temperature/pressure) explicit, never defaulted silently.
- Ledger `scenarios` table rows: IR, seed, frames, load cases, environment, validity — the Five Explicits applied to loads.

## Acceptance criteria
Scenario values round-trip IR↔memory↔ledger; compatibility checks catch seeded violations (incompatible inlet flux, inconsistent frame chains) with structured fixes; ensembles reproduce bitwise from seed; Kanai–Tajimi realizations match target spectra statistically (verified spectral estimates).

## Testing
G0: frame-transform composition laws; unit coherence across all BC types (G3 rescaling); ensemble determinism (G5). Logs: scenario validation reports, ensemble seeds, spectra-match statistics.

title: Solver stack: matrix-free p-MG + SA-AMG coarse, Chebyshev, mixed-precision Krylov, block/Schur — resumable/adjoint-equipped
description:
## What
The solver stack (plan §8.9): matrix-free p-multigrid with SA-AMG coarse solve, Chebyshev smoothing, mixed-precision Krylov wrappers, and block preconditioners — ALL resumable, cancellable, deterministic-mode capable, and adjoint-equipped.

## Scope
- p-multigrid: level hierarchy over polynomial orders (high-order FEEC operators applied MATRIX-FREE via sum factorization at every level; assemble ONLY the lowest-order coarse problem); smoothed-aggregation AMG (fs-sparse-precond) on that assembled coarse level; Chebyshev smoothing throughout (many-core-honest — no sequential dependencies).
- Krylov wrappers: CG/GMRES/MINRES with mixed-precision inner work + f64 refinement (fs-la-mixed-precision policy engine); flexible variants where preconditioners vary; deterministic dot products (fixed-shape reductions).
- Saddle-point systems (Stokes, contact, incompressibility): block preconditioners with Schur-complement approximations (pressure mass/PCD-class options documented per physics).
- The four contract obligations on EVERY solver (AGENTS.md invariants): resumable (SolverState serialize/pause/fork per fs-exec-speculation); cancellable (poll points each iteration/level); deterministic-mode capable; ADJOINT-EQUIPPED (transposed-system solves sharing the same preconditioner infrastructure — the fs-ad-adjoint-infra IFT contract's enabler).
- Error transparency: residual histories, convergence diagnostics, and achieved-tolerance evidence flow to the Error Ledger; a stalled solve returns a structured diagnosis (preconditioner quality indicators), not a timeout mystery.

## Acceptance criteria
Grid/order-independent-ish iteration counts on Poisson/elasticity ladders (envelope-gated); matrix-free p-MG matches assembled-reference solutions to tolerance; mixed-precision path achieves f64 accuracy with measured speedup on the bandwidth-starved machine; pause/resume/fork bit-exact continuation (G4); transposed solves converge comparably to primal (adjoint readiness).

## Testing
G1-graded solver ladders; G0: preconditioner symmetry/SPD preservation where claimed; G4 storms mid-solve; G5 determinism across thread counts. Logs: per-level residual histories, iteration counts vs ladder, precision-ladder decisions, checkpoint events.

title: Domain decomposition: BDDC/FETI-DP w/ sheaf-harmonic coarse spaces, CCD-aligned subdomains [F]
description:
## What
Domain decomposition for extreme core counts (plan §8.9 [F]): BDDC/FETI-DP-family methods whose COARSE SPACE is computed as approximate HARMONIC SECTIONS OF THE INTERFACE SHEAF — the same cellular-sheaf machinery as watertightness (Bet 11 earning its keep twice).

## Scope
- Substructuring: subdomain partitioning aligned with CCD topology (the 96–128-core machines' L3 islands are natural subdomains — soft-NUMA-as-DD), local solvers per subdomain (solver-stack instances), interface operator assembly.
- BDDC preconditioner: primal coarse DOFs (corners/edges/faces selection), weighted averaging operators, and the sheaf-theoretic coarse space: interface sheaf whose stalks are local trace spaces, restriction maps from subdomain solutions; approximate harmonic sections (low-energy representatives) = coarse basis — mathematically principled coarse spaces that adapt to coefficient jumps.
- FETI-DP variant sharing infrastructure; condition-number estimation and coarse-space enrichment policy (adaptive BDDC-class) when estimates exceed thresholds.
- Cancellation/resumability inherited (each subdomain solve is a scoped, resumable unit — a cancelled sweep loses nothing but in-flight subdomains).

## Why staged at P6
Single-machine CCD-aware p-MG covers the plan's committed scale; DD is the extreme-scale path and the sheaf coarse space is [F/M] — promoted only with Gauntlet evidence (§17 risk: [M] math fails validation → [S] spine ships regardless).

## Acceptance criteria
Condition-number bounds match BDDC theory on model problems (log²(H/h) scaling verified); coefficient-jump robustness demonstrated (jumps up to 10⁶ with bounded iterations); sheaf coarse space ≥ standard corner/edge coarse space on the adversarial fixture set (measured, honestly reported if not); CCD-aligned partitioning shows the expected locality wins.

## Testing
G0: preconditioner properties; scaling studies (subdomain counts sweep) ledgered; comparison harness sheaf-coarse vs classical-coarse. Logs: condition estimates, coarse-space dimensions, per-subdomain timings.

title: fs-time: variational/symplectic, SE(3) Lie-group, generalized-alpha, IMEX/exponential, embedded-pair PI adaptivity — all resumable + adjointed
description:
## What
`fs-time`: structure-preserving time integration (plan §8.5) — variational/symplectic, Lie-group SE(3), structural-dynamics, IMEX, exponential, and adaptive embedded-pair integrators; every one a resumable state machine (P7) and every one shipping its adjoint (§8.7).

## Scope
- Variational/symplectic integrators for conservative dynamics: discrete-Lagrangian construction (Marsden–West lineage); energy behavior over 10⁶ steps that dissipative-by-accident schemes cannot match (the seismic flagship's long-duration drift-free requirement, §15.2 step 3).
- Lie-group integrators on SE(3) for rods and rigid bodies (fs-ga motors as the state representation): no quaternion drift, no renormalization hacks; Munthe-Kaas/RKMK-class and variational Lie-group variants.
- Newmark/generalized-α for structural dynamics with CONTROLLABLE numerical dissipation (high-frequency damping without destroying the physics — parameter exposure with documented spectral properties).
- IMEX splittings for stiff-nonstiff mixtures; exponential integrators where the stiff part is linear (fs-la Krylov-exponential actions).
- Embedded-pair adaptivity with PI step-size controllers (smooth step sequences, rejection handling); budget-aware (P4: step-size floors tied to wall-clock budgets with structured warnings).
- The two universal obligations: resumable SolverState (checkpoint mid-trajectory, ledger-spill via revolve for adjoints) and DISCRETE ADJOINTS of each integrator (adjoint of the stepper, not just the RHS — verified like everything else).

## Acceptance criteria
Symplectic: bounded energy oscillation (no secular drift) over 10⁶-step Hamiltonian fixtures vs RK4's visible drift (the demonstrable payoff); SE(3): rigid-body gyroscope fixtures conserve momentum maps to roundoff-class; generalized-α: spectral-radius-at-infinity behavior matches theory; embedded pairs: order verified (G1-style) + controller smoothness; adjoint-vs-Dual gradient checks per integrator (merge gate).

## Testing
G0: symplecticity residuals (Jacobian ω-preservation spot checks), group-manifold retention (‖det(R)−1‖, motor normalization) over long runs; G4 pause/resume equality mid-trajectory. Logs: energy/momentum traces, step-size sequences, adjoint check tables.
