title: fs-solid elasticity: linear->hyperelastic (via fs-material), TDNNS locking-free mixed, all 3 frontends
description:
## What
`fs-solid` elasticity core (plan §8.2): linear elasticity through finite-strain hyperelasticity with locking-free mixed formulations — FEEC-native, CutFEM-and-body-fitted-and-IGA capable.

## Scope
- Linear elasticity: FEEC displacement formulation with the standard verification ladder; the P2-milestone workhorse.
- Finite-strain hyperelasticity: Neo-Hookean/Mooney–Rivlin/Ogden energies VIA fs-material (constitutive laws live there; this bead owns kinematics, weak forms, and Newton loops with line search/trust-region globalization + consistent tangents from material cards).
- Locking-free mixed formulations: TDNNS-style (tangential-displacement normal-normal-stress) / weakly-imposed-symmetry FEEC elements where near-incompressibility or bending-domination demands them; documented element-selection guidance per regime (fs-regime's structural indicators inform the choice).
- All three frontends: body-fitted simplicial, CutFEM-on-SDF (the topology-optimization path — topo-simp solves THIS physics on octrees), IGA where spline charts exist.
- fs-scenario BC integration (traction/displacement/contact-ready boundary terms); Evidence-typed results (energy, stresses with error indicators).
- Defined via fs-opdsl where the DSL covers it (residual + adjoint + MMS generated); hand paths pass the same gates.

## Acceptance criteria
G1: optimal orders across element families × frontends × BC types; G2: Cook's membrane envelope green; locking battery: near-incompressible (ν→0.5) and thin-bending fixtures show NO locking degradation (the TDNNS payoff, measured against standard elements' documented failure); Newton robustness on large-strain fixtures (buckling-adjacent states converge with globalization).

## Testing
G0: objectivity (superposed rigid motions), material-frame indifference through the fs-material interface, adjoint consistency (merge gate); patch tests exact. Logs: convergence tables, Newton iteration histories, locking-indicator metrics.

title: fs-solid advanced: Cosserat rods on SE(3), fiber-section beams (Mander/Menegotto-Pinto), J2 plasticity
description:
## What
`fs-solid` advanced structural elements (plan §8.2): geometrically exact Cosserat rods, fiber-section beams, and inelasticity — the seismic flagship's structural vocabulary.

## Scope
- Geometrically exact Cosserat rods: Lie-group state on SE(3) (fs-ga motors; fs-time SE(3) integrators for dynamics), shear/torsion/extension/bending strains, quadrature along centerlines; the member/strut model for frames and lattice structures (topo-truss's nonlinear sizing pass re-analyzes with THESE).
- FIBER-SECTION BEAM-COLUMNS: cross-sections discretized into fibers, each with a uniaxial fs-material law — Mander confined/unconfined concrete + Menegotto–Pinto steel for the reinforced-concrete frame use-case; section state update = the batched-small-dense hot loop (fs-la-batched — §15.2 explicitly names this pairing as the performance mitigation for nonlinear time-history volume).
- J2 plasticity integration at continuum level (return mapping via fs-material) for solid elements; distributed-plasticity beam variants (force-based elements with section integration along length).
- Cyclic/hysteretic correctness: the make-or-break for seismic — energy dissipation per cycle, stiffness/strength degradation tracking, residual drift accumulation.

## Acceptance criteria
Rod statics: Elastica/helical benchmark curves matched; rod dynamics: energy/momentum behavior under fs-time symplectic integration; fiber sections: moment-curvature hysteresis matches published El Centro-class fiber-model results (the G2 envelope case); batched section updates hit the fs-la-batched throughput targets (this IS the flagship's inner loop — perf-gated).

## Testing
G0: rod strain objectivity, section-state determinism; hysteresis loop battery vs published data; adjoint checks where gradients claimed (sizing derivatives). G4: cancellation mid-time-history leaves resumable state. Logs: hysteresis data, section-update throughput, energy-dissipation ledgers.

title: fs-solid stability: LOBPCG buckling pencils, pseudo-arclength continuation, Koiter post-buckling [F]
description:
## What
`fs-solid` stability analysis (plan §8.2): buckling eigenproblems, pseudo-arclength continuation, and Koiter post-buckling asymptotics — because the steel-minimizing frame that ignores buckling IS A PAPERCLIP.

## Scope
- Geometric-stiffness buckling eigenproblems: (K + λ·K_G)φ = 0 pencils solved matrix-free via fs-la-eigen LOBPCG (the named consumer); linearized buckling loads + modes as standard outputs with Evidence (discretization-error indicators on λ).
- PSEUDO-ARCLENGTH CONTINUATION through limit points: path-following of equilibrium branches where load-control fails (snap-through/snap-back); branch-point detection (singular tangent stiffness monitoring), branch switching; resumable path state (a continuation run is a checkpointable trajectory).
- Koiter-style post-buckling asymptotics [F]: initial post-buckling coefficients (stable/unstable bifurcation classification, imperfection sensitivity — the a/b coefficients that tell you whether a design is imperfection-tolerant) — flagged frontier; sampled nonlinear continuation is the fallback oracle.
- Optimization integration: buckling constraints with eigenvalue derivatives (via fs-la-eigen adjoint hooks) feeding topo-truss sizing and topo-simp eigen-constraints; clustered-eigenvalue handling (the classic differentiability trap — documented smooth aggregation approach).

## Acceptance criteria
Plate/column buckling loads match analytic references (G2 envelope: plate buckling named in the canonical set); continuation traces snap-through fixtures (arch/cylindrical shell classics) matching published load-deflection curves; Koiter coefficients validated against literature fixtures; eigenvalue derivatives pass gradient gates including near-cluster cases.

## Testing
G0: pencil symmetry, mode orthogonality; continuation step-control robustness battery; imperfection-sensitivity spot checks vs full nonlinear runs. Logs: eigenvalue tables, continuation paths, branch events, derivative checks.

title: fs-solid contact: IPC barrier formulation on SDF charts, intersection-free, differentiable [F]
description:
## What
`fs-solid` contact (plan §8.2 [F]): SDF-native barrier contact in the Incremental Potential Contact (IPC) family — smooth, robust, INTERSECTION-FREE BY CONSTRUCTION.

## Scope
- Barrier formulation: log-barrier potentials on unsigned distances activating within a threshold; the IPC guarantee — trajectories remain intersection-free because the barrier diverges before penetration (no penetration-then-push-out cheats).
- THE FRANKENSIM ADVANTAGE (why this is "unusually easy for us"): penetration-depth/distance queries against SDF charts are MORPH ONE-LINERS (geom-queries clearance fields) — where generic IPC implementations fight mesh-distance computation, we query certified distance fields directly; mesh-mesh contact falls back to point-triangle distance machinery (fs-rep-mesh).
- Friction: smoothed Coulomb (IPC-style lagged friction) with documented smoothing-parameter semantics; fs-scenario contact-law components supply coefficients.
- Solver integration: barrier Hessians into Newton with line-search filtering (continuous collision detection gating step sizes — conservative CCD via certified distance bounds); adaptive barrier stiffness policy.
- Adjoints: contact-force derivatives for grasp/fixture/assembly optimization (smooth barrier = differentiable contact, one of IPC's strategic payoffs; documented validity near the barrier's stiff region).

## Acceptance criteria
Zero interpenetration across the torture battery (stacked objects, sliding blocks, tight clearances) — verified by certified distance audits each step; friction cone consistency tests; energy behavior physically plausible (barrier energy accounted in ledgers); gradient checks on contact-inclusive objectives.

## Testing
G0: barrier smoothness/monotonicity; CCD conservativeness (no tunneling at large steps — property-tested with adversarial velocities); comparison vs analytic Hertz-class fixtures (within model expectations, honestly labeled). Logs: contact-set evolution, barrier stiffness adaptation, penetration audits (must be zero).

title: fs-flux NS: H(div) pressure-robust FEEC Navier-Stokes, DG convection, LES (labeled), continuous adjoint
description:
## What
Incompressible Navier–Stokes, FEEC-native (plan §8.3 [F]): H(div)-conforming velocity with PRESSURE-ROBUST discretization — a modern correctness property most production codes lack — plus LES closures with honesty labels.

## Scope
- H(div)-conforming velocity spaces (Raviart–Thomas/BDM-class in FEEC language) with exactly divergence-free discrete velocities: PRESSURE-ROBUSTNESS (velocity error independent of pressure — no pressure-induced pollution; the exactness of the discrete de Rham sequence cashing out in fluid accuracy).
- Convection: upwinded DG treatment for the H(div) setting; energy-stability documented.
- Time stepping: projection variants (fast) AND fully coupled variants (robust) — both, deliberately; IMEX via fs-time; saddle-point solves via solver-stack block preconditioners.
- Turbulence honesty (§8.3): LES with Smagorinsky/WALE subgrid models for resolved-eddy regimes; RANS-style closures ONLY as clearly-labeled low-fidelity screens (model cards mark them; the multi-fidelity optimizer is the answer to "LES everywhere is unaffordable" — NOT wishful meshing); model-form evidence on every turbulent result (evidence-model-ledger: closure discrepancy dominates drag error — the system must SAY so).
- Adjoints: continuous-adjoint NS for gradient-based fluid shape steps (the plan's §8.7 honest position: this, not LBM adjoints, is the committed fluid-gradient path).
- fs-scenario inlet/outlet/wall BCs with compatibility checks; fs-regime gating (Re-appropriate model selection).

## Acceptance criteria
G1 MMS (steady + transient, slope-gated); G2: lid-driven cavity Re1000 vs Ghia centerlines + Taylor–Green vortex decay curves + cylinder Cd/Strouhal Re100 — the three canonical envelopes green; pressure-robustness demonstrated (velocity error unchanged under pressure-field manipulation fixtures); divergence-free verified to roundoff; adjoint gradient gates green.

## Testing
G0: discrete energy balance (no spurious production), div u = 0 bitwise-class; LES: TGV energy-spectrum sanity at moderate Re with resolution-honesty labels attached to results. Logs: benchmark tables vs envelopes, divergence audits, adjoint checks, closure model cards on every run.

title: fs-lbm core: cumulant D3Q19/27 on sparse VDB tiles, Bouzidi SDF boundaries, lattice-scaling assistant, >=1.0/0.6 GLUP/s
description:
## What
`fs-lbm` core (plan §8.3 [S]): lattice Boltzmann — THE MANY-CORE QUEEN. D3Q19/D3Q27 on sparse FrankenVDB tiles with cumulant collision, SDF-sampled curved boundaries, and the lattice-scaling assistant.

## Scope
- D3Q19/D3Q27 stream+collide on SPARSE FrankenVDB tiles (shared substrate with fs-rep-sdf — the geometry IS the lattice's sparsity pattern): AA-pattern or esoteric-twist in-place streaming (bandwidth-optimal), tile-boundary exchange via tile-layout halos.
- CUMULANT/central-moment collision operators for high-Re stability (the modern replacement for BGK's instability; well-conditioned moment transforms as batched small dense).
- Curved boundaries: interpolated (Bouzidi-type) bounce-back with wall distances sampled DIRECTLY FROM SDF CHARTS (no stair-casing, no mesh — the MORPH∘FLUX bridge); momentum-exchange force evaluation (drag/lift for the ornithoid's refinement stage).
- THE LATTICE-SCALING ASSISTANT: automates dx/dt/τ/Mach bookkeeping under stability constraints — a chronic source of human LBM error that AGENTS SHOULD NEVER BE EXPOSED TO RAW; consumes fs-regime's dimensionless groups; produces Evidence-typed scaling plans with stability margins.
- Performance doctrine: embarrassingly tile-parallel, bandwidth-bound with a PRECISELY COMPUTABLE roofline (~152 B/LUP for D3Q19): targets ≥1.0 GLUP/s (M-class), ≥0.6 (TR 96c) — §14.1's headline row; kernels via fs-tilelang; deterministic mode (fixed tile order, per-tile Philox where stochastic).
- ExecMode + cancellation: poll at tile boundaries (e-racing kills LBM candidates mid-run — this is the flagship-scale cancellation consumer).

## Acceptance criteria
G2: Taylor–Green + cavity + cylinder Cd/Strouhal envelopes green at documented resolutions; Bouzidi boundaries: cylinder-drag convergence vs resolution matches published curved-boundary behavior (beats stair-step visibly); GLUP/s targets met on both machines (ledgered); scaling assistant produces stable plans across the fixture regime sweep (zero unstable runs when margins respected); bit-deterministic across thread counts.

## Testing
G0: mass/momentum conservation per step to roundoff-class (exact for the collide stencil, documented for boundaries); Galilean-invariance spot checks (cumulant payoff); G3 frame/rescaling relations via the scaling assistant. Perf lane per §14.1. Logs: GLUP/s tables, stability margins, conservation ledgers per run.

title: fs-lbm extensions: octree refinement w/ rescaling, thermal, Carreau/power-law, free-surface VOF (mass-strict)
description:
## What
`fs-lbm` extensions (plan §8.3): octree refinement, thermal coupling, non-Newtonian rheology, and FREE-SURFACE flow — the vessel flagship's physics.

## Scope
- Octree grid refinement with rescaling: level-transition physics (distribution rescaling across resolution jumps, conservative coarse-fine coupling), refinement driven by dwr-adaptivity signals or geometric criteria (near-lip bands for the vessel).
- Thermal: double-population (separate temperature distribution) with Boussinesq coupling option.
- Non-Newtonian: LOCAL relaxation-rate adaptation for power-law/Carreau rheology — "a liquid of a given viscosity is a parameter, including shear-dependent ones" (the vessel's `(carreau :mu0 0.12Pa*s :n 0.8)` node lowers to this); stability interplay with the scaling assistant (shear-thinning τ floors).
- FREE-SURFACE LBM: mass-tracking VOF-style (Körner lineage) — interface cells with fill fractions, cell conversion (fluid↔interface↔gas) with STRICT mass bookkeeping, surface-tension via curvature from the SDF/fill-fraction field, contact-line behavior on lip SDFs (the honest §15.3 caveat: contact-line modeling fidelity is a genuinely open problem — MODEL BRACKETING per evidence-model-ledger: bracket contact-angle models and report the objective's sensitivity band rather than pretending certainty).
- Pouring/sloshing scenario support: tilt schedules as moving frames (fs-scenario), inflow rate schedules; Plateau–Rayleigh breakup scoring hooks for the free jet (the vessel objective's validator component).
- Adjoint honesty (plan §8.7 [M]): free-surface LBM adjoints are a research swamp — NOT promised; gradients via surrogate/gradient-free lanes; model card says so.

## Acceptance criteria
Refinement: acoustic/shear-wave transmission across level jumps within published tolerances; thermal: Rayleigh–Bénard onset matches theory; non-Newtonian: Poiseuille profiles for power-law fluids match analytic; free surface: MASS CONSERVED to strict tolerance over long pours (the make-or-break audit), dam-break benchmark vs experiment envelope, breaking-jet qualitative battery; first spout pour end-to-end (P3 exit criterion component).

## Testing
G0: conservation ledgers (mass/momentum incl. interface cells); G3 frame invariance under tilt-schedule transforms; bracketing runs (contact-angle model A vs B) produce the documented sensitivity band. Logs: mass ledgers per step, cell-conversion statistics, interface-quality metrics.

title: fs-material: constitutive-law kernel — consistent tangents, thermodynamic admissibility, model cards, calibration + uncertainty
description:
## What
`fs-material` (patch Rev E): the constitutive-law kernel. Materials are NOT constants — they are mathematical objects with calibration data, tangent operators, thermodynamic constraints, hysteresis, failure modes, and uncertainty. Owning them in one crate (instead of scattering through solid mechanics) is what makes structural claims credible.

## Scope
- ConstitutiveLaw trait: `stress(strain, state, cx) -> Evidence<Stress>`, `tangent(...) -> Evidence<TangentOperator>` (CONSISTENT tangents — algorithmic, matching the stress update exactly, or Newton dies), `update_state(strain_path, state, cx) -> Evidence<State>`, `admissibility() -> MaterialAdmissibility`.
- Law families: isotropic/orthotropic/anisotropic linear elasticity; hyperelastic energy potentials (Neo-Hookean, Mooney–Rivlin, Ogden — the plan's §8.2 set); J2 plasticity with return mapping; damage models; the REINFORCED-CONCRETE FLAGSHIP PAIR: Mander confined-concrete + Menegotto–Pinto steel (fiber-section beams consume these — their hot loop is fs-la-batched shaped); foam/printed-material placeholders staged.
- THERMODYNAMIC GUARDRAILS (each law declares): stored energy function if applicable; dissipation inequality compliance; convexity/polyconvexity properties where known; tangent symmetry expectations; failure envelope; CALIBRATION DOMAIN (strain ranges/rates where parameters were fitted — outside = validity-domain exit flagged in Evidence).
- Model cards (evidence-model-ledger): every law ships one — assumptions, validity domain, known failures, calibration artifacts, uncertainty envelopes on parameters (feeding UQ's material-scatter KL expansions).
- Parameter calibration: fitting from experimental data (CSV via fs-io catalogs) with uncertainty quantification (posterior/confidence envelopes stored on the card).
- Homogenization hooks: homogenized laws from microstructure (topo-lattice's unit-cell results register as materials with provenance).

## Acceptance criteria
Consistent-tangent verification: tangent vs FD-of-stress on random strain paths for EVERY law (merge gate, same discipline as adjoints); return-mapping convergence + consistency on cyclic paths (Mander/Menegotto–Pinto hysteresis loops match published fixture curves); admissibility declarations machine-checked where possible (polyconvexity spot tests); calibration round-trip on synthetic data recovers known parameters.

## Testing
G0: frame-indifference (objectivity) tests — rotated strain → rotated stress; dissipation non-negativity along random cycles; unit coherence. Hysteresis fixture battery with published curves. Logs: tangent-check tables, hysteresis plots data, card completeness lint.

title: fs-bem/fs-fmm: Chebyshev bbFMM + Laplace BEM panels, Kutta condition, free wakes, BEM adjoints [F]
description:
## What
`fs-bem` + `fs-fmm` (plan §8.3 [F]): kernel-independent black-box FMM driving Laplace BEM — potential-flow panel methods with Kutta condition and free wakes for O(N) exterior aerodynamics SCREENING (the ornithoid flagship's wide-search stage).

## Scope
- Kernel-independent bbFMM: Chebyshev-interpolation-based (fs-cheb tensor machinery) — works for any smooth kernel without analytic multipole expansions; octree decomposition, M2M/M2L/L2L via interpolation operators, SIMD-batched near-field.
- Laplace BEM: collocation/Galerkin panels on fs-rep-mesh surfaces; single/double-layer potentials; solve via GMRES (solver-stack) with FMM-accelerated matvec.
- Aerodynamics layer: KUTTA CONDITION enforcement at trailing edges (circulation determined by smooth-flow-off-edge; ties to fs-feec-cohomology's harmonic circulation story); FREE WAKES: force-free wake sheets shed from trailing edges, convected with the induced flow (FMM-accelerated) — the flapping-gait screening tool (§15.1 step 2: vortex-particle wake shed from trailing edges for FLAPPING gaits pairs with fs-vpm).
- Force/moment extraction (pressure integration), induced-drag decomposition; BEM-level ADJOINTS (the committed gradient path for aero polish while LBM adjoints stay [M] — §15.1 step 5's honest labeling).
- Elastostatic BEM staged later (noted, not promised).

## Acceptance criteria
FMM: O(N log N)-class scaling measured to 10⁷+ points with controllable accuracy (interpolation-order sweep vs direct-sum oracle on subsets); BEM: sphere/ellipsoid potential-flow analytic solutions matched at documented order; airfoil sections: pressure distributions + lift vs thin-airfoil/XFOIL-class references (honesty labels — inviscid screening); wake roll-up qualitative fixtures stable; adjoint gradient gates green.

## Testing
G0: reciprocity/symmetry identities of layer potentials; translation-invariance of FMM (G3); accuracy-vs-order curves ledgered. Logs: octree stats, per-stage timings, accuracy sweeps, circulation values.

title: fs-vpm: vortex particles w/ FMM Biot-Savart, PSE viscosity, remeshing, BEM hybridization for flapping [F]
description:
## What
`fs-vpm`: vortex particle methods (plan §8.3 [F]) — Lagrangian vorticity with FMM-accelerated Biot–Savart, the NATURAL tool for unsteady, wake-dominated flapping flight (§15.1).

## Scope
- Vortex particles: vector-valued vorticity carriers; Biot–Savart velocity evaluation via fs-fmm (the kernel-independent design pays off — same machinery, different kernel); vortex stretching (the 3D term, evaluated with the same FMM pass derivatives).
- Viscous diffusion via PARTICLE STRENGTH EXCHANGE (PSE): conservative kernel-based exchange; core-spreading fallback documented.
- Remeshing/spatial adaptivity: periodic particle redistribution onto regular arrangements (M4'-class interpolation) to control Lagrangian distortion — with conservation audits (circulation/linear/angular impulse).
- HYBRIDIZATION with BEM surface solution (§8.3): BEM provides the boundary condition (no-penetration via panels + Kutta shedding), VPM carries the wake — the flapping-gait screening stack; shedding models at trailing edges with circulation handoff (consistent with BEM's Kutta treatment).
- Divergence-free maintenance: relaxation schemes (vorticity field solenoidality drift control), monitored + ledgered.

## Acceptance criteria
Vortex-ring fixtures: translation speed vs analytic (thin-core), leapfrogging pair vs published; PSE: diffusion of a Lamb–Oseen vortex matches analytic evolution; hybrid BEM+VPM: pitching/plunging airfoil unsteady lift vs Theodorsen/published CFD envelopes (the flapping credential); conservation audits within tolerances over long runs.

## Testing
G0: Biot–Savart symmetry/impulse conservation identities; remeshing conservation exactness (moment-preserving interpolation verified); determinism (particle ordering canonicalized). Logs: impulse/circulation ledgers, remeshing events, hybrid coupling residuals.

title: fs-couple: port-Hamiltonian Dirac interconnection (passive by construction), multirate, Aitken/IQN-ILS
description:
## What
`fs-couple`: multiphysics composition through port-Hamiltonian Dirac structures (plan §8.4 [F]) — partitioned coupling that is PASSIVE BY CONSTRUCTION, structurally excluding added-mass instabilities and energy-drift pathologies of ad-hoc FSI staggering.

## Scope
- Power-conjugate ports: subsystems (structure, fluid, thermal; later EM) expose effort/flow pairs (force/velocity, pressure/flux, temperature/entropy-flow) with Qty-typed conjugacy verified at composition time (effort × flow = power, dimensionally CHECKED).
- Dirac-structure interconnection: composition conserves power EXACTLY at the discrete level; the composition API is literally morphism composition in the category of open port-Hamiltonian systems — "the category theory is not decorative, it is the coupling API's type discipline."
- Partitioned schemes: loosely coupled (multirate co-simulation — fast fluid, slow structure — inheriting stability guarantees from passivity) and strongly coupled with interface acceleration: AITKEN dynamic relaxation and INTERFACE QUASI-NEWTON (IQN-ILS) for the hard FSI cases.
- Energy audit instrumentation: interface power balance ledgered every exchange (the "passive by construction" claim is continuously MEASURED, not assumed — any nonzero interface power generation is a bug alarm).
- Consumers: ornithoid aeroelasticity (BEM/LBM fluid ↔ IGA shell/rod structure), conjugate heat transfer, vessel sloshing-structure if needed.

## Acceptance criteria
Discrete power conservation at interconnections to roundoff-class (the G0 law); classic FSI fixtures: added-mass-instability-prone cases (light structure in dense fluid) run STABLY where naive staggering demonstrably fails (comparison harness included); IQN-ILS convergence acceleration measured vs fixed-point on reference FSI benchmarks; multirate stability under rate ratios up to documented bounds.

## Testing
G0: Dirac-structure algebraic properties (power conservation identity, composition associativity); energy-drift audits over long coupled runs; G4 cancellation mid-exchange leaves consistent state. Logs: interface power ledgers, iteration counts, relaxation factors.

title: DWR goal-oriented adaptivity: one signal driving octree-h, anisotropic-metric, p-enrichment, wavelet thresholding
description:
## What
Goal-oriented adaptivity (plan §8.6 [F]): dual-weighted-residual (DWR) error estimates driving ALL FOUR refinement mechanisms — because in a design-optimization system, "accurate simulation" is not the goal; ACCURATE OBJECTIVE AND GRADIENT is, and DWR is the estimator that knows the difference.

## Scope
- DWR machinery: adjoint solution (fs-adjoint-core, weighted by the ACTUAL objective functional from ASCENT) weights local residuals by their influence on the quantity being optimized; refinement follows the product. Higher-order/enriched adjoint recovery for the weighting (documented options: higher-p solve, patch recovery).
- The four coordinated refinement forms, ONE signal: octree h-refinement (CutFEM/LBM grids), anisotropic METRIC-DRIVEN remeshing (metric tensor field synthesized from directional DWR indicators → fs-mesh-remesh), p-enrichment (high-order FEEC local order increase), and wavelet-style coefficient thresholding on tiles (compression-as-adaptivity for storage-bound fields).
- Marking strategies: Dörfler/fixed-fraction with deterministic tie-breaking (P2); effectivity tracking (estimated vs true error on fixtures where truth is known — the estimator's own honesty metric, ledgered).
- Error Ledger integration: DWR estimates are the discretization-error entries in the composed error budget (fs-plan-models); the planner reallocates budget based on them (Bet 12's loop).
- fs-opdsl generates DWR residual terms per operator (one source of truth includes the estimator).

## Acceptance criteria
Effectivity indices near 1 (within documented bands) on MMS fixtures across frontends; goal-oriented beats uniform refinement by the expected margins on localized-QoI fixtures (drag of a small body in a big domain — the classic demo), measured as accuracy-per-DOF, ledgered; anisotropic metric path produces aligned meshes that beat isotropic at equal DOF; estimator monotonicity under refinement (G3 relation).

## Testing
G0: adjoint-weighting consistency checks; convergence of estimated error at theoretical rates (G1-graded); marking determinism. Logs: effectivity tables, refinement decision maps, accuracy-per-DOF curves.

title: fs-adjoint: IFT discrete adjoints, time-dependent w/ revolve+ledger spill, Hadamard shape gradients, Sobolev smoothing, CI gradient gate
description:
## What
`fs-adjoint`: discrete adjoints and shape derivatives — GRADIENT TRUTH (plan §8.7). The FLUX-side realization of Bet 4 (adjoint-native everything).

## Scope
- Discrete adjoints EVERYWHERE: hand-derived/opdsl-generated transposed operators sharing the primal's matrix-free infrastructure and preconditioners (solver-stack transposed solves); differentiation through solvers via the implicit function theorem (fs-ad-adjoint-infra's contract) — NEVER through Krylov iterations.
- Time-dependent adjoints: reverse sweeps under binomial (revolve) checkpointing with Ledger-backed spill (long-horizon LES/time-history adjoints degrade to storage, not OOM).
- SHAPE GRADIENTS: Hadamard boundary formulas evaluated on FEEC traces (boundary-integral shape derivatives — the mathematically clean form); level-set/density chain rules for CutFEM/SIMP paths (volumetric derivative forms where boundary forms are awkward); coupling to fs-xform Jacobian actions (parameterization chain rule).
- SOBOLEV (H¹) GRADIENT SMOOTHING as the Riesz-representation step — "the single most important practical trick in shape optimization": raw boundary gradients are mesh-noise amplifiers; solving the H¹ Riesz problem turns them into smooth descent directions (Bet 6's right-inner-product doctrine made concrete). Smoothing operator configurable (metric choice = preconditioner choice).
- GRADIENT VERIFICATION AS A GAUNTLET GATE: every adjoint checked against forward-mode duals AND complex-step-quality finite differences on randomized problems in CI; A SOLVER WITHOUT A PASSING GRADIENT CHECK CANNOT MERGE (wired via ci-gauntlet-pipeline).
- Honesty note carried in model cards: free-surface LBM adjoints deferred [M]; gradient-based fluid shape steps use continuous-adjoint NS; LBM under gradient-free/surrogate-gradient optimization.

## Acceptance criteria
Adjoint-vs-dual-vs-FD agreement on the randomized battery for every registered operator/solver (tolerance model documented per conditioning); shape-gradient verification: Hadamard formula vs perturbation-resolve on fixtures (sphere volume/compliance classics); Sobolev smoothing demonstrably rescues a mesh-noisy gradient (fixture with visible improvement, ledgered); revolve spill correctness (bit-equal gradients with/without spill).

## Testing
G0: ⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ suite; G3 adjoint-vs-FD along random directions; end-to-end: one CutFEM shape-gradient descent run reduces the objective monotonically on a fixture. Logs: gradient-check tables per operator, smoothing effects, checkpoint schedules.

title: fs-uq: KL random fields, PCE/QMC/MLMC propagation, Kanai-Tajimi + CQC + IDA fragility, e-process stopping
description:
## What
`fs-uq`: uncertainty quantification (plan §8.8) — random fields, spectral + sampling propagation, multilevel Monte Carlo, and seismic-specific machinery, all under anytime-valid stopping.

## Scope
- Random inputs as KARHUNEN–LOÈVE expansions of random fields (material scatter, geometric tolerance, gust spectra): covariance-operator eigendecomposition (fs-la-eigen), truncation with captured-variance evidence.
- Propagation: POLYNOMIAL CHAOS (PCE) for smooth low-dimensional cases (sparse grids/regression construction); QMC (fs-rand-qmc) as the general workhorse (1–2 orders over MC at these dimensionalities); MULTILEVEL MONTE CARLO coupling coarse/fine discretizations for order-of-magnitude variance-per-cost wins [F] — level design tied to solver ladders, telescoping-sum correctness audited.
- Seismic specifics (the frame flagship's stack): stochastic ground motion via fs-scenario's Kanai–Tajimi-class spectra + recorded suites; RESPONSE-SPECTRUM CQC fast path (modal combination for the quick screen); nonlinear time-history slow path (fs-solid-advanced fiber frames); FRAGILITY via incremental dynamic analysis (IDA) — P(exceedance) curves with confidence machinery.
- ANYTIME-VALID STOPPING inherited from fs-eproc (Bet 5): every stochastic estimate runs under e-process confidence sequences — sample until the CS is tight enough FOR THE DECISION AT HAND, then stop, validly, automatically (the §15.2 step-4 mechanic: "the study stops itself the moment the fragility estimate is decision-grade").
- Risk functionals: CVaR/quantile estimation feeding ASCENT's robust formulations (Rockafellar–Uryasev consumption).

## Acceptance criteria
KL: eigenpair accuracy + variance-capture bookkeeping on analytic covariances (Matérn family); PCE: exponential convergence on smooth fixtures; MLMC: variance-decay telescoping verified, cost model matches theory (documented per-level accounting); CQC vs time-history consistency on linear fixtures; IDA fragility curves reproduce published fixture studies within envelopes; e-stopped estimates hit nominal coverage empirically (with certify-certifiers' adversarial-stopping trials).

## Testing
G0: estimator unbiasedness/telescoping identities; QMC-vs-MC convergence-slope comparisons ledgered; determinism from seeds (G5). Logs: per-level MLMC tables, CS widths over time, stopping decisions with e-values, fragility curves with bands.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: fs-uq note: fs-eproc closed — GaussianMixtureCs gives you anytime-valid stopping for MC estimators today (sub-Gaussian case); empirical-Bernstein bounded variant recorded as eproc follow-up scope if you need it for bounded QoIs.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: fs-uq: QMC landed — Sobol::scrambled + random access for your propagation workhorse; NOTE dims<=10 v1 (KL truncations beyond that need the Joe-Kuo table import — flag it early if you need it and it becomes a quick data task).

title: FLUX conformance: G1 matrix, G2 canonical benchmarks, cross-solver (NS vs LBM) agreement, adjoint gate sweep
description:
## What
The FLUX conformance suite: G1 MMS batteries + G2 canonical benchmarks + adjoint gates wired across every physics frontend — the layer-wide definition of "the physics is right."

## Scope
- G1 coverage matrix EXECUTED: every PDE frontend (body-fitted FEEC, CutFEM, IGA) × element family × BC type with slope-gated order verification, INCLUDING adjoint orders; coverage gaps lintable (the declared-matrix discipline from gauntlet-g1).
- G2 canonical set wired to envelopes: Cook's membrane (fs-solid), cavity Re1000/Ghia + TGV + cylinder Cd–Strouhal Re100 (fs-ns-feec AND fs-lbm — two solvers, one truth), NAFEMS shells + locking (fs-iga/fs-solid), plate buckling (fs-solid-stability), NACA 0012 polars with LES honesty labels (fs-lbm/fs-ns), El Centro-class RC frame time history vs published fiber-model results (fs-solid-advanced + fs-time + fs-scenario).
- Cross-solver consistency: cavity/TGV/cylinder solved by BOTH NS-FEEC and LBM must agree within composed model+discretization evidence (a uniquely FrankenSim audit — two independent discretizations cross-validating, with disagreement decomposition via evidence machinery).
- Conservation/structure audits: energy balance in coupled runs (fs-couple power ledgers), mass in free-surface (fs-lbm-extensions), symplectic drift bounds (fs-time) — the G0 conservation laws at integration scale.
- Adjoint gate sweep: all FLUX solvers through the gradient-check battery.
- Regime-consistency: every benchmark carries its RegimeReport; solver-validity predicates verified against the benchmark set (fs-regime's similarity engine calibrated here).

## Acceptance criteria
Full matrix green on both ISAs nightly; envelopes versioned with provenance; cross-solver agreements within stated bounds; every failure mode has a seeded self-test proving detection.

## Testing
This IS the test bead. Logging: per-case structured records (benchmark id, discretization ladder, QoIs vs envelopes, orders fitted, adjoint checks, regime report hash) — replayable via golden ledgers.

title: fs-regime: Buckingham-Pi groups, nondimensionalization, solver-validity predicates, HELM admission gating
description:
## What
`fs-regime` (patch Rev A): the physics-regime and nondimensionalization kernel — the formal layer answering "WHICH SOLVER IS EVEN VALID for this physical situation?" before FLUX runs. Without it, an agent can run a beautiful, fast, WRONG simulation. This multiplies the value of every other kernel.

## Scope
- Dimensional reduction / Buckingham-Pi machinery: from a problem's Qty-typed inputs, compute the governing dimensionless groups automatically (Reynolds, Mach, Weber, Capillary, Bond, Ohnesorge, Deborah, Froude, Cauchy, Strouhal, reduced frequency, aeroelastic mass ratios; structural: slenderness, damping ratio, P-Delta sensitivity indices).
- Canonical nondimensionalization of PDEs and objectives: recommended scaling maps (length/velocity/time/pressure scales) improving CONDITIONING (scale-aware transforms exposed to solvers); coherence with the LBM lattice-scaling assistant (dx/dt/tau/Mach bookkeeping — fs-lbm consumes regime scales).
- `RegimeReport { groups, dominant_balance, valid_models, invalid_models (with reasons), recommended_scaling, conditioning_risk, ledger_receipt }` returned as `Evidence<RegimeReport>`.
- Solver-validity predicates: each FLUX solver/model registers its validity domain in dimensionless-group space (model cards); `regime.allows(flux.free-surface-lbm)` becomes a checkable, ledgerable assertion. HELM ADMISSION INTEGRATION: fs-ir-admission rejects or warns on solver choices violating the report (the enforcement point).
- Physical-similarity engine: match a problem to known regimes/benchmarks (nearest G2 cases — "your Re=98 cylinder is near the Re=100 benchmark; expected Cd ≈ 1.3"); sanity thresholds with warning/error grading.
- Flagship examples wired as fixtures: spout (Re/We/Ca/Oh/Bond across the tilt schedule), ornithoid (Re/Ma/St/reduced frequency per gait), frame (slenderness/ductility/modal participation).

## Acceptance criteria
Pi-group extraction correct on textbook batteries (algorithmically verified rank/nullspace of dimension matrix); validity predicates catch seeded misuse (creeping-flow solver at Re=10⁴ → structured refusal listing valid alternatives); scaling recommendations demonstrably improve conditioning on fixture systems (condition-number before/after, ledgered); reports reproducible + ledgered.

## Testing
G0: dimensional-consistency laws (groups are dimensionless BY CONSTRUCTION — Qty-typed verification); G3 unit-rescaling invariance of all groups (the definitive test); fixture regime tables vs hand calculations. Logs: full RegimeReports, refusal diagnostics with ranked alternatives.

title: fs-opdsl: typed operator IR — one definition generates residual/JVP/VJP/adjoint/DWR/preconditioner-hints/MMS/tile-kernels
description:
## What
`fs-opdsl` (patch Rev C): the typed mathematical OPERATOR IR and compiler — physics operators represented symbolically (forms, cochains, traces, exterior derivatives, Hodge stars, constitutive maps, boundary operators, residuals, objectives) and LOWERED so that primal, adjoint, JVP, VJP, error estimators, and test hooks come from ONE SOURCE OF TRUTH.

## Why (the patch calls this the single most important implementation addition)
The plan proposes a huge collection of hand-built kernels; maintaining primal/adjoint/estimator CONSISTENCY across the stack is the core scaling problem. FrankenSim should not merely contain kernels — it should contain a kernel-generating algebra. This prevents "the primal changed but the adjoint didn't" rot, gives the autotuner kernel metadata before execution, lets the Gauntlet test generated adjoints against generated JVPs mechanically, and shrinks each agent's implementation target.

## Scope
- Typed operator AST: differential-form-aware (degree/kind checked), Qty-dimension-checked, referencing FEEC building blocks (d, star, wedge, traces), constitutive maps (fs-material laws as opaque-but-differentiable nodes), boundary terms (fs-scenario BCs).
- Generation targets from one definition: residual apply; Jacobian-vector product; vector-Jacobian product / DISCRETE ADJOINT; DWR local residual terms (dwr-adaptivity's food); block-preconditioner structure hints (which blocks exist, their coupling strengths); matrix-free tile kernels (lowering INTO fs-tilelang); MMS manufactured-solution hooks (forcing-term generation feeding gauntlet-g1); dimensional/unit checks.
- Symbolic layer: differentiation of forms/functionals (exterior calculus rules + chain rule through constitutive nodes), simplification, common-subexpression sharing.
- Escape hatch policy: hand-written operators remain ALLOWED (some physics won't fit) but must hand-supply the same artifact set (adjoint, JVP, MMS hook) and pass the same generated-vs-hand consistency gates; the lint makes hand-written status visible.
- Seed already proven in vertical-skeleton (PV); this bead is the real system.

## Acceptance criteria
Poisson/elasticity/convection-diffusion defined in opdsl generate: primal matching hand FEEC implementations to roundoff; adjoints passing ⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ bitwise-class checks; JVP-vs-VJP transpose consistency verified mechanically; MMS hooks feeding G1 with correct observed orders; generated tile kernels within stated performance factor of hand kernels (measured, ledgered — target ≥80% initially, documented).

## Testing
G0: generation determinism; algebraic-identity preservation (d∘d=0 respected by generated code); differentiation correctness vs Dual<N> through generated JVPs. Golden generated-code snapshots with semantic-change justification policy. Logs: generation reports (what was derived from what), consistency-gate tables, perf comparisons.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Note for fs-opdsl (from fs-ad close): the Real trait (fs-ad) is now the generic-scalar contract — opdsl-generated kernels should emit Real-generic code so dual stress-testing is automatic. Also recorded: fs-vskeleton wants a Real-generic refactor when opdsl lands (its acceptance test).

title: fs-feec complexes: oriented incidence d (dd=0 bitwise), cochains, Whitney forms, Hodge stars
description:
## What
`fs-feec` complexes: the exterior-calculus core (plan §8.1, Bet 3) — cell complexes, cochain storage, the exact combinatorial d, and discrete Hodge stars. Fields are cochains; ALL metric information and ALL approximation lives in the stars; d is exact.

## Scope
- Cell-complex construction from fs-rep-mesh tet/hex complexes: oriented incidence structure (vertices/edges/faces/cells) with incidence MATRICES as the exterior derivative (d⁰, d¹, d²) — purely combinatorial, integer-valued, and satisfying dd=0 BITWISE (the G0 law; orientation bookkeeping correctness inherited from fs-rep-mesh is what makes this possible).
- Cochain storage: tile-major layouts (SoA per degree), Qty-dimensioned values, FrankenNumpy zero-copy views.
- Whitney forms (lowest order): P₁Λᵏ basis, interpolation/de-Rham maps (point evaluation ↔ cochain), mass/stiffness assembly via fs-la-batched element kernels with deterministic tiled assembly (fs-sparse COO path).
- Discrete Hodge stars: diagonal (barycentric/circumcentric) and Galerkin (mass-matrix) variants with documented accuracy/monotonicity tradeoffs.
- The exactness payoff (why this is the spine, not a garnish): grad→curl→div identities hold to machine precision, killing spurious pressure modes, spurious EM modes, and checkerboarding BY CONSTRUCTION rather than stabilization folklore; one assembly/apply machinery serves elasticity, flow, heat (and later EM) — "generic, maximally reusable mechanics" is satisfied by choosing the formalism where those physics ARE already the same code.

## Acceptance criteria
dd=0 bitwise on the full fixture zoo; de-Rham complex exactness dimensions verified (rank-nullity bookkeeping matches Betti numbers from validity-certificates on fixtures); Whitney interpolation converges at theoretical order (G1); assembly determinism across thread counts (G5).

## Testing
G0: dd=0, Stokes-theorem discrete identities (⟨dα,β⟩ vs ⟨α,δβ⟩ adjointness through stars); patch tests (constant/linear fields reproduced exactly). G1 MMS for Poisson in mixed and primal forms. Logs: incidence audits, assembly timings, exactness verdicts.

title: fs-feec high-order: PrLk families, tensor-product elements, sum-factorized matrix-free apply (>=30% peak)
description:
## What
`fs-feec` high-order families + sum-factorized matrix-free apply (plan §8.1): hierarchical PrΛᵏ/Pr⁻Λᵏ on simplices, tensor-product families on cubes/octree cells, and the P6-honest apply path.

## Scope
- High-order FEEC element families: hierarchical bases for the full de Rham sequence (H¹/H(curl)/H(div)/L² — the Nédélec/Raviart–Thomas family tree in FEEC language) on simplices; tensor-product families on hexes/octree cells (CutFEM's background elements).
- SUM-FACTORIZED matrix-free operator apply for tensor-product elements: O(p⁴)→O(p³·something) per-element cost via dimension-by-dimension contraction (fs-la-batched small-GEMM chains); roofline target ≥30% of peak FLOPs at p=4 (§14.1, ledgered).
- Matrix-free doctrine (P6): never assemble what we can apply — assembled matrices only where coarse solvers demand them (solver-stack's AMG coarse level).
- Static condensation / p-hierarchy hooks for p-multigrid (solver-stack's levels).
- fs-opdsl integration: generated operators lower onto this apply machinery; hand-written applies must match generated reference.
- Orientation handling for high-order DOFs on shared faces/edges (the classic high-order FEEC bug farm — deterministic global orientation convention, exhaustively tested).

## Acceptance criteria
G1 MMS convergence at orders r=1..6 for all four space types (slope gate); sum-factorized apply matches assembled-matrix apply to roundoff; performance target met on both ISAs; orientation battery (randomly permuted mesh labelings → identical operators, G3-class) green.

## Testing
G0: commuting-diagram properties (interpolation commutes with d); exact-sequence dimensions at high order. Perf lane: apply throughput vs p sweep. Logs: per-order convergence tables, apply GFLOP/s, orientation audit results.

title: fs-feec cohomology: harmonic cochains, Hodge decomposition, multiply-connected correctness (circulation->lift)
description:
## What
`fs-feec` cohomology: harmonic cochains and Hodge decomposition (plan §8.1) — the CORRECT treatment of multiply-connected domains, where naive FEM silently produces the wrong answer or an underdetermined system.

## Scope
- Harmonic-cochain computation: kernel of the Hodge Laplacian per degree (fs-la-eigen LOBPCG with solver-stack preconditioning; dimension = Betti number — cross-checked against validity-certificates' persistent homology, a beautiful internal consistency check between geometry and physics layers).
- Discrete Hodge decomposition: any cochain → exact + coexact + harmonic components (projection solves via solver-stack) — ALSO consumed by fs-sheaf-repair (same machinery, sheaf Laplacian variant).
- The physical payoffs wired as first-class: CIRCULATION AROUND A WING → LIFT (the harmonic 1-cochain on the multiply-connected exterior carries the Kutta-condition circulation; fs-bem-fmm and fs-ns-feec both consume this); pressure fields in domains with handles; cohomology-aware solver setup (augmenting saddle-point systems with harmonic representatives so they're well-posed).
- Periodic/multiply-connected fixture support in the complex builder (torus, annulus, exterior domains with cutouts).

## Acceptance criteria
Harmonic dimensions match Betti numbers on the fixture zoo (torus: b₁=2, etc.); decomposition components orthogonal (to solver tolerance) and re-sum exactly; wing-circulation fixture: computed lift via harmonic circulation matches analytic thin-airfoil reference within discretization error (G1-graded).

## Testing
G0: projection idempotence, orthogonality, dd=0-implied containments (exact ⊥ harmonic etc.); refinement stability of harmonic representatives. Logs: Betti cross-check tables, decomposition residuals, circulation values vs reference.

title: fs-cutfem: CutFEM-on-SDF — certified cut cells, ghost penalty, aggregation fallback, Nitsche BCs, zero meshing
description:
## What
`fs-cutfem`: CutFEM on SDFs (plan §8.1 frontend 2 [F]) — THE MARQUEE BRIDGE: level-set/SDF geometry simulated with FEM-grade accuracy and ZERO MESHING, which is what makes topology-optimization loops run at interactive cadence (Bet 3's biggest single payoff).

## Scope
- Octree background grids ALIGNED WITH FrankenVDB tiles (shared spatial substrate with fs-rep-sdf — one tile geometry, three consumers: SDF storage, LBM lattices, CutFEM cells): non-uniform resolution comes FREE from the octree; hanging-node constraints handled in the element spaces.
- Cut-cell machinery at the zero level set: cut classification (inside/outside/cut per cell from SDF interval bounds — certified classification via fs-ivl, no misclassified cells), CUT QUADRATURE (accurate integration on implicitly-defined subdomains — moment-fitting/tessellation-based with error control), interface quadrature for boundary terms.
- GHOST-PENALTY stabilization: the small-cut robustness fix (jump penalties on ghost faces near the interface restoring conditioning independent of how the interface cuts cells — without it, arbitrarily small cuts blow up the condition number and Newton/Krylov die).
- Aggregated-element fallback: cell agglomeration for pathological cuts (belt + suspenders with ghost penalty; policy documented).
- Weak boundary conditions on embedded interfaces: Nitsche's method with parameter selection tied to the certified cut geometry (fs-scenario BCs applied on level sets).
- Adaptivity integration: octree h-refinement driven by dwr-adaptivity's estimates.

## Acceptance criteria
G1 MMS: optimal convergence orders maintained ACROSS RANDOMIZED CUT CONFIGURATIONS (the CutFEM-specific battery — slivers deliberately included; conditioning bounded verified via eigenvalue estimates); embedded-BC accuracy matches body-fitted references on shared fixtures; a moving-interface sequence (level-set evolution) runs without re-meshing with stable accuracy (the topology-optimization rehearsal).

## Testing
G0: cut-classification certification (interval-verified, zero misclassification on adversarial tangent/near-tangent interfaces); quadrature exactness on polynomial fixtures over cut cells; ghost-penalty parameter sweeps showing conditioning independence of cut fraction (documented curves). Logs: cut statistics, condition estimates, per-configuration convergence tables.

title: fs-iga: Galerkin on splines w/ Bezier extraction, mortar multi-patch, Kirchhoff-Love shells (NAFEMS-gated)
description:
## What
`fs-iga`: isogeometric analysis (plan §8.1 frontend 3 [S/F]) — Galerkin directly on NURBS/B-spline spaces. Geometry basis = analysis basis: the CAD→mesh information massacre simply never happens.

## Scope
- Spline function spaces as FEEC-compatible elements: B-spline/NURBS bases with k-refinement (the IGA superpower: smooth high-order bases with few DOFs), Bézier extraction for element-loop compatibility with fs-feec assembly/apply machinery (batched small dense again).
- Multi-patch coupling via MORTAR METHODS: weak continuity across non-conforming patch interfaces (the sheaf-certificate machinery audits the resulting interface consistency — two layers, one seam story).
- KIRCHHOFF–LOVE SHELLS on spline surfaces: rotation-free thin-shell formulation exploiting C¹ continuity (impossible on C⁰ FEM without tricks) — THE RIGHT TOOL for thin aero skins (ornithoid wing surfaces are IGA shells in flagship step 1) and vessel walls; membrane/bending split, geometrically nonlinear (large rotation) variant.
- Quadrature: reduced/weighted rules for spline spaces (cost control at high continuity).
- fs-scenario BC application on patch boundaries/edges with the same typed discipline.

## Acceptance criteria
G1 MMS at orders p=2..5 including k-refined spaces (slope gate); NAFEMS shell obstacle course + locking tests green (G2 — the named acceptance envelope set); mortar-coupled multi-patch fixtures converge at theory rates with interface consistency certified; large-rotation shell fixtures (cantilever roll-up) match analytic/published curves.

## Testing
G0: partition of unity, patch tests (exact constant-strain reproduction), Bézier-extraction equivalence (spline-space vs extracted-element evaluation identical). Shell battery with published references. Logs: per-order convergence, locking indicators, mortar interface residuals.

title: Foundations: workspace, constellation, CI, contracts, safety, vertical skeleton
description:
## Mission
Cross-cutting project foundations for FrankenSim: the Cargo workspace, the Franken-constellation dependency policy, CI on both target ISAs, the per-crate CONTRACT.md + conformance-suite discipline, unsafe-capsule safety cases, compile-time dimensional analysis, and the PV "vertical skeleton" proving slice.

## Why this epic exists
FrankenSim is sized for a swarm of AI coding agents with human architectural review (plan §16.2). The load-bearing practices that make that safe are all here: **one crate = one contract** (CONTRACT.md + executable conformance suite); **IR as the integration language** (agents integrate against frozen semantics, never each other's internals); **golden ledgers** (every merged feature lands with a replayable acceptance run); **the Decalogue as tie-breaker** (disputes resolve by principle number). The repo is organized so the maximum context an agent needs is one crate + its contracts + the IR spec.

## The Decalogue (governs every child bead)
P1 pure memory-safe Rust, Franken-only deps · P2 determinism as a contract · P3 everything differentiable or certifiable · P4 budgets first · P5 structure preservation · P6 matrix-free & roofline-honest · P7 cancellation-correct compute · P8 one data model (complexes/cochains) · P9 provenance-complete · P10 agent-first ergonomics with the Five Explicits (units, seeds, budgets, versions, capabilities).

## Members
workspace-scaffold, constellation-integration, qty-units, ci-gauntlet-pipeline, contract-conformance-infra, unsafe-safety-cases, vertical-skeleton.

## Success Criteria
Workspace + CI + contract discipline exist and are enforced mechanically; PV vertical skeleton passes end-to-end; every later epic can rely on these rails without re-deriving them.

title: Workspace scaffold: flat fs-* crates, layer enforcement, lint/format rails
description:
## What
Create the FrankenSim Cargo workspace: flat `fs-*` crate layout with a strict acyclic dependency order, Rust 2024 edition, and the mechanical rails every other bead assumes.

## Scope
- Workspace `Cargo.toml` + initial crate skeletons for the L0/L1 spine (fs-substrate, fs-simd, fs-alloc, fs-exec, fs-la, fs-sparse, fs-fft, fs-ivl, fs-rand) with placeholder lib.rs, CONTRACT.md stubs, and test dirs. Other crates added by their own beads.
- Layer-dependency enforcement: a CI-runnable check (cargo-deny-style config or a small in-repo tool) that fails if any crate depends on a higher layer (L0 SUBSTRATE ← L1 BEDROCK ← L2 MORPH ← L3 FLUX ← L4 ASCENT / L5 LUMEN ← L6 HELM; lower layers must NEVER know about higher ones — plan §4, AGENTS.md dependency direction).
- `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` default at workspace level; per-crate opt-out only for registered unsafe capsules (see unsafe-safety-cases bead).
- Feature-flag convention: `[F]` frontier features behind `frontier-*` flags, `[M]` moonshots behind `moonshot-*` flags, DEFAULT OFF. Nothing [M] gates anything [S].
- Lint baseline: clippy -D warnings, rustfmt config, doc requirements for public items.
- CODEOWNERS-style conventions doc: one crate = one contract; agents integrate against contracts and IR, never each other's internals; the Decalogue is the tie-breaker in disputes (resolve by principle number).

## Why
The plan (§16.2) sizes FrankenSim for an agent swarm; the discipline that keeps a 45-crate system from rotting is that each crate ships a CONTRACT.md stating invariants, a conformance suite any reimplementation must pass, and zero access to layers above it. That must be mechanical from day one — retrofitting layering is how archipelagos form.

## Acceptance criteria
- `cargo check --all-targets`, clippy, fmt green on empty skeletons.
- Layer-violation check demonstrably fails on a seeded violation (test the enforcer itself).
- A template crate (`fs-template` or docs) shows: CONTRACT.md skeleton (purpose/layer, invariants, error model, determinism class, cancellation behavior, unsafe boundary, feature flags, conformance tests, no-claim boundaries), unit-test layout, conformance-test layout.

## Testing
Unit tests for the layering checker (positive + seeded-violation negative cases). CI dry-run proving the whole lane runs on both a macOS/aarch64 and Linux/x86-64 runner. Log every check with crate name, layer, verdict.
close_reason: Implemented and verified. Workspace: resolver-3, edition 2024, pinned nightly toolchain, 12 crate skeletons (fs-qty/fs-obs UTIL + P0 spine L0/L1) each with Cargo.toml layer metadata, lib.rs, CONTRACT.md (all 10 required sections), tests/conformance.rs. Policy enforcement: xtask check-layers/check-deps/check-contracts with JSON-lines verdicts; L4/L5 sibling rule encoded; Franken-only dependency gate; dev-dep exemptions reported visibly. Verified: cargo fmt/clippy(-D warnings)/test green (32 tests), live seeded L0->L1 violation caught with exit 1 + correct diagnostic, revert restores green; unit tests cover parser, layer matrix, policy gate, TOOL non-dependability. docs/CONVENTIONS.md (Decalogue-as-policy, unsafe capsule rules, panic policy, manifest conventions) + docs/CONTRACT_TEMPLATE.md. NOTE: both-ISA CI matrix lands with ci-gauntlet-pipeline bead; all checks verified locally on aarch64-apple only so far.

title: MILESTONE P1: geometry + eyes — certified round-trips, watertight-vs-oracle, target ray rates
description:
## Phase P1 — Geometry + eyes (weeks ~6-14)
GATE (plan §16.1): fs-geom, SDF/F-rep/mesh charts, Rep Router v1, dual contouring, Delaunay tet, LUMEN preview tracer.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- Chart round-trips CERTIFIED (conversion functor laws within composed bounds — morph-conformance G0/G3 suites green).
- Watertightness certificate validated against the ray-parity oracle on the adversarial seam zoo.
- Sphere-traced turntable of an F-rep at target ray rates (≥80 Mray/s M-class / ≥120 TR-class primary rays) with the no-tunneling certificate holding.

## Notes
Geometry hardens while physics starts (phases overlap deliberately). The fixture zoo curated here becomes the shared geometric test vocabulary for all later phases.

title: MILESTONE P2: elasticity + first optimization — marquee no-mesh topopt demo w/ certificate, G1 + adjoint gates green
description:
## Phase P2 — Elasticity + first optimization (weeks ~14-24)
GATE (plan §16.1): FEEC elasticity, CutFEM-on-SDF, matrix-free p-MG + AMG, fs-ad adjoints, SIMP + Helmholtz filter — plus the patch's P2-era additions (fs-scenario, fs-material, fs-regime, fs-opdsl cores) that the physics beds on.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- MARQUEE DEMO: topology optimization on a raw SDF — no mesh in the loop — with a composed error certificate (flagship-topopt-demo green, replayable golden ledger in CI).
- G1 convergence orders verified (slope gate ≤0.2 deviation) for the elasticity battery across frontends.
- Adjoint FD-checks green (the gradient merge gate live and enforced).

## Strategic weight
This is the phase where the continuum thesis pays out visibly: MORPH∘FLUX∘ASCENT∘HELM composing with certificates riding inside the values.

title: MILESTONE P3: fluids I — LBM G2 envelopes green, GLUP/s targets, first spout pour end-to-end
description:
## Phase P3 — Fluids I (weeks ~24-34)
GATE (plan §16.1): fs-lbm (cumulant, sparse, free-surface), lattice-scaling assistant, thermal, non-Newtonian; FEEC NS online for cross-validation.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- Cavity Re1000 (Ghia) / Taylor-Green / cylinder Cd-Strouhal Re100 G2 envelopes green.
- ≥ targets on GLUP/s (≥1.0 M-class / ≥0.6 TR-class per §14.1, ledgered with fingerprints — machine-adaptive tuning pass complete for the fluid kernels).
- FIRST SPOUT POURS END-TO-END (free-surface LBM pour across a tilt schedule, mass-conservation audit green — the vessel flagship's physics stage standing up).

title: MILESTONE P4: structures at scale — frame flagship v1 w/ anytime-valid fragility, NAFEMS green
description:
## Phase P4 — Structures at scale (weeks ~34-44)
GATE (plan §16.1): IGA + Kirchhoff-Love shells, fiber beams, ground-structure PDHG, Kanai-Tajimi + MLMC + e-stop.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- FRAME FLAGSHIP v1: fragility with ANYTIME-VALID stopping — the study stops itself when the estimate is decision-grade, validity verified empirically (adversarial-stopping trials for the e-machinery).
- NAFEMS shell suite green (G2 envelopes).
- Fiber-beam batched hot loop at target throughput (the time-history-volume mitigation proven).

title: MILESTONE P5: aero stack — ornithoid flagship v1 with live e-raced generations
description:
## Phase P5 — Aero stack (weeks ~44-56)
GATE (plan §16.1): BEM+FMM+Kutta, vortex particles, Dirac coupling, SE(3) integrators, Koopman surrogates.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- ORNITHOPTER FLAGSHIP v1: screened → refined Pareto with E-RACED GENERATIONS LIVE (dominated candidates cancelled mid-solve, cores reclaimed within budget, tournaments bit-reproducible from seed).
- e-racing payoff measured against the 2-5x generations/core-hour claim (honestly reported either way).
- Surrogate certify-or-escalate operating with verified conformal coverage.

title: MILESTONE P6: certificates & self-optimization — [M] features pass certifier trials or ship flagged-off; planner beats hand allocation
description:
## Phase P6 — Certificates & self-optimization (weeks ~56-68)
GATE (plan §16.1): SOS/Lasserre SDP, sheaf certificates promoted (+ repair), conformal e-prediction hardened, §11.4 planner, diff-rendering.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- All [M] features pass §13.2 (certifying-the-certifiers trials) OR ship flagged-off with honest no-claim boundaries — the [M] discipline's final accounting.
- The planner BEATS hand-allocated budgets on all three flagships (accuracy-per-second, measured, ledgered) — else v2 stays flagged and v1 greedy ships.
- Flagship e2e suite fully staged (smoke/mid/full golden ledgers) on both ISAs.

## Coda discipline
Nothing [M] gates anything [S]; the solid spine ships regardless (§17). Scope creep must displace something or wait.

title: Observability standard: one structured event schema for kernels/tests/ledger, tracing on scope trees, log-replay lint
description:
## What
The unified observability and logging standard: ONE structured-event schema that every kernel, solver, test suite, and e2e script emits — the cross-cutting contract behind every other bead's "detailed logging" requirement, and the enforcement of AGENTS.md's output-style rule (core library code never prints casually; observability is structured tracing or ledger events).

## Why this is its own bead
Roughly forty beads specify per-suite logging ("structured JSON records sufficient to reproduce any failure from logs alone"). Without a single owned schema, forty suites invent forty dialects and the promise dies. This bead makes "diagnosable from logs alone" a checkable property instead of a slogan — and it directly serves the two consumers the system is built for: agents parsing outcomes without scraping, and the ledger's events table ingesting the same stream that tests emit (one pipeline, two sinks).

## Scope
- Event schema v1 (versioned, serde-defined, JSON-lines wire format): common envelope (timestamp, session, study/op id, scope path, TileId where applicable, ExecMode, machine fingerprint hash, severity, event kind) + typed payloads per kind (solver_residual, tile_complete, cancellation, budget_delta, gradient_check, conformance_case, benchmark_result, storm_assertion, race_record, degradation_event, import_receipt, certificate_verdict...). Payload kinds are registered like operators — schema-validated in CI, additive evolution rules.
- Tracing integration: in-house structured tracing layer (P1: no external tracing crates in production paths) with spans mirroring asupersync scopes — the scope tree IS the trace tree; latency-lane export to the ledger `events` table; bounded-overhead design (sampling/severity gates for hot paths; overhead budget measured in the roofline harness).
- Test-log convention: every Gauntlet/conformance/e2e suite emits events in this schema; the "replayable from seed + log alone" doctrine gets a mechanical check — a log-replay lint verifying failure records carry seeds, fixture hashes, and case ids.
- Determinism interaction: event CONTENT deterministic in deterministic mode (timestamps segregated into a non-hashed envelope field so log diffs are meaningful across runs).
- Agent ergonomics: `--log-format jsonl|pretty` on every binary; pretty is a rendering of the JSONL, never a separate code path (no drift between what agents and humans see).
- Redaction/size policy: field-level truncation rules with content-hash pointers into the artifact store for large payloads (a 10^8-cell residual field is an artifact reference, not a log line).

## Acceptance criteria
Schema crate + validator land with the envelope and an initial payload registry; the G0 harness and conformance harness emit conformant streams (validated in CI); a seeded malformed event fails the schema gate; log-replay lint demonstrably rejects a failure record missing its seed; overhead measured ≤ documented budget on a hot tile kernel (ledgered).

## Testing
G0: schema round-trips, additive-evolution compatibility checks (old readers on new streams); determinism: hashed-content equality across thread counts in deterministic mode; integration: one storm run's full event stream replayed into a ledger fixture and queried back. Logs: this bead's tests are self-hosting — their logs ARE conformant streams, verified reflexively.

title: Constellation integration: pinned Franken deps, lockfile, contracts, policy gate
description:
## What
Integrate and pin the Franken constellation — the ONLY permitted runtime dependencies besides std: asupersync, FrankenSQLite, FrankenNumpy, FrankenTorch, FrankenScipy, FrankenPandas, FrankenNetworkx (Decalogue P1).

## Scope
- A constellation lockfile pinning exact versions per project; the lock hash becomes part of every ledger op's Five Explicits so `explain()` can always answer "which FrankenTorch produced this gradient" (plan §12).
- Thin adapter modules documenting each contract (division of labor, §4.1/§12):
  - asupersync = execution substrate: structured concurrency scopes, sub-ms latency-to-cancel at tile granularity, cancellation trees deep enough for speculative races over solver hierarchies. FrankenSim returns upstream: model-checking harness, cancellation-latency benchmarks, the two-lane scheduler pattern.
  - FrankenSQLite = the Design Ledger: WAL crash safety under append-heavy logging with concurrent snapshot readers, multi-GiB blobs, predictable mid-sweep checkpoints. We return a punishing OLTP+blob stress suite.
  - FrankenNumpy = zero-copy interchange membrane: every FrankenSim buffer exposes zero-copy strided views; ufuncs run on the throughput lane.
  - FrankenTorch = reverse-mode tape for surrogate training + neural charts; we contribute custom differentiable ops upstream (adjoint-linear-solve, IFT node, conformal calibration layer).
  - FrankenScipy = conformance oracle: where semantics overlap, our kernels must MATCH it in the Gauntlet and beat it on the roofline; disagreements become bug reports for one side or the other.
  - FrankenPandas = results plane (ledger frames, notebooks); FrankenNetworkx = everything graph-shaped (ground structures, mesh partitioning, constraint propagation, max-plus critical paths).
- Dependency policy gate in CI: build fails if any non-constellation runtime dep (BLAS/LAPACK/C/C++/FFI) appears in the production graph. Dev-only oracles must be isolated and documented.

## Acceptance criteria
Lockfile exists and hash is programmatically retrievable; policy gate demonstrably rejects a seeded forbidden dependency; each adapter has a smoke test exercising the real library.

## Testing
Per-adapter smoke tests with structured logs (library version, capability check results). CI negative test for the dependency gate.
close_reason: Implemented and verified. constellation.lock pins all 7 libraries (version + git head; asupersync 0.3.5@86bcbb1, fsqlite crates, fnx/fnp/ft/fsci/fp workspaces) with lock_hash f92683cc4572a198 covering PORTABLE identity only (paths excluded by design + tested); xtask lock-constellation/check-constellation commands (drift = CI failure with teaching message); dependency-policy gate updated to REAL member-crate prefixes (asupersync, fsqlite*, fnx-, fnp-, ft-, fsci-, fp-) with over-acceptance boundary documented + tested; asupersync wired as fs-exec's first real dependency with a smoke test exercising Budget::INFINITE/ZERO/MINIMAL (the exact P4 vocabulary Cx will carry) — builds clean, 89s cold. SCOPE REFINEMENT (recorded): per-library adapter smokes distributed to their consumer crates (fsqlite->fs-ledger, fnx->rep-router/topo-truss, fnp->soa-derive views, ft->fs-ad-adjoint-infra, fsci->bedrock-conformance, fp->fs-report) — the consumer beads' descriptions already name these integrations; a standalone adapter layer would be dead code until then. Five-Explicits hash plumbing lands with fs-ledger as designed. Workspace: 75 tests green, clippy clean, policy OK.
