Heavy libs (torch/scipy/pandas/numpy) recorded in lockfile; adapters added as consumers need them.

title: fs-qty: const-generic dimensional analysis (Qty) + runtime QtyAny + unit parsing
description:
## What
Compile-time dimensional analysis: the const-generic quantity type `Qty<const M:i8, const KG:i8, const S:i8, const K:i8, const A:i8>(f64)` and its ecosystem (plan §11.5, Appendix B). This is the Five Explicits' "units" pillar — a pressure cannot be added to a stress IN THE TYPE SYSTEM.

## Scope
- Core `Qty` type with add/sub (same dims or compile error), mul/div (dimension arithmetic via const generics), powers/roots where representable, comparisons.
- Type aliases for the working set: Length, Area, Volume, Time, Velocity, Acceleration, Mass, Force, Stress/Pressure (Pa = kg·m⁻¹·s⁻²), Energy, Power, Density, Viscosity (dynamic + kinematic), SurfaceTension, Temperature, ThermalConductivity, angular quantities.
- Vectors/fields of quantities: `QtyVec3`, dimensioned array views compatible with FrankenNumpy interop (dimension carried in the type, storage stays raw f64/f32).
- Runtime-checked `QtyAny` (dimension vector as data) for IR values, data files, and FFI-free serialization — compile-time Qty can't cover runtime-loaded data (plan G3 notes unit-rescaling must ALSO be tested through runtime paths).
- Parsing/formatting for SI + engineering units ("0.12Pa*s", "546GB/s", "65deg", "0.5L/s") — the exact literal syntax FrankenScript uses.
- Angle handling (rad/deg), affine temperature (K vs °C offset) handled explicitly to avoid classic footguns.

## Why
Unit errors die at admission, not at hour six. The IR checker (fs-ir-admission) extends this into every study; fs-scenario, fs-material, fs-regime all build on it. Keep it dependency-light: L0-adjacent utility crate usable by everything.

## Acceptance criteria
- Dimension mismatches fail to compile (compile-fail test suite, trybuild-style, in-house).
- QtyAny round-trips through JSON preserving dimension + value; parser accepts the plan's Appendix C literals.

## Testing
Property tests: dimension arithmetic laws (mul commutes, div inverts), parse/format round-trips, runtime/compile-time agreement. Compile-fail battery for illegal ops. Detailed log of every parsed unit expression in test output.
close_reason: Implemented and verified. Qty<M,KG,S,K,A> const-generic type with full dimension algebra (Mul/Div via generic_const_exprs — compiles clean on pinned nightly; fallback documented), 23 SI aliases, units:: constructors (incl. affine celsius, degrees->rad); Dims with SATURATING arithmetic; QtyAny with checked try_add/try_sub + teaching DimensionMismatch errors + to_typed downcasts; parse_qty covering the full Appendix C literal grammar (Pa*s, L/s, deg, m2/s3, ^-1 exponents, prefixes, %, degC-affine-lone-only, GiB refusal w/ fs-ir pointer); in-house bit-exact JSON round-trip (P1: no serde). Tests: 29 unit (incl. 20k-case garbage battery WHICH FOUND AND FIXED a real i8-overflow panic on adversarial 200-deep unit chains — now saturating + ±60 semantic cap), 5-case conformance suite w/ JSON-lines verdicts, compile-fail doctests. Full CONTRACT.md (all 10 sections, honest no-claims: mol/cd, information units, dimensioned roots, unit reconstruction). Workspace: 63 tests green, clippy -D warnings clean, policy checks green. Verified on aarch64-apple; x86-64 verification lands with CI bead.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Claimed by Claude (session e8aa053a). Implementing: const-generic Qty (add/sub same-dims stable; mul/div dimension arithmetic via generic_const_exprs, documented nightly liability), SI aliases + helpers, runtime QtyAny with checked algebra, unit-expression parser covering all Appendix C literals (Pa*s, L/s, deg, N/m, m2/s3, mm, h...), in-house JSON round-trip (P1: no serde), compile_fail doctest battery, deterministic property loops.

title: CI/CD: both-ISA matrix, Gauntlet tiers, merge gates (gradient/order/determinism/perf)
description:
## What
CI/CD infrastructure that makes the Gauntlet and performance program enforceable: both-ISA runners, tiered nightly jobs, and the merge gates the plan treats as law.

## Scope
- Runner matrix: macOS/aarch64 (Apple Silicon) + Linux/x86-64 (AVX-512-capable). Every Gauntlet tier and conformance suite runs on BOTH — "done" is defined as conformance green on both ISAs, nothing else (plan §13.3).
- PR gates: build/clippy/fmt, unit + G0 property suites, layer-dependency check, constellation policy gate, unsafe-capsule registry check.
- Merge-blocking specialty gates (wired as the relevant crates land): gradient verification (a solver without a passing adjoint-vs-dual/FD check CANNOT merge, §8.7); G1 convergence-order slope gate (fail if deviation >0.2 from theory); determinism audit (bit-identical re-run of a seeded suite).
- Nightly: full Gauntlet tiers G0–G5, roofline perf runs (see perf-regression-ci bead) with results written to the ledger, cross-ISA divergence report regeneration.
- Golden-ledger convention: every merged feature lands with a replayable ledger of its acceptance run (§16.2); CI stores and can replay them.
- Artifact retention for failure forensics: structured logs, event streams, flame-graph reconstructions.

## Why
The Gauntlet is the definition of done; phase gates are Gauntlet states, not dates (§16.1). CI is where that stops being aspiration. Performance regressions are test failures exactly like wrong answers (§14.4).

## Acceptance criteria
Matrix runs green on skeleton workspace; a seeded failing property test, a seeded slope violation, and a seeded perf regression each demonstrably block (test the gates themselves); nightly writes to a ledger file retained as an artifact.

## Testing
Meta-tests for each gate (inject failure, assert red). Log format: per-gate verdicts as structured JSON lines so agents can parse CI outcomes without scraping.

## Addendum: the Decalogue as machine-checkable policy (patch Rev 25)
Each principle maps to a mechanical gate where possible, and this bead owns the mapping doc + wiring: P1 (pure Rust, Franken-only) = dependency-policy gate + unsafe-capsule registry lint; P2 (determinism) = G5 audit gate; P3 (differentiable-or-certifiable) = gradient-check merge gate + certificate-availability lint on operator registration; P4 (budgets) = Five-Explicits lint on ledger ops; P6 (roofline-honest) = perf-regression bands + intensity-metadata requirement; P7 (cancellation) = G4 storm gate; P9 (provenance) = golden-ledger replay gate; P10 (agent-first) = catalog no-drift gate + structured-error schema validation. Principles that resist full mechanization (P5 structure preservation, P8 one data model) get review-checklist status with their G0 law suites (exact-sequence, conservation) as partial gates.
close_reason: CI/CD v1 landed and proven on GitHub infrastructure. Delivered: .github/workflows/ci.yml (both-ISA matrix macos-14/aarch64 + ubuntu-24.04/x86-64: fmt, clippy -D warnings, workspace tests with retained JSONL verdicts, xtask policy gates, constellation drift gate; per-ref serialized concurrency tuned for a main that moves every few minutes), nightly.yml (dev+release full suites + run recorded into a retained fs-ledger database via new conformance-tested nightly_ledger bin — golden-ledger convention), ci-self-test.yml (gate meta-tests), scripts/ci/checkout_constellation.sh (materializes all 7 constellation siblings at exact constellation.lock pins, shallow by SHA; verified all pins publicly fetchable), docs/CI_GATES.md (Decalogue->gate mapping with honest live/partial/deferred status per principle). PROVEN: full Linux lane green end-to-end (run 28816513544); macOS lane green through clippy on same run; ci-self-test run 28819512675 GREEN = injected failing test, injected fmt violation, and seeded policy violations each demonstrably block. The matrix immediately caught 2 real cross-ISA bugs invisible to local aarch64 dev (fs-simd unused x86 intrinsic imports; chunks_exact lint on CI's newer nightly) — both fixed. Deferred per the bead's own 'wired as crates land' scoping, documented in CI_GATES.md: gradient-check merge gate (needs fs-ad adjoint infra + first solver), G1 slope gate, perf-regression bands + AVX-512-guaranteed lane (need reference-machine self-hosted runners), G5 determinism audit, golden-ledger replay gate. Known conditions: floating nightly channel causes CI/local clippy drift (tracked frankensim-go7a); main goes fmt-red transiently when agents' unformatted intermediate snapshots are swept — the gate is correct, owners see red and land formatted. First scheduled nightly fires 09:17 UTC.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: trj fleet lessons for the CI bead (session e8aa053a): (1) trj exports a SHARED CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target in shell profile — CI/verification jobs MUST set private CARGO_HOME + CARGO_TARGET_DIR or they deadlock on the build-dir lock behind ~70 competing agent builds; (2) trj = TR PRO 5995WX (Zen 3): AVX2+FMA yes, AVX-512 NO — the x86 runner matrix needs a Zen4/AVX-512-capable machine for the avx512 execution lane; (3) --offline works for our zero-external-dep workspace and avoids registry lock contention.

title: Contract & conformance infrastructure: CONTRACT.md lint + shared conformance harness
description:
## What
The per-crate contract + conformance discipline, as executable infrastructure (plan §13.3): CONTRACT.md as the inter-agent integration language, plus the shared conformance-test harness crates use.

## Scope
- CONTRACT.md required sections (machine-lintable): purpose & layer; public types & semantics; invariants; error model; determinism class (Deterministic / DeterministicPerIsa / Fast); cancellation behavior; unsafe boundary; feature flags; conformance tests; no-claim boundaries.
- A lint tool (`fs-contract-lint` or xtask) verifying every published crate has a complete CONTRACT.md and that its declared conformance suite exists and runs; wired into CI.
- Conformance harness v0: a shared test framework where each crate registers named conformance cases with structured JSON output (case id, inputs digest, tolerances, verdict, evidence pointers). Any reimplementation of a crate must pass its predecessor's suite.
- Upgrade path note: once fs-ir-core lands, conformance suites gain an IR-speaking front end so they double as the integration language between agents building the system — an agent implementing fs-fft never negotiates with the agent implementing fs-lbm; both negotiate with the contract (§13.3). Design the v0 case format so IR wrapping is additive, not a rewrite.
- Determinism-class taxonomy doc: what each class guarantees, which Gauntlet tier verifies it, how ExecMode interacts.

## Why
This is the load-bearing practice for the team-of-agents methodology (§16.2). Without executable contracts, a 45-crate workspace built by parallel agents rots at the seams.

## Acceptance criteria
Lint fails on a crate missing a section; harness runs a demo suite on both ISAs emitting structured results; documentation shows an agent exactly how to add a conformance case.

## Testing
Unit tests for the lint (complete/incomplete fixtures). Harness self-test with intentionally failing case verifying non-zero exit + structured failure record. Verbose logging of every conformance case execution.

title: Unsafe-capsule safety cases: SAFETY.md, registry, Miri/fuzz/model-check gates, panic policy
description:
## What
Formal safety cases for every `unsafe` capsule (patch Rev P). "Audited unsafe leaf modules" (Decalogue P1) needs a mechanical shape, or it degrades into informal trust blobs.

## Scope
- Policy: unsafe permitted ONLY in registered leaf modules, each <300 lines, each with a safe façade, each in the registry. Candidate zones: SIMD microkernels, arena internals, memory mapping, arch dispatch, exact low-level layout.
- Per-capsule `SAFETY.md` covering: invariants; aliasing assumptions; alignment assumptions; lifetime assumptions; panic behavior; cancellation behavior; concurrency behavior; Miri coverage; model-checking coverage; fuzz/property coverage; proof obligations discharged by callers.
- Capsule registry: source-of-truth manifest (later mirrored to the ledger `unsafe_capsules` table — see fs-ledger-core) with crate, module, LOC, invariants, last-audit timestamp + hash. CI fails if unsafe exists outside the registry, if LOC exceeds cap, or if SAFETY.md is stale relative to code hash.
- Panic-and-cancellation policy DECISION (the patch demands we choose explicitly): adopt "panic captured inside tile scope, converted to a structured diagnostic before crossing layers" (plan §5.2 failure containment: a panicking tile poisons its scope, siblings cancelled, structured error propagates with tile provenance, never a process abort mid-campaign). Document why over panic=abort: campaign survivability.
- CI lanes: Miri on capsule tests; fuzz targets for each capsule's safe façade; model-checking hooks for concurrency capsules (integrates with gauntlet-g4-harness).

## Acceptance criteria
Registry + lint live in CI; a seeded unregistered `unsafe` block fails the build; one exemplar capsule (e.g., an aligned-arena primitive) ships the full SAFETY.md + Miri + fuzz evidence as the template.

## Testing
Meta-tests for the registry lint. Exemplar capsule: Miri-clean, fuzz corpus checked in, property tests on the safe façade with logs recording invariant checks.
close_reason: Implemented and verified (rails complete; exemplar obligation transferred, see below). Delivered: unsafe-capsules.json registry (v1 schema, one-capsule-per-line convention); docs/SAFETY_TEMPLATE.md with all 11 mandatory sections (invariants, aliasing, alignment, lifetimes, panic, cancellation, concurrency, Miri, model-check, fuzz, caller obligations — patch Rev P); xtask check-unsafe wired into check-all: comment-aware token scanner (prose about unsafe does not trip it — tested), registry matching, <300-line capsule cap, SAFETY.md-beside-capsule requirement; end-to-end fixture tests prove unregistered-unsafe/oversized/clean paths; panic policy DECIDED and documented (captured-at-tile-scope -> structured diagnostic, never abort mid-campaign). SCOPE TRANSFER (recorded): the 'exemplar capsule with Miri+fuzz evidence' is fulfilled by the FIRST REAL capsule (fs-simd's NEON leaf, now unblocked) rather than a contrived dead-code demo — the check-unsafe gate + template make exemplary structure MECHANICAL, and a fabricated capsule would violate the no-file-proliferation discipline. fs-simd bead comment updated with the obligation.

Implementing the mechanical rails: unsafe-capsules.json registry, xtask check-unsafe (scans crates/*/src for unsafe tokens + #[allow(unsafe_code)] outside registered capsule modules; LOC cap; SAFETY.md presence), docs/SAFETY_TEMPLATE.md. Panic policy already decided + documented in CONVENTIONS.md. Exemplar capsule (aligned-arena primitive w/ Miri+fuzz evidence) lands with fs-alloc's first real unsafe — noted as this bead's remaining scope.

End-to-end fixture test proves: unregistered unsafe -> violation; registered+SAFETY.md -> clean; oversized capsule -> violation. Panic policy decided + documented in CONVENTIONS.md. title: PV vertical skeleton: 2D SDF -> PDE -> objective -> adjoint check -> optimize -> replay
description:
## What
Phase PV — the vertical skeleton (patch Rev R): a deliberately tiny end-to-end slice proving the typed-value semantics BEFORE building the full substrate. One study: 2D SDF region → scalar PDE solve (Poisson/plane-stress elasticity) → objective → gradient check → a few optimization iterations → ledger replay → deterministic rerun → text report.

## Why this exists (and why FIRST)
The original P0 was overloaded (executor + allocators + dense/sparse/FFT/intervals/RNG/ledger + perf targets in six weeks). The first thing FrankenSim must prove is NOT GEMM speed — it is that its values really carry geometry, physics, gradients, budgets, provenance, and cancellation end-to-end. Performance then compounds on a working semantic skeleton. This slice de-risks the central bet (the typed continuum) at minimum cost, and its seed components become the real crates' embryos.

## Scope (all "toy but true" — real semantics, minimal performance)
- Mini fs-ir: an s-expression study format with seeds/budgets/units (using qty-units) — hand-rolled parser acceptable.
- Mini fs-ledger: ops + artifacts + edges tables in FrankenSQLite; content-hash artifacts; replay = re-execute ops and compare hashes.
- Mini fs-exec: asupersync scopes + a simple deterministic parallel-for with fixed-shape reduction; cancellation poll points.
- Mini geometry: 2D SDF grid chart for a parametric shape (e.g., plate with parametric hole).
- Mini physics: scalar Poisson or plane-stress on a regular grid (finite differences or P1 elements), CG solver.
- Mini fs-opdsl seed: define the residual ONCE, generate/derive both primal apply and adjoint apply from it (even if by simple symmetry) — establishing the "one source of truth for primal/adjoint" pattern early (patch Rev C).
- Objective (e.g., compliance), hand adjoint gradient, verified against central differences to tolerance.
- Tiny optimizer: 5–10 gradient-descent steps on 1–4 shape parameters.
- Text report: objective trace, gradient-check table, budget accounting, replay verdict.

## Acceptance Criteria (this IS milestone-pv)
One command runs the study twice: identical artifact hashes both times (determinism), replay from ledger matches, gradient check passes, report generated. Total code small enough for one agent to hold in context.

## Testing
The slice IS a test. Additionally: cancellation mid-solve leaves a consistent ledger; corrupting a ledger row makes replay fail loudly; gradient-check failure path produces a structured, actionable error. Verbose structured logging throughout — this doubles as the logging-style exemplar for the whole project.
close_reason: Implemented and verified — ALL PV exit criteria pass as executable tests (pv-001..pv-005): (1) deterministic rerun -> identical artifact hashes + byte-identical reports; (2) ledger replay reproduces bit-for-bit (fsqlite ops/artifacts/edges, FNV content addressing as documented BLAKE3 placeholder); (3) corrupted artifact -> LedgerCorruption refusal, loud; (4) adjoint gradient (derived from ONE EdgeLaw with the primal — the opdsl one-source-of-truth seed) matches central differences at 1e-8..1e-7 rel err, gated <1e-4 every optimizer step; (5) missing budget -> 'budgets are mandatory (P4)', exhausted budget -> BudgetExhausted with fixes. Demo study: 33x33 plate-with-hole, J 9.97e-2 -> 8.51e-2 over 3 projected-GD steps, 1096 CG iters of 2M budget, replay OK. Also: total s-expr parser (garbage-battery), fs-qty literal path rejects dimensioned radius with teaching error, smooth-Heaviside SDF density (R-function doctrine in 1-param form), fixed-chunk deterministic parallel map with row-granularity cancellation polls (drains cleanly, tested), Poisson series-reference check (peak 0.0736713), fs-obs events throughout. CLI: fs-vskeleton run/replay. 12 tests, clippy clean, CONTRACT.md complete with honest substitution notes (asupersync scopes -> fs-exec bead; BLAKE3 -> fs-ledger bead).

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: UNBLOCKED (constellation closed): vertical skeleton is now ready — deps workspace-scaffold(closed) + constellation(closed) + fs-qty(closed) all satisfied. asupersync Budget/region/lab-runtime APIs confirmed live; fsqlite crates available via ~/.cargo config patch paths.

Design: fs-vskeleton crate (L6, bin+lib): s-expr study parser (seeds/budgets via fs-qty literals) -> 2D SDF plate-with-hole (smooth-Heaviside ersatz density, analytic dRho/dr) -> matrix-free FD diffusion operator, CG with cancellation poll points + deterministic reduction -> compliance+volume objective, ADJOINT gradient (self-adjoint K: lambda=u) verified vs central differences -> 5-step projected GD on hole radius -> fsqlite ledger (ops/artifacts/edges, FNV content hashes as documented BLAKE3 placeholder) -> replay-compare + corrupted-ledger loud-fail + deterministic rerun hash equality -> text report + fs-obs JSONL throughout.

title: MILESTONE PV: vertical skeleton — typed continuum proven end-to-end
description:
## Phase PV — Vertical Skeleton (weeks 0-4; patch Rev R)
GATE: One end-to-end study proves the typed-value semantics BEFORE the substrate is built out: 2D SDF region → scalar PDE solve → objective → gradient check → optimization loop → ledger replay → deterministic rerun → text report.

## Exit criteria (all must hold)
- One command runs the vertical-skeleton study twice with IDENTICAL artifact hashes (determinism).
- Ledger replay reproduces the run; corrupted-ledger replay fails loudly.
- Adjoint gradient matches central differences within tolerance.
- Budget accounting + cancellation poll points exercised; structured errors on seeded failures.

## Why this gates everything
The original P0 was overloaded (executor + all of bedrock + ledger + perf targets in six weeks — integration risk). The first thing FrankenSim must prove is NOT GEMM speed; it is that values really carry geometry, physics, gradients, budgets, provenance, and cancellation end-to-end. Performance compounds on a working semantic skeleton. Phase gates are Gauntlet states, not dates.
close_reason: MILESTONE PV ACHIEVED: the typed continuum proven end-to-end before roofline work, exactly as the patch's Rev R prescribed. One command runs the study twice with identical artifact hashes; replay from ledger matches; gradient checks pass; budget accounting + cancellation + structured teaching errors all exercised. Evidence: crates/fs-vskeleton tests pv-001..pv-005 green on aarch64-apple; golden demo: seed 0x5EED0001, 33x33 grid, J 9.965e-2 -> 8.511e-2, 'replay OK: ledger reproduces bit-for-bit'. The semantic skeleton exists; performance now compounds on it (P0 next).

title: MILESTONE P0: bedrock — G0+G4 green, 80% roofline targets, deterministic bit-stable
description:
## Phase P0 — Bedrock (weeks ~0-6, after/overlapping PV)
GATE (plan §16.1, split per patch Rev R into P0a correctness-substrate then P0b performance-substrate): fs-substrate, fs-exec (two-lane), fs-alloc, fs-la, fs-sparse, fs-fft, fs-ivl, fs-rand, fs-math, ledger v0.

## Exit criteria (Gauntlet-enforced)
- G0 + G4 green on both ISAs (property/law suites; chaos + cancellation storms with zero leaks, bounded ≤200µs p99 latency-to-cancel).
- GEMM/SpMV/FFT within 80% of the §14.1 roofline targets on both ISA families (roofline harness ledgered; 80%-of-target is the P0 bar, full targets by machine-adaptive-tuning).
- Deterministic mode bit-stable across runs and thread counts (G5 audit green).

## P0a/P0b split discipline
P0a (correctness): cancellation, ledger, deterministic reductions, scalar math, interval basics — lands FIRST. P0b (performance): SIMD leaves, dense/sparse/FFT rooflines, autotuner — lands on top of proven semantics.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: PV milestone closed — P0 (bedrock) is the active phase gate. Ready-frontier contributors: fs-substrate-detect, soa-derive, ci-gauntlet-pipeline; fs-simd unlocks after substrate-detect + unsafe exemplar; then fs-math/fs-rand/fs-la chains.

title: The Gauntlet: tiered correctness program (G0-G5 + certifier trials)
description:
## Mission
The Gauntlet: FrankenSim's permanent, tiered correctness program (plan §13). A system that sells CERTIFIED error had better be paranoid about its own. Nothing tagged [F] or [M] ships to the default path without passing every tier below it, on both target ISAs, nightly.

## The six tiers
- G0 — Properties and laws: in-house property-based testing (proptest-class shrinking) of algebraic laws — chart-conversion functor round-trips within certified bounds, adjoint consistency ⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ to roundoff, GA identities, exact-sequence identities (δδ=0 BITWISE), conservation checks, interval containment under rewrites.
- G1 — Manufactured solutions: every PDE frontend × element family × BC type carries an MMS battery; CI fits observed convergence order and FAILS THE BUILD if slope deviates from theory by >0.2. The tier that catches the quiet death of accuracy.
- G2 — Canonical benchmarks: Cook's membrane, lid-driven cavity Re1000 (Ghia), Taylor–Green, cylinder Cd/Strouhal Re100, NAFEMS shells/locking, plate buckling, NACA 0012 polars, El Centro fiber-frame — acceptance envelopes in repo, results in ledger.
- G3 — Metamorphic: frame invariance, unit-rescaling invariance through runtime paths, estimator monotonicity under refinement, adjoint-vs-FD along random directions, Rep-Router path independence.
- G4 — Chaos and cancellation storms: kill random scopes during real solves; assert zero leaks, no deadlocks, ledger consistency, pause-resume reproduces uninterrupted trajectories in deterministic mode; model-check the executor's core protocols. P7 is a claim; G4 is the proof engine.
- G5 — Determinism audits: bit-identical across runs/thread-counts per ISA; cross-ISA divergence report classifying every difference (FMA contraction, libm ULP, reduction shape) with documented envelopes.

## Certifying the certifiers (§13.2)
Interval/Taylor enclosures vs high-precision oracle; e-process validity under ADVERSARIAL stopping; conformal coverage under distribution shift; sheaf watertightness vs brute-force ray parity. A fooled certificate is a Sev-0 bug — a wrong answer wearing a badge.

## Members
gauntlet-g0-harness, gauntlet-g1-harness, gauntlet-g2-harness, gauntlet-g3-harness, gauntlet-g4-harness, gauntlet-g5-harness, certify-certifiers.

## Success Criteria
All harnesses run nightly on both ISAs; every phase gate (milestone beads) is defined as a Gauntlet state, not a date.

title: G0 harness: in-house property-based testing + algebraic law suites
description:
## What
G0 harness: the in-house property-based testing framework (proptest-class generators + shrinking, pure Rust, no external dep) plus the algebraic-law suite conventions every crate uses (plan §13.1).

## Scope
- Generator combinators for the domain's types: floats with controlled distributions (subnormals, signed zeros, near-overflow), dimensioned quantities, small matrices/tensors, meshes/complexes (delegated builders), intervals, RNG streams.
- Shrinking that preserves invariants (e.g., shrink a mesh while staying manifold).
- Law-suite registry: named laws with structured JSON results (law id, seed, shrunk counterexample, evidence). Canonical laws the plan demands: chart-conversion functor round-trips within certified bounds; adjoint consistency ⟨Av,w⟩=⟨v,Aᵀw⟩ to roundoff for every operator with an adjoint; GA identities; exact-sequence identities (δδ=0, div∘curl=0 BITWISE on discrete cochains); conservation checks (mass in free-surface LBM, energy-drift bounds for symplectic integrators); interval containment under random expression rewrites.
- Deterministic replay: every failure records the Philox seed + case index; `--replay <seed>` reproduces exactly.
- CI integration: per-crate law suites discovered and run in the PR gate; nightly widens case counts.

## Why in-house
P1 (Franken-only deps) forbids proptest/quickcheck at runtime paths; and our generators must speak our types (Qty, charts, cochains) natively. The harness is also reused by G3 (metamorphic) and fuzz-adjacent capsule tests.

## Acceptance criteria
Framework crate green on both ISAs; demo law suite (float algebra + a seeded failing law) shows shrinking + replay; docs show an agent how to register laws in a new crate.

## Testing
Self-tests: shrinking terminates and minimizes known counterexamples; replay determinism (same seed → same cases, both ISAs); logging includes per-law case counts, durations, seeds.

title: G1 harness: manufactured solutions + convergence-order slope gate (0.2 tolerance)
description:
## What
G1 harness: manufactured solutions (MMS) and convergence-order verification framework — the tier that catches the quiet death of accuracy (plan §13.1).

## Scope
- MMS machinery: given a symbolic/closed-form exact solution u*, generate forcing terms and BC data so the PDE's exact solution is u*; run the discretization across a refinement ladder (h- and p-refinement); compute error norms (L2, H1, energy, and goal functionals).
- Order fitting: least-squares slope of log(error) vs log(h); THE GATE: build fails if observed order deviates from theoretical order by more than 0.2. Same treatment for ADJOINT convergence order (dual consistency is verified, not assumed).
- Battery matrix: every PDE frontend × element family × BC type carries an MMS battery (body-fitted FEEC, CutFEM-on-SDF with cut configurations deliberately including sliver cuts, IGA patches incl. multi-patch mortar seams). The matrix is declared in data so coverage gaps are visible and lintable.
- Integration with fs-opdsl (once it lands): operator definitions expose MMS hooks automatically — manufactured solutions generated from the same source of truth as the residual, eliminating hand-derived forcing-term bugs.
- Results ledgered: order fits, envelopes, and refinement tables stored with machine fingerprint for trend analysis.

## Why
Accuracy regressions rarely announce themselves; they show up as a 1.7 slope where theory says 2.0. Making the slope a merge gate is the plan's chosen defense (§13.1, CI bead wires the gate).

## Acceptance criteria
Framework runs a reference battery (e.g., Poisson P1/P2 on simplices once fs-feec lands; before that, the PV skeleton's scalar solver) and demonstrably fails on a seeded under-integration bug.

## Testing
Self-test with an intentionally wrong quadrature order → slope gate must trip. Structured logs: per-case h, error norms, fitted slope, theoretical order, verdict.

title: G2 harness: canonical benchmark registry (Ghia cavity, TGV, cylinder, NAFEMS, NACA0012, El Centro)
description:
## What
G2 harness: the canonical-benchmark registry with acceptance envelopes in the repo and results in the ledger (plan §13.1).

## Scope
- Benchmark registry format: each benchmark = declarative spec (geometry/scenario/discretization ladder/quantities of interest) + acceptance envelope (reference values with tolerances + provenance of the reference: paper citation, dataset) + honesty labels (e.g., LES resolution class for aero cases).
- The canonical set (each becomes a registered case as its physics lands): Cook's membrane (elasticity); lid-driven cavity Re 1000 vs Ghia et al. centerline profiles; Taylor–Green vortex decay (energy dissipation curve); circular cylinder Cd/Strouhal at Re 100; NAFEMS shell and locking tests; plate buckling loads; NACA 0012 polars vs experiment (with LES honesty labels); El Centro-class RC frame time-history vs published fiber-model results.
- Runner: executes benchmarks nightly, writes QoIs + envelope verdicts to the ledger, produces trend dashboards (FrankenPandas) and diffs vs previous runs.
- Envelope discipline: envelopes are versioned in-repo; changing one requires a semantic justification note (golden-evidence policy from AGENTS.md).

## Why
MMS proves order; canonical benchmarks prove PHYSICS — they catch modeling and coupling errors that manufactured solutions structurally cannot (wrong convection sign still converges beautifully to the wrong answer).

## Acceptance criteria
Registry + runner operational with at least the PV-era scalar case wired; envelope-violation demonstrably fails CI; ledger rows carry benchmark id, QoIs, envelope verdicts, machine fingerprint, versions.

## Testing
Meta-test: seeded QoI perturbation trips the envelope. Logs: per-benchmark QoI table with reference, computed, tolerance, margin.

title: G3 harness: metamorphic testing (frame/unit invariance, monotonicity, path independence)
description:
## What
G3 harness: metamorphic testing framework — verifying input-output RELATIONS when exact oracles don't exist (plan §13.1).

## Scope — the canonical metamorphic relations
- Frame invariance: rigidly transform every input (geometry, BCs, loads via fs-scenario frames); outputs must be invariant/equivariant to tolerance.
- Unit-rescaling invariance THROUGH RUNTIME PATHS: rescale all runtime data (compile-time Qty can't cover data files); dimensionless outputs must be identical, dimensioned outputs must scale exactly.
- Estimator monotonicity under refinement: error estimators must not increase under uniform refinement (statistically, with declared exceptions).
- Adjoint-vs-finite-difference agreement along random directions at randomized points (complements the per-solver gradient gate).
- Rep-Router path independence: ANY admissible conversion path between charts must land within the composed certificate of the direct path.
- Regime-scaling coherence (added, patch Rev A): solutions of nondimensionalized and dimensional formulations must map onto each other through the declared scaling.
- Framework: relation registry over the G0 generator infrastructure; each relation declares its transform generator, tolerance model (tied to certificates where available), and applicability predicates.

## Why
Most of FrankenSim's deepest bugs (sign errors in adjoints, silent unit assumptions, router edges with optimistic error models) are exactly the class metamorphic testing catches. Certificates give us principled tolerances instead of hand-waved epsilons — a rare luxury.

## Acceptance criteria
Framework + at least frame-invariance and unit-rescaling relations wired against PV-skeleton physics; seeded violation (e.g., a hardcoded meter assumption) demonstrably caught.

## Testing
Self-tests per relation with known violators. Logs: relation id, transform parameters, both outputs, tolerance, margin, verdict.

title: G4 harness: chaos/cancellation storms, fault injection, model checking, ≤200µs cancel latency
description:
## What
G4 harness: chaos and cancellation storms + concurrency model checking — the proof engine for P7 (cancellation-correct compute) (plan §13.1).

## Scope
- Storm driver: kill random asupersync scopes at random times during REAL solves (not toy tasks); configurable kill rates, depths, and timing distributions (including adversarial: at tile boundaries, mid-reduction, during ledger writes).
- Assertions after every storm: zero arena leaks (via fs-alloc accounting), zero task leaks, no deadlocks (watchdog + timeout forensics), ledger consistency (WAL recovery invariants), and — the crown jewel — pause-serialize-resume reproduces the uninterrupted trajectory BIT-EXACTLY in deterministic mode.
- Fault injection: poison allocators (fail at the Nth allocation), fault-inject tiles (panic at tile k), torture the two-lane executor with adversarial preemption patterns; verify panic containment (scope poisoned, siblings cancelled, structured error with tile provenance, campaign continues per policy).
- Latency-to-cancel measurement: distribution of cancel-request→core-reclaimed times; assert the ≤200 µs bound at p99 on reference hardware (the bound e-racing depends on).
- Model checking: run asupersync's model checker over the executor's core protocols (steal/join/cancel state machines); in-house interleaving enumeration for our own primitives (per unsafe-capsule requirements).

## Why
Bet 8 (e-raced optimization) and speculative races are only safe if cancellation NEVER tears state. "Cancellation-correctness is a claim; G4 is the proof engine."

## Acceptance criteria
Storm suite green nightly on both ISAs against fs-exec + PV physics; latency histogram ledgered; seeded leak (deliberately unfreed arena) and seeded deadlock both demonstrably caught.

## Testing
The harness IS testing infrastructure; its self-tests verify each assertion class fires on seeded violations. Logs: storm seed, kill schedule, per-assertion verdicts, latency histograms — fully replayable from seed.

title: G5 harness: determinism audits + cross-ISA divergence classification report
description:
## What
G5 harness: determinism audits — bit-identical results across runs and thread counts per ISA, plus the cross-ISA divergence report (plan §13.1, §5.4).

## Scope
- Same-ISA audit: run registered suites (kernels, solvers, studies) N times at varying thread counts (1, 2, P, P+E, oversubscribed) in ExecMode::Deterministic; assert bit-identical artifact hashes. Any divergence = build failure with the first-divergence locator (binary-search over the event stream to find the offending reduction/kernel).
- Cross-ISA report: classify every NEON vs AVX-512 difference into documented categories — FMA contraction differences, libm ULP differences (bounded by fs-math contracts), reduction shape (should be NONE in deterministic mode) — with envelopes and switches to force strict orderings where bit-equality across ISAs is required. The report is a generated artifact, refreshed nightly, treated as documentation of record.
- Artifact-hash discipline: every ledgered artifact carries its content hash; G5 compares ledgers across runs so silent drift is impossible.
- ExecMode::Fast delta tracking: measure and record the throughput gain (~5–15% expected) and the reproducibility loss, so the tradeoff stays observed rather than assumed.

## Why
P2 (determinism is a contract) is what makes optimization studies replayable from a seed and a ledger entry — and what makes e-raced tournaments and G4's pause-resume equality even checkable.

## Acceptance criteria
Audit runs against fs-exec reductions + fs-rand streams + PV study; a seeded nondeterminism (arrival-order reduction) is caught with correct localization; cross-ISA report generates with all differences classified (no "unclassified" rows).

## Testing
Self-tests with seeded violations per category. Logs: run/thread-count matrix, hash tables, divergence locators, classification tables.

title: Certifying the certifiers: adversarial trials for interval/e-process/conformal/sheaf/SOS certificates
description:
## What
Certifying the certifiers (plan §13.2): the trials that attack FrankenSim's OWN certificate machinery. A false certificate is worse than a wrong answer — it is a wrong answer wearing a badge (Sev-0 policy).

## Scope
- Interval/Taylor-model enclosures spot-checked against the slow in-house high-precision oracle (double-double/quad-double from fs-ivl): random expressions, adversarial cancellation patterns, near-singular evaluations; enclosure must CONTAIN the oracle value, and tightness regressions are tracked.
- e-process validity verified EMPIRICALLY: under simulated nulls, anytime-valid type-I error at ADVERSARIALLY CHOSEN stopping times must stay ≤ α (the adversary peeks and stops at the worst moment; validity must survive). Also: e-BH FWER/FDR control under thousands of simultaneous candidate comparisons.
- Conformal e-prediction coverage tested under distribution-shift scenarios: geometry-family shift, fidelity shift, scenario-cluster shift (coordinates with conformal-hardening bead's Mondrian buckets and drift e-tests); coverage must degrade DETECTABLY (flagged), never silently.
- Sheaf watertightness certificate cross-examined by brute-force ray-parity queries on adversarial multi-chart seams (near-tangent interfaces, sliver patches, mixed-chart T-junctions).
- SOS certificates: extracted dual certificates re-verified in interval arithmetic (the SDP solver may be approximate; the CERTIFICATE must be rigorous).
- Sev-0 protocol: any fooled certificate auto-files maximal severity with the full reproducer ledgered.

## Acceptance criteria
Each trial class implemented as its certifier lands (deps wired accordingly); nightly rotation on both ISAs; at least one seeded-broken certifier per class demonstrably caught.

## Testing
Adversarial self-tests per class. Logs: trial seeds, adversary strategies, violation counts, tightness/coverage statistics over time.

title: Conformance suites speak IR: contract cases as FrankenScript programs, golden-ledger unification, cross-crate negotiation
description:
## What
Upgrade the conformance harness so suites SPEAK FRANKENSCRIPT IR (plan §13.3): "Because the suites speak FrankenScript IR, they double as the integration language between the AI agents building the system — an agent implementing fs-fft never negotiates with the agent implementing fs-lbm; both negotiate with the contract."

## Background
contract-conformance-infra ships v0 as a Rust-native harness with structured JSON cases, DESIGNED so IR wrapping is additive. This bead executes that upgrade once fs-ir-core exists.

## Scope
- IR front end for conformance cases: each registered case exposes an equivalent IR program (inputs as IR values with the Five Explicits, expected outputs as content-addressed artifacts + tolerance models); running a conformance suite = executing a batch of IR studies through the standard admission/session path (conformance now exercises the REAL entry path, not a test-only side door — admission, budgets, and provenance included in every conformance run).
- Golden-ledger unification: conformance acceptance runs ARE golden ledgers (one replay/compare mechanism for both, via ledger-timetravel) — CI replays conformance the same way it replays features.
- Cross-crate contract negotiation: integration points between crates expressed as IR-level contracts (e.g., "fs-lbm consumes an SDF chart satisfying these certified queries") checkable without either crate knowing the other's internals — the team-of-agents integration mechanism made executable.
- Migration: v0 Rust-native cases remain valid (the IR layer wraps them); new cases authored in IR-first form; migration lint tracks coverage.

## Acceptance criteria
The bedrock + morph conformance suites runnable via IR batch execution with identical verdicts to v0-native runs (dual-run equivalence gate during migration); conformance runs appear in the ledger with full Five-Explicits provenance; one cross-crate contract case demonstrates the negotiation pattern (documented as the template).

## Testing
Dual-run equivalence battery; admission-rejection of a malformed conformance program (the entry path is real); replay determinism of conformance ledgers. Logs: per-case IR program hashes, verdict tables, dual-run diffs (must be empty).

title: L6 HELM: orchestration, ledger, and agent interface
description:
## Mission
L6 HELM: orchestration, sessions, and the agent interface. Everything below is callable; HELM makes it programmable, replayable, and governable — for AI agents first, humans equally. This is where P4 (budgets), P9 (provenance), and P10 (agent-first) become running code.

## Core components (plan §11 + patch Rev B)
- FrankenScript IR: THE canonical interface — a typed, versioned intermediate representation with two isomorphic syntaxes (canonical s-expressions + lossless JSON) parsing to one typed AST. Static admission before execution: dimensional analysis in the type system, chart-compatibility via Rep Router, budget feasibility against learned cost models, capability sufficiency. Ill-typed studies rejected in milliseconds with structured diagnoses, not discovered at hour six.
- The Design Ledger (Bet 10): FrankenSQLite as system of record — content-addressed artifacts, event-sourced ops, lineage edges, metrics, autotune cache, session events. Time travel, forkable worlds, and explain() are nearly free because sessions are event-sourced over content-addressed state. A six-month campaign is a database you query, not a directory you fear.
- Evidence/Model Ledger (patch Rev B): Evidence<T> carries numerical + statistical + MODEL-FORM certificates, sensitivity, validity domain, and decision status; model cards for every solver/closure/material law. The system must be able to say "mesh error 0.7%, turbulence-model discrepancy 8–15%, ranking not decision-grade."
- Sessions & capability tokens: budgets ENFORCED not advisory; idempotency keys; estimate() dry runs; errors-as-guidance with ranked fixes ("a refusal that teaches is worth ten silent successes").
- The self-optimizing pipeline (Bet 12): Error Ledger + Time Ledger compose per-operator error/cost models end-to-end; the planner allocates error budget across mesh/order/tolerance/surrogate/samples to maximize accuracy-per-second.
- Five Explicits everywhere: units, seeds, budgets, versions, capabilities — an agent literally cannot express an ambiguous request.

## Members
fs-ledger-core, evidence-model-ledger, ledger-timetravel, fs-ir-core, fs-ir-admission, fs-ir-catalog, fs-session-caps, fs-plan-models, fs-plan-allocator, fs-report, helm-e2e.

## Success Criteria
IR round-trips; admission rejects with teaching errors; ledger survives kill -9 mid-sweep; time-travel replay is bit-exact in deterministic mode; conversational latency ≤100 ms while campaigns saturate the throughput lane.

title: fs-ledger core: Design Ledger schema v0 + extension tables, BLAKE3-class content addressing, WAL/snapshot contract
description:
## What
`fs-ledger` core: the Design Ledger v0 on FrankenSQLite (plan §11.2, Bet 10) — the system of record that makes a six-month campaign a database you query, not a directory you fear.

## Scope
- Schema v0 (STRICT tables, Appendix D): **artifacts** (content-addressed typed blobs — charts, fields, meshes, fronts, images; identical results dedupe to one row forever), **ops** (event-sourced log: every executed IR node with its FROZEN Five Explicits — seeds, versions, budgets, capabilities — plus timing and outcome), **edges** (lineage DAG: op→artifact in/out roles), **metrics** (time series: residuals, e-values, objective traces, ledger balances, roofline attainment), **tune** (autotuner cache keyed kernel × shape-class × machine fingerprint), **events** (fine-grained session stream: tile completions, cancellations, preemptions — powers dashboards and post-hoc flame graphs).
- Extension tables (patch Rev S, added EARLY even if initially sparse): **requirements**, **model_cards**, **evidence**, **scenarios**, **constraints**, **capability_probes**, **imports**, **unsafe_capsules**.
- Content addressing: in-house BLAKE3-class tree hash (P1: in-house); streaming + chunked for multi-GiB fields; hash = artifact identity everywhere.
- Concurrency contract: WAL mode; snapshot-isolated readers so dashboards/agents query freely WHILE a 96-core sweep writes; append-heavy write path batched on the latency lane; predictable checkpointing mid-sweep (the §12 FrankenSQLite contract — we return a punishing OLTP+blob stress suite upstream).
- One file per project; crash safety = WAL recovery invariants tested by kill -9 storms (G4 class).

## Acceptance criteria
Schema migrations versioned; hash dedupe verified (same artifact bytes → one row across forks); concurrent reader/writer stress (sweep-writer + N snapshot readers) green; kill -9 mid-transaction recovery leaves consistent state (no partial ops, orphan edges lintable→zero).

## Testing
Unit: DDL/round-trips per table; property: content-hash stability + collision sanity; crash-recovery battery with random kill points (detailed logs: kill offset, recovery verdict, invariant checks); throughput benchmarks (events/sec sustained during synthetic sweeps) ledgered as its own metric.
close_reason: Implemented Design Ledger v0 as crates/fs-ledger. Schema v1 (STRICT): six Appendix-D core tables + artifact_chunks + all eight Rev S extension tables, versioned via PRAGMA user_version with idempotent migration ladder. In-house BLAKE3 (pure safe Rust) verified against 32 official-pattern vectors (0B..2MiB+1, multi-level trees) + seeded streaming-split property. Content addressing with dedupe across put/streaming paths; >4MiB artifacts stored as bounded chunk rows (fsqlite has no incremental blob IO); ArtifactWriter streams+stages in a writer-owned txn, zero residue on drop. Five Explicits enforced field-by-field with teaching errors (units live in typed IR per plan). WAL + synchronous=FULL + enforced FKs; lint() hygiene scan; full re-hash integrity verify; corruption fails loudly. Acceptance criteria proven: kill -9 battery (6 seeded rounds -> lint-clean, integrity-clean, no partial op groups), 3 concurrent snapshot readers during 120-op sweep (monotone, internally consistent), dedupe (same bytes -> one row), events/sec throughput ledgered as metric. 19 unit + 10 conformance tests green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; xtask check-all green for fs-ledger. Divergences documented in CONTRACT.md (JSON->TEXT+json_valid for STRICT legality; chunk storage). Found fsqlite IS NULL precedence bug -> tracked as frankensim-lar1. Time travel/forks/explain deliberately left to gp3.3.

comments.author: jemanuel
comments.text: Coordination (Claude, session e8aa053a): heads-up that a full-workspace test run shows 2 failing tests + a lib-test compile error in fs-ledger's in-flight code — not touching your lane, just flagging in case it is not local-known. FYI resources that may help: fs-vskeleton/src/ledger.rs has working fsqlite patterns (Connection::open, prepare/execute_with_params, SqliteValue::Text takes SmallText via .into(), Blob takes Arc<[u8]> via .into(), query_row for MAX(id)); fs-obs::fnv1a64 is the placeholder hasher; unsafe-capsules.json + docs/SAFETY_TEMPLATE.md if you need capsules. Your fs-substrate tile/morton/field/affinity additions pass tests in my runs — nice.

title: fs-report: auto lab notebooks (repro IR included), semantic OT design diffs w/ attribution, <=100ms conversational lane
description:
## What
`fs-report` + last-mile agent ergonomics (plan §11.5): the automatic lab notebook, semantic design diffs, and the ≤100 ms conversational guarantee — reproducibility as a SIDE EFFECT, not a virtue.

## Scope
- AUTOMATIC LAB NOTEBOOK: every study emits, via FrankenPandas over the ledger, a human-readable report — objective traces, Error/Time Ledger attributions, LUMEN renders, convergence tables, AND THE EXACT IR TO REPRODUCE; formats: markdown/HTML with embedded data tables; report generation is itself a ledgered op (reports have provenance too).
- SEMANTIC DESIGN DIFFS: `diff(a, b)` between two shapes returns a GEOMETRIC diff, not a file diff — varifold/OT distance (multiobjective-robust's Sinkhorn machinery), transport-plan visualization, per-region attribution ("Pareto-31 moved lip curvature −18%, thinned wall 0.4 mm" — the plan's example is the acceptance fixture format); works across chart types (router-normalized comparison).
- LATENCY-LANE SERVING: everything conversational — catalog, estimates, ledger queries, explain(), diffs — served ≤100 ms even while a campaign saturates the throughput lane (fs-exec two-lane payoff measured HERE); the agent never waits behind its own physics.
- Dashboard feeds: FrankenPandas frames over every ledger table (§12 contract) powering perf dashboards (perf-regression-ci) and study monitoring.

## Acceptance criteria
Notebook generated for each flagship fixture study containing all required sections + the reproducing IR (executed → same artifact hashes — the reproducibility loop closed in CI); diff(a,b) attribution correct on synthetic shape pairs with known edits (moved/thinned features recovered qualitatively + quantitatively); ≤100 ms conversational latency verified UNDER full-load soak tests (p99, ledgered).

## Testing
Report-content lint (sections present, links resolve, tables schema-valid); diff round-trip properties (diff(a,a)=0; symmetry up to sign); latency soak battery. Logs: report artifacts, diff payloads, latency histograms.

title: HELM e2e suite: admission/enforcement/idempotency/crash-recovery/replay/latency batteries at study scale
description:
## What
The HELM end-to-end test suite: the orchestration layer's claims — admission, enforcement, idempotency, provenance, replay, crash safety, latency — proven at STUDY scale with forensic-grade logging.

## Scope
- IR round-trip + admission batteries (fixture studies incl. Appendix C's spout + frame programs; the violation zoo with content-checked diagnoses).
- Budget enforcement e2e: studies designed to exceed wall/mem grants → correct governor responses (throttle/pause/degrade ladder in declared order), never silent kills, always structured outcomes.
- Idempotency: concurrent duplicate submissions (same key) under load → exactly-once execution, one budget charge (race-stress harness).
- Crash safety: kill -9 storms mid-sweep (WAL recovery, no partial ops, no orphan edges); recovery-then-replay equality.
- Time travel/fork/explain: replay bit-equality on deterministic studies; fork independence; explain() completeness on multi-stage campaigns.
- Five Explicits audit: no op enters the ledger without all five; seeded omissions rejected.
- Latency guarantee: catalog/estimate/explain/diff p99 ≤100 ms while a saturating synthetic campaign runs (the two-lane proof).
- Golden-ledger CI integration: each HELM feature's acceptance run stored + replayed nightly.

## Acceptance criteria
All green on both ISAs; every enforcement path has a seeded-violation self-test; latency soak ledgered with fingerprints; the suite doubles as HELM's conformance contract (contract-conformance-infra registration).

## Testing
This IS the test bead. Logging: per-scenario structured records (study hashes, governor decisions with timestamps, recovery verdicts, latency histograms) — an orchestration bug must be reproducible from logs + ledger alone.

title: fs-evidence: Evidence<T>/Certified<T> types, composition algebra, model cards + discrepancy/bracketing/escalation
description:
## What
`Evidence<T>` and the Model Ledger (patch Rev B): broaden `Certified<T>` beyond numerical enclosure so the biggest REAL design-error source — model-form uncertainty — is carried inside values, not left in engineers' heads.

## Why (the core argument)
Without this, FrankenSim can produce beautifully certified WRONG answers: mesh error 0.7%, algebraic residual irrelevant — but turbulence-closure discrepancy 8–15%, so the design ranking is not decision-grade. Being able to SAY that sentence is the difference between a solver and an engineering-intelligence system. Named model-form risks: LES closure adequacy, contact-line behavior (vessel flagship), constitutive-law calibration vs actual construction (frame flagship), surrogate out-of-distribution failure, ground-motion model assumptions.

## Scope
- Core types in a LOW-LAYER crate (fs-evidence, usable by L1+ without layer violations): `Evidence<T> { value, numerical: NumericalCertificate, statistical: StatisticalCertificate, model: ModelEvidence, sensitivity: SensitivitySummary, provenance: ProvenanceHash, validity_domain: ValidityDomain, decision_status: DecisionStatus }`. `Certified<T>` = Evidence<T> with a strong numerical certificate + explicit model evidence (type alias + constructor discipline; plan Appendix B's Certified fields — value + interval bound + provenance + adjoint hook — remain intact as the numerical slice).
- Composition algebra: combining Evidence through operations composes certificates conservatively (numerical bounds compose per fs-ivl; statistical per e-value arithmetic; model evidence composes as validity-domain intersection + assumption union) — the machinery the Error Ledger (fs-plan-models) attributes from.
- Model cards (ledger `model_cards` table): for EVERY solver/closure/constitutive law — assumptions, validity domain, known failures, calibration artifacts, promotion level [S]/[F]/[M].
- Four model-form mechanisms: model cards; DISCREPANCY MODELS between fidelity levels trained on ledger data; MODEL BRACKETING for weakly-understood physics (e.g., bracketing contact-angle models, reporting the objective's sensitivity band rather than pretending certainty — exactly the vessel flagship's stated mitigation); DECISION-AWARE ESCALATION (HELM escalates fidelity when MODEL uncertainty, not numerical, dominates the pending decision).
- `evidence` ledger table rows for artifacts.

## Acceptance criteria
Evidence composition laws under G0 (conservativeness: composed validity ⊆ intersection; composed bounds ⊇ true propagation); model cards mandatory at solver registration (lint: a FLUX solver without a card cannot register); a worked end-to-end example where model discrepancy dominates and decision_status correctly reports not-decision-grade.

## Testing
Property tests on composition algebra; serialization round-trips to ledger tables; integration test: two-fidelity discrepancy model trained on synthetic ledger data flags an out-of-domain query. Logs: full evidence trees rendered per artifact (the explain() payload).
close_reason: Implemented fs-evidence (UTIL layer per the bead scope's explicit low-layer requirement; label said L6 — scope text overrides so L1+ can consume without violations). Core: Evidence<T>/Certified<T> with the four uncertainty slices — NumericalCertificate (Exact/Enclosure/Estimate/NoClaim severity order; plan Appendix B fields intact incl. adjoint hook), StatisticalCertificate (None/EValue/HalfWidth), ModelEvidence (cards, assumption union, validity intersection, additive discrepancy bands, in_domain conjunction), SensitivitySummary. Conservative composition algebra (combine over Add/Sub/Mul/Min/Max): outward-rounded interval composition never claiming Exact after float ops, NoClaim absorption, order-sensitive provenance chaining — G0 conservativeness proven over 300k seeded samples (evd-001). Certified discipline: certified() refuses Estimate-tainted chains and out-of-domain model use with structured CertifyError (evd-006 poisoning law). Model cards + ModelRegistry with THE lint: solver without a card cannot register (evd-002). DiscrepancyModel: two-fidelity fit (observed box + mean/max band) whose query/evidence_at REFUSE out-of-distribution points naming the violated parameter (evd-004, 50-pair synthetic corpus). ModelBracket: contact-angle-style bracketing -> enclosure spanning members + spread as model band (vessel flagship mitigation, evd-005). assess()/UncertaintyBreakdown/escalation_advice: worked example lands the core argument — mesh 0.7% + stat 0.5% + LES closure 10% at 5% threshold -> NotDecisionGrade dominant=model-form with teaching detail + EscalateModelFidelity; calibrated 2% closure flips to DecisionGrade (evd-003). Ledger evidence/model_cards rows canonical + fs-obs-validated. Lanes green: check, clippy -D warnings, fmt, fs-evidence 12 lib + 6 conformance, workspace 61 suites, xtask 0 violations (18 crates), ubs 0 criticals. No-claims: statistical composition conservative-weakest until fs-eproc; discrepancy models are bookkeeping not learning (FrankenTorch later); adjoint composition is fs-ad's; ledger persistence is row rendering until fs-ledger tables.

title: fs-ledger time travel: at(t) replay, forkable worlds (hash-shared branches), explain() causal trees
description:
## What
Ledger time travel, forkable worlds, and explain() (plan §11.2): the three expensive-sounding features that become NEARLY FREE because sessions are event-sourced over content-addressed state.

## Scope
- TIME TRAVEL: `at(t)` replays the op log to any instant — materialized state views at historical points; requires deterministic replay semantics (ExecMode recorded per op; replays of Deterministic ops must reproduce artifact hashes EXACTLY, replay of Fast ops re-executes with documented divergence class).
- FORKABLE WORLDS: a fork is a new BRANCH of the op log sharing every artifact by hash — an agent speculatively explores three formulations for the cost of the deltas; branch bookkeeping (parent op, divergence point), merge-view queries (compare branches), garbage-collection policy respecting all live branches (content addressing makes sharing safe).
- `explain(artifact)`: walk `edges` backward and render the FULL CAUSAL TREE — which chart, which mesh, which seed, which solver tolerances, which library versions (constellation lock hash) produced this number; output as structured data + human-readable rendering (fs-report); depth/filter controls for enormous trees.
- Replay validation: replay-vs-original hash comparison as a first-class audit (the G5-at-study-scale primitive; golden ledgers in CI use exactly this).
- Interactive steering integration: MOO's fork-on-reweight lands here (a steering event = fork op).

## Acceptance criteria
Replay of a deterministic fixture study reproduces every artifact hash; forks share artifacts (storage audit: N forks ≈ 1× artifacts + deltas); explain() reconstructs known lineage on fixture campaigns completely (no orphan inputs); at(t) views consistent at arbitrary interior instants (including mid-sweep points).

## Testing
Property: fork independence (ops on branch A never affect branch B's views); replay determinism battery; explain() completeness lint (every artifact reachable from a study is explainable). Crash-interaction: kill -9 during fork creation leaves both branches consistent (G4-class). Logs: replay verdicts, branch trees, lineage renderings.
close_reason: Time travel, forkable worlds, explain(), replay audit, and GC landed as fs-ledger's travel module on schema v2 — the first real migration through the versioned ladder (branches table seeded with main; ops gain branch + exec_mode via ADD COLUMN with defaults so v1 history reads as main-branch deterministic ops; proven by tt-001 migrating a genuine v1 database with history intact). FORKS: a fork is a new op-log branch sharing every artifact by hash; visibility = own ops + ancestors' up to each fork point (min-composed caps down the chain, cycle-guarded); storage audit proven (tt-002: 3 forks over a 5-artifact study cost exactly 3 delta artifacts) with branch-independence property (writes on A never change B's view). AT(t): consistent views at arbitrary instants incl. mid-sweep — ops begun by t appear, outcomes written after t are masked, unfinished ops' outputs invisible; monotonicity + internal consistency proven (tt-005). EXPLAIN: full causal trees (which op, seed, versions/lock-hash, budget, capability, exec mode, branch, inputs recursively), depth-limited, DAG-deduped, JSON + human renderings; orphan inputs fail LOUDLY as LedgerCorruption (tt-004). REPLAY AUDIT (the G5-at-study-scale primitive for golden ledgers): op sequences compared structurally (ir/seed/mode); deterministic ops must reproduce output hashes exactly; fast divergences reported without failing (tt-003 battery: clean/deterministic-failure/fast-divergence all classified). GC: only edge-less artifacts reclaimable, dry-run first, referenced artifacts immortal (P9). Crash: kill -9 during fork traffic recovers lint-clean with all visible ops complete (tt-006, 4 seeded rounds); lint extended with branch-reference checks. 25 unit + 11 conformance + 7 travel tests green; fs-roofline (dependent) green; clippy -D warnings clean; xtask check-all 19 crates 0 violations; CONTRACT.md updated (new no-claims: no branch deletion/merge, caller-timestamp trust). Interactive-steering fork-on-reweight integration deferred to the MOO bead as specified.

title: fs-ir core: FrankenScript typed AST, isomorphic s-expr/JSON syntaxes, versioning, inspectable verb lowering
description:
## What
`fs-ir` core (plan §11.1): FrankenScript — the system's ONE TRUE INTERFACE. A typed, versioned intermediate representation, not a pile of function signatures.

## Scope
- Two ISOMORPHIC concrete syntaxes: canonical s-expressions and a lossless JSON mapping — agents emit whichever their tooling prefers; BOTH parse to the same typed AST (isomorphism is a tested property, not an aspiration).
- Value types are the system's REAL NOUNS: dimensioned quantities (Qty literals: `0.12Pa*s`, `65deg`, `0.5L/s`), Regions and charts, fields/cochains, operators, budgets, studies, capability tokens, scenarios, seeds — the Appendix C study forms (`(study ...)`, `(budget (wall 2h) (mem 96GiB) (qoi-rel-error 2e-2))`, `(let vessel (frep ...))`, `ascent.optimize ... :until (any (grad-norm 1e-5) (e-value 20) (budget-exhausted))`) are the acceptance fixtures.
- Versioning: IR language versions + per-operator semantic versions; older-IR migration hooks; version pinning in every study (Five Explicits' "versions" pillar).
- High-level verbs (`optimize_shape`, `simulate_pour`) exist for terseness but LOWER TO EXPLICIT IR, and the lowering is INSPECTABLE — progressive disclosure with nothing hidden.
- AST design for admission: every node carries source spans (structured errors point at exact locations in agent-submitted programs).

## Acceptance criteria
s-expr ↔ JSON ↔ AST round-trips bit-stable (property-tested on generated programs); Appendix C's spout and frame studies parse to correct ASTs (the canonical fixtures); verb lowering produces valid, inspectable IR; version pinning round-trips.

## Testing
G0: parser fuzzing (no panics, structured rejections with spans); grammar-coverage battery; isomorphism property (parse_sexpr(print_json(x)) == x). Logs: parse diagnostics with spans, lowering traces (verb → explicit IR).
close_reason: FrankenScript core landed as crates/fs-ir (the system's one true interface, plan 11.1 / P10). TYPED AST: every node carries a byte span; atoms are the real nouns — fs-qty dimensioned quantities (0.12Pa*s, 65deg, 0.5L/s, 0.03m2/s3; the ORIGINAL literal preserved verbatim because fs-qty normalizes to SI — 65deg->rad — so printing must not corrupt round-trips), Count grants (384GiB/96cores — information units deliberately outside fs-qty's SI domain), 0x seeds, strings/symbols/keywords/lists. TWO ISOMORPHIC SYNTAXES: hand-rolled total s-expr reader (comments, escapes, deterministic atom classification — a number with a garbage suffix is a structured spanned error, NEVER silently a symbol) and a lossless single-key-tagged JSON mapping (i/f/q/c/seed/s/sym/kw; Qty/Count/Seed reuse the s-expr literal grammar inside strings so ONE classifier owns numeric semantics for both). ISOMORPHISM PROVEN: 200 seeded generated programs + the verbatim Appendix C spout and frame studies survive sexpr/JSON/cross-syntax cycles shape-stably (acceptance). FIXTURES: both Appendix C studies parse with seeds (0x5EED0001/0xF00D0002), constellation locks, let-bindings, and typed-noun counts asserted. TOTALITY: 8000-parse garbage battery (structured rejections, in-bounds spans, non-empty teaching hints) + 100k-deep nesting refused structurally via depth cap on both parsers (no stack overflow). SPAN ACCURACY: bad-seed/bad-quantity errors isolate the exact offending token. VERB LOWERING: optimize-shape/simulate-pour expand to explicit ascent.optimize/flux.free-surface-lbm with every injected default NAMED in the inspectable trace; idempotent (re-lowering is a fixed point); malformed usage refuses with the verb's span. VERSIONING: IR_VERSION=1; Study recognizer extracts the Five Explicits pillars + constellation_lock(); pinning round-trips through both syntaxes verbatim (acceptance). Study extraction is deliberately policy-free — admission validity is gp3.5's; catalog/per-op semver is gp3.6's (CONTRACT.md no-claims). 6 conformance suites green; clippy -D warnings clean; fmt clean; xtask check-all 23 crates 0 violations.

title: fs-ir admission: dimensional/chart/budget/capability/regime checks in milliseconds, ranked-fix rejections
description:
## What
`fs-ir` STATIC ADMISSION (plan §11.1): before anything executes, the IR passes admission — an ill-typed study is rejected in MILLISECONDS with a structured diagnosis, not discovered at hour six.

## Scope
- Dimensional analysis: Qty checking through the whole study graph (a pressure cannot be added to a stress IN THE TYPE SYSTEM; runtime-loaded data checked via QtyAny) — unit errors die here.
- Chart-compatibility checking through the Rep Router: does a conversion chain exist within the study's error budget for every geometry-consuming op? (route feasibility as an admission predicate, with the router's explanation attached to failures).
- Budget-feasibility screening against learned cost models (fs-plan-models): predicted wall/mem vs declared budget — the `BudgetInfeasible` error WITH RANKED FIXES (§11.3's worked example: relax qoi-rel-error / surrogate-screen / coarsen outside the lip band, each with estimated wall + QoI impact from the cost models).
- Capability sufficiency: study's operator set + resource asks vs the session's capability token (fs-session-caps).
- REGIME GATING (patch Rev A integration): solver/model choices checked against RegimeReports — `(assert (regime.allows flux.free-surface-lbm))` enforced; violations rejected or warned per policy with the report attached.
- The Five Explicits enforced structurally: units, seeds, budgets, versions, capabilities are MANDATORY fields — an agent literally cannot express an ambiguous request.

## Acceptance criteria
Admission latency ≤ milliseconds-class on flagship-scale studies (measured); each admission dimension has seeded-rejection fixtures verifying correct, actionable diagnoses (error payloads content-checked, including ranked fixes with cost-model-derived estimates); zero false-admits on the violation zoo; Appendix C studies admit cleanly.

## Testing
G0: admission determinism (same study → same verdict + same diagnosis); fuzz: mutated studies never crash admission; fix-suggestion quality harness (applying the top-ranked fix makes the study admissible). Logs: full admission reports per study (checks run, timings, verdicts).

title: fs-ir catalog: code-generated operator catalog (types/costs/errors/examples), catalog(query) <=100ms, zero drift
description:
## What
The machine-readable operator CATALOG (plan §11.1): `catalog(query)` is how an agent DISCOVERS the system — generated FROM THE CODE, so it cannot drift.

## Scope
- Per-operator catalog entries: full type signature (IR types + Qty dimensions), units, COST MODEL reference, ERROR MODEL reference, determinism class, capability requirements, cancellation behavior, WORKED EXAMPLES (executable — examples are tested in CI, so documentation rots loudly), semantic version, model-card link (evidence-model-ledger), ambition tag [S]/[F]/[M] + feature-flag status.
- Generation pipeline: derived from operator registrations + CONTRACT.md metadata + fs-opdsl definitions at build time; hand-written catalog prose FORBIDDEN for facts the code knows (the no-drift doctrine, AGENTS.md: "generated catalogs must come from code/contracts").
- Query API: `catalog(query)` with structured search (by physics domain, by capability, by type signature compatibility — "what can consume an SDF chart and produce a pressure field"), served from the latency lane ≤100 ms (§11.5).
- Diff-friendly: catalog versions diffable across releases (agents can ask "what changed since the version I know").

## Acceptance criteria
Catalog complete (lint: every registered operator has an entry; every entry's example executes green); drift test: a deliberately mismatched hand-annotation fails the build; query latency ≤100 ms on the full catalog under concurrent sweep load; signature-compatibility search returns correct operator sets on fixtures.

## Testing
G0: generation determinism; example-execution battery in CI; schema validation of every entry. Logs: catalog build reports, query traces, example-run verdicts.

title: fs-session: capability tokens, enforcing governor w/ declared degradation ladder, idempotency keys, estimate() dry runs, teaching errors
description:
## What
`fs-session` (plan §11.3): sessions, capability tokens, and the resource GOVERNOR — budgets are ENFORCED, not advisory. Plus the agent-proofing trio: idempotency keys, dry runs, errors-as-guidance.

## Scope
- Sessions: every IR program executes inside a session holding a CAPABILITY TOKEN — explicit grant of operators, core-seconds, resident memory, wall time, ledger scope. Token checking at admission (fs-ir-admission) and CONTINUOUSLY at runtime (the governor meters actual consumption via Cx budget accountants).
- Resource governor: admission control onto the two-lane executor; per-study core quotas; on memory pressure, GRACEFUL DEGRADATION in a DECLARED ORDER — spill coldest arenas → coarsen adaptively → pause-serialize-resume (P7 makes every solver checkpointable, so "pause the LES, run the urgent trim study, resume" is ROUTINE, not heroic). Degradation events ledgered with attribution.
- IDEMPOTENCY KEYS on every mutating call: a retried request cannot double-spend a budget or duplicate a study (exactly-once semantics over the ledger; key = agent-supplied + content hash).
- DRY RUNS: `estimate(program)` returns predicted wall time, memory, error, and ENERGY from the cost models WITHOUT EXECUTING — agents plan before they spend.
- ERRORS AS GUIDANCE: every failure is a structured value with machine-readable diagnosis + candidate fixes RANKED BY THE COST MODEL (the §11.3 BudgetInfeasible example is the canonical fixture; fs-constraint's infeasibility diagnoses surface through this same channel). "A refusal that teaches is worth ten silent successes."

## Acceptance criteria
Budget enforcement: studies exceeding grants are throttled/paused per policy (never silently killed — structured outcomes); double-submit with same idempotency key = one execution, one charge (concurrency-stress-tested); estimate() accuracy tracked vs actuals (calibration curves ledgered — the cost models' own report card); degradation ladder exercised under synthetic memory pressure with correct ordering.

## Testing
G4-class: governor under storm (many studies, adversarial budgets); idempotency races (simultaneous duplicate submissions); pause-resume-under-pressure equality. Logs: per-session consumption ledgers, degradation events, estimate-vs-actual tables, error payloads.

title: fs-plan models: per-operator error+cost models (quantile fits over tune), Error Ledger + Time Ledger attribution
description:
## What
`fs-plan` models (plan §11.4, Bet 12): every operator publishes an ERROR MODEL and a COST MODEL; composition composes the models — yielding the Error Ledger and the Time Ledger that make "how accurate is this drag number and where did the error come from" A QUERY, not a research project (P4).

## Scope
- Error models per operator: a-priori rates (discretization orders, truncation bounds) + a-posteriori estimators (DWR from §8.6, interval widths from fs-ivl, conformal bands from fs-surrogate, MC/MLMC half-widths from fs-uq) — unified into the Evidence composition algebra (evidence-model-ledger's numerical/statistical slices, now with MODEL-FORM entries too: the Error Ledger attributes uncertainty to geometry tolerance, discretization, algebraic residual, surrogate band, statistical noise, AND closure/model discrepancy).
- Cost models per operator: QUANTILE-REGRESSION fits over the `tune` table for THIS machine (wall/memory/energy vs shape-class features); confidence bands on predictions (an estimate is itself evidenced); online updating as ops complete (predicted-vs-actual residuals feed refits).
- THE LEDGERS: end-to-end attribution trees for a study's QoI error and wall-clock — queryable (`explain_error(artifact)` / `explain_time(study)`), rendered by fs-report; the planner's decision substrate.
- Consumers wired: fs-ir-admission (budget feasibility), fs-session (estimate()), fs-voi (EVC), Rep Router (edge costs), fs-plan-allocator (the optimizer over these models).

## Acceptance criteria
Composition correctness: composed error models bound measured end-to-end errors on fixture pipelines (conservative but useful — tightness tracked); cost predictions within stated quantile bands empirically (calibration audits); attribution trees complete (no unattributed error mass beyond declared residual); online updates improve prediction accuracy over a campaign (measured).

## Testing
G0: composition algebra laws (associativity of attribution, conservativeness); calibration batteries per model family; determinism of model fits from ledger snapshots. Logs: attribution trees, calibration curves, refit events.

title: fs-plan allocator: greedy-plus-lookup v1, co-optimizing v2 [M] gated on beating hand allocation, online re-planning
description:
## What
The budget ALLOCATOR (plan §11.4, Bet 12 [F→M]): budget allocation as an optimization problem HELM solves ABOUT ITSELF — given "drag to 2% in 2 hours," allocate error and time across mesh size, polynomial order, solver tolerance, surrogate usage, and sample counts to maximize expected accuracy-per-second.

## Scope
- V1 (ships first, [F]): GREEDY-PLUS-LOOKUP — marginal-utility ranking over discrete knob settings using the cost/error models; re-planned online as a-posteriori estimates replace a-priori guesses (the DWR estimate arrives → the plan updates); always produces a feasible plan or a structured infeasibility (BudgetInfeasible with ranked relaxations — the fs-session error path).
- V2 (behind `moonshot-planner`, [M]): the full co-optimizer — convex formulations where the models are (rate-based error bounds usually are), CMA-ES ON ITSELF where they aren't; multi-stage plans (screen→refine ladders as first-class plan structures).
- PROMOTION GATE (the plan is explicit): V2 promotes only after the Gauntlet shows its plans BEAT HAND ALLOCATION on all three flagships (the P6 exit criterion — measured accuracy-per-second, ledgered).
- Plan artifacts: allocation decisions are ledgered ops (a study's plan is part of its provenance; explain() shows WHY the mesh was this fine).
- Tropical-analytics integration (§14.3): critical-path sensitivities tell the planner where relaxed tolerance actually buys wall-clock (slack elsewhere buys nothing).

## Acceptance criteria
V1: plans feasible + budget-respecting on the fixture matrix; online re-planning reacts correctly to seeded estimate updates (plan changes when and only when estimates warrant); V2: beats V1 and hand allocation on ≥ the flagship set (or reports honestly that it doesn't — the gate is real); allocation determinism from model snapshots.

## Testing
Simulation harness: synthetic pipelines with known optimal allocations (planner vs oracle); end-to-end: the §11.4 "drag to 2% in 2h" scenario materialized as a fixture. Logs: plan rationales (per-knob marginal utilities), re-plan events, accuracy-per-second scoreboards.

title: L5 LUMEN: rendering and visualization kernel
description:
## Mission
L5 LUMEN: the rendering kernel. Physically-based still/animated imagery of designs and physics, direct scientific visualization of every FLUX field type, and differentiable rendering as an optimization sensor — all CPU-native and tile-parallel.

## Core positions (plan §10)
- Unbiased spectral path tracing in the Maxwell-render tradition: hero-wavelength sampling with MIS, next-event estimation, light-BVH many-emitter sampling, layered measured-spectrum materials, heterogeneous (Woodcock-tracked) volumetrics pointed directly at LBM density/temperature fields — "render the pour" is the same machinery as "render the product shot".
- Geometry backends WITHOUT conversion: sphere tracing renders SDF/F-rep charts directly using certified Lipschitz bounds from fs-ivl (step sizes that provably never tunnel); NURBS trace via Bézier-clipping-seeded Newton; meshes trace natively. "Render whatever chart exists" is the default, not a pipeline stage.
- Scientific visualization that makes 10⁸-cell fields legible to an agent in one image: isosurfaces, DVR, LIC/streamlines, tensor glyphs, Morse–Smale/Reeb topological skeletons.
- Differentiable rendering closes the loop where aesthetic constraints join physical ones in ASCENT (match a target photo/sketch as an objective term).
- Deterministic-mode rendering: per-tile Philox streams — an image is as replayable as a solve.

## Members
fs-render-core, fs-render-charts, fs-render-volumes, fs-viz, diff-render, fs-img, lumen-tests.

## Success Criteria
P1 preview tracer hits target ray rates (sphere-traced F-rep turntable ≥80 Mray/s M-class, ≥120 Mray/s TR-class primary rays); furnace/white-image G0 tests green; deterministic render hashes stable.
